Exodus22:1 A fine must be paid by anyone who steals an ox or sheep and then kills or sells it. For oxen the fine is five oxen for each one stolen. For sheep the fine is four sheep for each one stolen.

Exodus22:3 But if it happens in daylight
Exodus22:4 If someone steals an ox or a donkey or a sheep and it is recovered alive

Exodus22:22 Do not exploit widows or orphans.

Exodus22:27 Your neighbor will need it to stay warm during the night. If you do not return it and your neighbor cries out to me for help
Exodus22:28 Do not blaspheme God or curse anyone who rules over you.
Exodus22:29 Do not hold anything back when you give me the tithe of your crops and your wine. You must make the necessary payment for redemption of your firstborn sons.

Exodus23:7 Keep far away from falsely charging anyone with evil. Never put an innocent or honest person to death. I will not allow anyone guilty of this to go free.
Exodus23:8 Take no bribes
Exodus23:9 Do not oppress the foreigners living among you. You know what it is like to be a foreigner. Remember your own experience in the land of Egypt.
Exodus23:10 Plant and harvest your crops for six years
Exodus23:11 but let the land rest and lie fallow during the seventh year. Then let the poor among you harvest any volunteer crop that may come up. Leave the rest for the animals to eat. The same applies to your vineyards and olive groves.
Exodus23:12 Work for six days
Exodus23:13 Be sure to obey all my instructions. And remember
Exodus23:14 Each year you must celebrate three festivals in my honor.
Exodus23:15 The first is the Festival of Unleavened Bread. For seven days you are to eat bread made without yeast
Exodus23:16 You must also celebrate the Festival of Harvest
Exodus23:17 At these three times each year
Exodus23:18 Sacrificial blood must never be offered together with bread that has yeast in it. And no sacrificial fat may be left unoffered until the next morning.
Exodus23:19 As you harvest each of your crops
Exodus23:20 See
Exodus23:21 Pay attention to him
Exodus23:22 But if you are careful to obey him
Exodus23:23 For my angel will go before you and bring you into the land of the Amorites
Exodus23:24 Do not worship the gods of these other nations or serve them in any way
Exodus23:25 You must serve only the LORD your God. If you do
Exodus23:26 There will be no miscarriages or infertility among your people
Exodus23:27 I will send my terror upon all the people whose lands you invade
Exodus23:28 I will send hornets ahead of you to drive out the Hivites
Exodus23:29 But I will not do this all in one year because the land would become a wilderness
Exodus23:30 I will drive them out a little at a time until your population has increased enough to fill the land.
Exodus23:31 And I will fix your boundaries from the Red Sea to the Mediterranean Sea
Exodus23:32 Make no treaties with them and have nothing to do with their gods.
Exodus23:33 Do not even let them live among you! If you do
Exodus24:1 Then the LORD instructed Moses: Come up here to me
Exodus24:2 You alone
Exodus24:3 When Moses had announced to the people all the teachings and regulations the LORD had given him
Exodus24:4 Then Moses carefully wrote down all the LORD's instructions. Early the next morning he built an altar at the foot of the mountain. He also set up twelve pillars around the altar
Exodus24:5 Then he sent some of the young men to sacrifice young bulls as burnt offerings and peace offerings to the LORD.
Exodus24:6 Moses took half the blood from these animals and drew it off into basins. The other half he splashed against the altar.
Exodus24:7 Then he took the Book of the Covenant and read it to the people. They all responded again
Exodus24:8 Then Moses sprinkled the blood from the basins over the people and said
Exodus24:9 Then Moses
Exodus24:10 There they saw the God of Israel. Under his feet there seemed to be a pavement of brilliant sapphire
Exodus24:11 And though Israel's leaders saw God
Exodus24:12 And the LORD said to Moses
Exodus24:13 So Moses and his assistant Joshua climbed up the mountain of God.
Exodus24:14 Moses told the other leaders
Exodus24:15 Then Moses went up the mountain
Exodus24:16 And the glorious presence of the LORD rested upon Mount Sinai
Exodus24:17 The Israelites at the foot of the mountain saw an awesome sight. The awesome glory of the LORD on the mountaintop looked like a devouring fire.
Exodus24:18 Then Moses disappeared into the cloud as he climbed higher up the mountain. He stayed on the mountain forty days and forty nights.
Exodus25:1 The LORD said to Moses
Exodus25:2 Tell the people of Israel that everyone who wants to may bring me an offering.
Exodus25:3 Here is a list of items you may accept on my behalf: gold
Exodus25:4 blue
Exodus25:5 tanned ram skins and fine goatskin leather; acacia wood;
Exodus25:6 olive oil for the lamps; spices for the anointing oil and the fragrant incense;
Exodus25:7 onyx stones
Exodus25:8 I want the people of Israel to build me a sacred residence where I can live among them.
Exodus25:9 You must make this Tabernacle and its furnishings exactly according to the plans I will show you.
Exodus25:10 Make an Ark of acacia wood--a sacred chest 3 3/4 feet long
Exodus25:11 Overlay it inside and outside with pure gold
Exodus25:12 Cast four rings of gold for it
Exodus25:13 Make poles from acacia wood
Exodus25:14 Fit the poles into the rings at the sides of the Ark to carry it.
Exodus25:15 These carrying poles must never be taken from the rings; they are to be left there permanently.
Exodus25:16 When the Ark is finished
Exodus25:17 Then make the Ark's cover--the place of atonement--out of pure gold. It must be 3 3/4 feet long and 2 1/4 feet wide.
Exodus25:18 Then use hammered gold to make two cherubim
Exodus25:19 Attach the cherubim to each end of the atonement cover
Exodus25:20 The cherubim will face each other
Exodus25:21 Place inside the Ark the stone tablets inscribed with the terms of the covenant
Exodus25:22 I will meet with you there and talk to you from above the atonement cover between the gold cherubim that hover over the Ark of the Covenant. From there I will give you my commands for the people of Israel.
Exodus25:23 Then make a table of acacia wood
Exodus25:24 Overlay it with pure gold and run a molding of gold around it.
Exodus25:25 Put a rim about three inches wide around the top edge
Exodus25:26 Make four gold rings
Exodus25:27 close to the rim around the top. These rings will support the poles used to carry the table.
Exodus25:28 Make these poles from acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
Exodus25:29 And make gold plates and dishes
Exodus25:30 You must always keep the special Bread of the Presence on the table before me.
Exodus25:31 Make a lampstand of pure
Exodus25:32 It will have six branches
Exodus25:33 Each of the six branches will hold a cup shaped like an almond blossom
Exodus25:34 The center stem of the lampstand will be decorated with four almond blossoms
Exodus25:35 One blossom will be set beneath each pair of branches where they extend from the center stem.
Exodus25:36 The decorations and branches must all be one piece with the stem
Exodus25:37 Then make the seven lamps for the lampstand
Exodus25:38 The lamp snuffers and trays must also be made of pure gold.
Exodus25:39 You will need seventy-five pounds of pure gold for the lampstand and its accessories.
Exodus25:40 Be sure that you make everything according to the pattern I have shown you here on the mountain.
Exodus26:1 Make the Tabernacle from ten sheets of fine linen. These sheets are to be decorated with blue
Exodus26:2 Each sheet must be forty-two feet long and six feet wide. All ten sheets must be exactly the same size.
Exodus26:3 Join five of these sheets together into one set; then join the other five sheets into a second set.
Exodus26:4 Put loops of blue yarn along the edge of the last sheet in each set.
Exodus26:5 The fifty loops along the edge of one set are to match the fifty loops along the edge of the other.
Exodus26:6 Then make fifty gold clasps to fasten the loops of the two sets of sheets together
Exodus26:7 Make heavy sheets of cloth from goat hair to cover the Tabernacle. There must be eleven of these sheets
Exodus26:8 each forty-five feet long and six feet wide. All eleven of these sheets must be exactly the same size.
Exodus26:9 Join five of these together into one set
Exodus26:10 Put fifty loops along the edge of the last sheet in each set
Exodus26:11 and fasten them together with fifty bronze clasps. In this way
Exodus26:12 An extra half sheet of this roof covering will be left to hang over the back of the Tabernacle
Exodus26:13 and the covering will hang down an extra eighteen inches on each side.
Exodus26:14 On top of these coverings place a layer of tanned ram skins
Exodus26:15 The framework of the Tabernacle will consist of frames made of acacia wood.
Exodus26:16 Each frame must be 15 feet high and 2 1/4 feet wide.
Exodus26:17 There will be two pegs on each frame so they can be joined to the next frame. All the frames must be made this way.
Exodus26:18 Twenty of these frames will support the south side of the Tabernacle.
Exodus26:19 They will fit into forty silver bases--two bases under each frame.
Exodus26:20 On the north side there will also be twenty of these frames
Exodus26:21 with their forty silver bases
Exodus26:22 On the west side there will be six frames
Exodus26:23 along with an extra frame at each corner.
Exodus26:24 These corner frames will be connected at the bottom and firmly attached at the top with a single ring
Exodus26:25 So there will be eight frames on that end of the Tabernacle
Exodus26:26 Make crossbars of acacia wood to run across the frames
Exodus26:27 and five for the south side. Also make five crossbars for the rear of the Tabernacle
Exodus26:28 The middle crossbar
Exodus26:29 Overlay the frames with gold and make gold rings to support the crossbars. Overlay the crossbars with gold as well.
Exodus26:30 Set up this Tabernacle according to the design you were shown on the mountain.
Exodus26:31 Across the inside of the Tabernacle hang a special curtain made of fine linen
Exodus26:32 Hang this inner curtain on gold hooks set into four posts made from acacia wood and overlaid with gold. The posts will fit into silver bases.
Exodus26:33 When the inner curtain is in place
Exodus26:34 Then put the Ark's cover--the place of atonement--on top of the Ark of the Covenant inside the Most Holy Place.
Exodus26:35 Place the table and lampstand across the room from each other outside the inner curtain. The lampstand must be placed on the south side
Exodus26:36 Make another curtain from fine linen for the entrance of the sacred tent
Exodus26:37 Hang this curtain on gold hooks set into five posts made from acacia wood and overlaid with gold. The posts will fit into five bronze bases.

Exodus28:1 Your brother
Exodus28:2 Make special clothing for Aaron to show his separation to God--beautiful garments that will lend dignity to his work.

Exodus28:4 They are to make a chestpiece
Exodus28:5 These items must be made of fine linen cloth and embroidered with gold thread and blue
Exodus28:6 The ephod must be made of fine linen cloth and skillfully embroidered with gold thread and blue
Exodus28:7 It will consist of two pieces
Exodus28:8 And the sash will be made of the same materials: fine linen cloth embroidered with gold thread and blue
Exodus28:9 Take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of the tribes of Israel.
Exodus28:10 Six names will be on each stone
Exodus28:11 Engrave these names in the same way a gemcutter engraves a seal. Mount the stones in gold settings.
Exodus28:12 Fasten the two stones on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for the people of Israel. Aaron will carry these names before the LORD as a constant reminder.
Exodus28:13 The settings are to be made of gold filigree
Exodus28:14 and two cords made of pure gold will be attached to the settings on the shoulders of the ephod.
Exodus28:15 Then
Exodus28:16 This chestpiece will be made of two folds of cloth
Exodus28:17 Four rows of gemstones will be attached to it. The first row will contain a red carnelian
Exodus28:18 The second row will contain a turquoise
Exodus28:19 The third row will contain a jacinth
Exodus28:20 The fourth row will contain a beryl
Exodus28:21 Each stone will represent one of the tribes of Israel
Exodus28:22 To attach the chestpiece to the ephod
Exodus28:23 Then make two gold rings and attach them to the top corners of the chestpiece.
Exodus28:24 The two gold cords will go through the rings on the chestpiece
Exodus28:25 and the ends of the cords will be tied to the gold settings on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod.
Exodus28:26 Then make two more gold rings
Exodus28:27 And make two more gold rings and attach them to the ephod near the sash.
Exodus28:28 Then attach the bottom rings of the chestpiece to the rings on the ephod with blue cords. This will hold the chestpiece securely to the ephod above the beautiful sash.
Exodus28:29 In this way

Exodus28:32 with an opening for Aaron's head in the middle of it. The opening will be reinforced by a woven collar so it will not tear.
Exodus28:33 Make pomegranates out of blue
Exodus28:34 The gold bells and pomegranates are to alternate all the way around the hem.
Exodus28:35 Aaron will wear this robe whenever he enters the Holy Place to minister to the LORD
Exodus28:36 Next make a medallion of pure gold. Using the techniques of an engraver
Exodus28:37 This medallion will be attached to the front of Aaron's turban by means of a blue cord.
Exodus28:38 Aaron will wear it on his forehead
Exodus28:39 Weave Aaron's patterned tunic from fine linen cloth. Fashion the turban out of this linen as well. Also make him an embroidered sash.
Exodus28:40 Then for Aaron's sons
Exodus28:41 Clothe Aaron and his sons with these garments
Exodus28:42 Also make linen underclothes for them
Exodus28:43 These must be worn whenever Aaron and his sons enter the Tabernacle or approach the altar in the Holy Place to perform their duties. Thus they will not incur guilt and die. This law is permanent for Aaron and his descendants.
Exodus29:1 This is the ceremony for the dedication of Aaron and his sons as priests: Take a young bull and two rams with no physical defects.
Exodus29:2 Then using fine wheat flour and no yeast
Exodus29:3 Place these various kinds of bread in a single basket
Exodus29:4 Present Aaron and his sons at the entrance of the Tabernacle
Exodus29:5 Then put Aaron's tunic on him
Exodus29:6 And place on his head the turban with the gold medallion.
Exodus29:7 Then take the anointing oil and pour it over his head.
Exodus29:8 Next present his sons
Exodus29:9 with their woven sashes and their headdresses. They will then be priests forever. In this way
Exodus29:10 Then bring the young bull to the entrance of the Tabernacle
Exodus29:11 You will then slaughter it in the LORD's presence at the entrance of the Tabernacle.
Exodus29:12 Smear some of its blood on the horns of the altar with your finger
Exodus29:13 Take all the fat that covers the internal organs
Exodus29:14 Then take the carcass (including the skin and the dung) outside the camp
Exodus29:15 Next Aaron and his sons must lay their hands on the head of one of the rams
Exodus29:16 as it is slaughtered. Its blood will be collected and sprinkled on the sides of the altar.
Exodus29:17 Cut up the ram and wash off the internal organs and the legs. Set them alongside the head and the other pieces of the body
Exodus29:18 and burn them all on the altar. This is a burnt offering to the LORD
Exodus29:19 Now take the other ram and have Aaron and his sons lay their hands on its head
Exodus29:20 as it is slaughtered. Collect the blood and place some of it on the tip of the right earlobes of Aaron and his sons. Also put it on their right thumbs and the big toes of their right feet. Sprinkle the rest of the blood on the sides of the altar.
Exodus29:21 Then take some of the blood from the altar and mix it with some of the anointing oil. Sprinkle it on Aaron and his sons and on their clothes. In this way
Exodus29:22 Since this is the ram for the ordination of Aaron and his sons
Exodus29:23 Then take one loaf of bread
Exodus29:24 Put all these in the hands of Aaron and his sons to be lifted up as a special gift to the LORD.
Exodus29:25 Afterward take the bread from their hands
Exodus29:26 Then take the breast of Aaron's ordination ram
Exodus29:27 Set aside as holy the parts of the ordination ram that belong to Aaron and his sons. This includes the breast and the thigh that were lifted up before the LORD in the ordination ceremony.
Exodus29:28 In the future
Exodus29:29 Aaron's sacred garments must be preserved for his descendants who will succeed him
Exodus29:30 Whoever is the next high priest after Aaron will wear these clothes for seven days before beginning to minister in the Tabernacle and the Holy Place.
Exodus29:31 Take the ram used in the ordination ceremony
Exodus29:32 Aaron and his sons are to eat this meat
Exodus29:33 They alone may eat the meat and bread used for their atonement in the ordination ceremony. The ordinary people may not eat them
Exodus29:34 If any of the ordination meat or bread remains until the morning
Exodus29:35 This is how you will ordain Aaron and his sons to their offices. The ordination ceremony will go on for seven days.
Exodus29:36 Each day you must sacrifice a young bull as an offering for the atonement of sin. Afterward make an offering to cleanse the altar. Purify the altar by making atonement for it; make it holy by anointing it with oil.
Exodus29:37 Make atonement for the altar every day for seven days. After that
Exodus29:38 This is what you are to offer on the altar. Offer two one-year-old lambs each day
Exodus29:39 one in the morning and the other in the evening.
Exodus29:40 With one of them
Exodus29:41 Offer the other lamb in the evening
Exodus29:42 This is to be a daily burnt offering given from generation to generation. Offer it in the LORD's presence at the Tabernacle entrance
Exodus29:43 I will meet the people of Israel there
Exodus29:44 Yes
Exodus29:45 I will live among the people of Israel and be their God
Exodus29:46 and they will know that I am the LORD their God. I am the one who brought them out of Egypt so that I could live among them. I am the LORD their God.

Exodus34:1 The LORD told Moses
Exodus34:2 Be ready in the morning to come up Mount Sinai and present yourself to me there on the top of the mountain.
Exodus34:3 No one else may come with you. In fact
Exodus34:4 So Moses cut two tablets of stone like the first ones. Early in the morning he climbed Mount Sinai as the LORD had told him
Exodus34:5 Then the LORD came down in a pillar of cloud and called out his own name
Exodus34:6 He passed in front of Moses and said
Exodus34:7 I show this unfailing love to many thousands by forgiving every kind of sin and rebellion. Even so I do not leave sin unpunished
Exodus34:8 Moses immediately fell to the ground and worshiped.
Exodus34:9 And he said
Exodus34:10 The LORD replied
Exodus34:11 Your responsibility is to obey all the commands I am giving you today. Then I will surely drive out all those who stand in your way--the Amorites
Exodus34:12 Be very careful never to make treaties with the people in the land where you are going. If you do
Exodus34:13 Instead
Exodus34:14 You must worship no other gods
Exodus34:15 Do not make treaties of any kind with the people living in the land. They are spiritual prostitutes
Exodus34:16 And you will accept their daughters
Exodus34:17 You must make no gods for yourselves at all.
Exodus34:18 Be sure to celebrate the Festival of Unleavened Bread for seven days
Exodus34:19 Every firstborn male belongs to me--of both cattle and sheep.
Exodus34:20 A firstborn male donkey may be redeemed from the LORD by presenting a lamb in its place. But if you decide not to make the exchange
Exodus34:21 Six days are set aside for work
Exodus34:22 And you must remember to celebrate the Festival of Harvest with the first crop of the wheat harvest
Exodus34:23 Three times each year all the men of Israel must appear before the Sovereign LORD
Exodus34:24 No one will attack and conquer your land when you go to appear before the LORD your God those three times each year. I will drive out the nations that stand in your way and will enlarge your boundaries.
Exodus34:25 You must not offer bread made with yeast as a sacrifice to me. And none of the meat of the Passover lamb may be kept over until the following morning.
Exodus34:26 You must bring the best of the first of each year's crop to the house of the LORD your God. You must not cook a young goat in its mother's milk.
Exodus34:27 And the LORD said to Moses
Exodus34:28 Moses was up on the mountain with the LORD forty days and forty nights. In all that time he neither ate nor drank. At that time he wrote the terms of the covenant--the Ten Commandments--on the stone tablets.
Exodus34:29 When Moses came down the mountain carrying the stone tablets inscribed with the terms of the covenant
Exodus34:30 And when Aaron and the people of Israel saw the radiance of Moses' face
Exodus34:31 But Moses called to them and asked Aaron and the community leaders to come over and talk with him.
Exodus34:32 Then all the people came
Exodus34:33 When Moses had finished speaking with them
Exodus34:34 But whenever he went into the Tent of Meeting to speak with the LORD
Exodus34:35 and the people would see his face aglow. Afterward he would put the veil on again until he returned to speak with the LORD.
Exodus35:1 Now Moses called a meeting of all the people and told them
Exodus35:2 Each week
Exodus35:3 Do not even light fires in your homes on that day.
Exodus35:4 Then Moses said to all the people
Exodus35:5 Everyone is invited to bring these offerings to the LORD: gold
Exodus35:6 blue
Exodus35:7 tanned ram skins and fine goatskin leather; acacia wood;
Exodus35:8 olive oil for the lamps; spices for the anointing oil and the fragrant incense;
Exodus35:9 onyx stones
Exodus35:10 Come
Exodus35:11 the entire Tabernacle
Exodus35:12 the Ark and its poles; the Ark's cover--the place of atonement; the inner curtain to enclose the Ark in the Most Holy Place;
Exodus35:13 the table
Exodus35:14 the lampstand and its accessories; the lamp cups and the oil for lighting;
Exodus35:15 the incense altar and its carrying poles; the anointing oil and fragrant incense; the curtain for the entrance of the Tabernacle;
Exodus35:16 the altar of burnt offering; the bronze grating of the altar and its carrying poles and utensils; the large washbasin with its pedestal;
Exodus35:17 the curtains for the walls of the courtyard; the posts and their bases; the curtain for the entrance to the courtyard;
Exodus35:18 the tent pegs of the Tabernacle and courtyard and their cords;
Exodus35:19 the beautifully stitched clothing for the priests to wear while ministering in the Holy Place; the sacred garments for Aaron and his sons to wear while officiating as priests.
Exodus35:20 So all the people left Moses and went to their tents to prepare their gifts.
Exodus35:21 If their hearts were stirred and they desired to do so
Exodus35:22 Both men and women came
Exodus35:23 Others brought blue
Exodus35:24 Others brought silver and bronze objects as their offering to the LORD. And those who had acacia wood brought it.
Exodus35:25 All the women who were skilled in sewing and spinning prepared blue
Exodus35:26 All the women who were willing used their skills to spin and weave the goat hair into cloth.
Exodus35:27 The leaders brought onyx stones and the other gemstones to be used for the ephod and the chestpiece.
Exodus35:28 They also brought spices and olive oil for the light
Exodus35:29 So the people of Israel--every man and woman who wanted to help in the work the LORD had given them through Moses--brought their offerings to the LORD.
Exodus35:30 And Moses told them
Exodus35:31 The LORD has filled Bezalel with the Spirit of God
Exodus35:32 He is able to create beautiful objects from gold
Exodus35:33 He is skilled in cutting and setting gemstones and in carving wood. In fact
Exodus35:34 And the LORD has given both him and Oholiab son of Ahisamach
Exodus35:35 The LORD has given them special skills as jewelers
Exodus36:1 Bezalel
Exodus36:2 So Moses told Bezalel and Oholiab to begin the work
Exodus36:3 Moses gave them the materials donated by the people for the completion of the sanctuary. Additional gifts were brought each morning.
Exodus36:4 But finally the craftsmen left their work to meet with Moses.
Exodus36:5 We have more than enough materials on hand now to complete the job the LORD has given us to do! they exclaimed.
Exodus36:6 So Moses gave the command
Exodus36:7 Their contributions were more than enough to complete the whole project.
Exodus36:8 The skilled weavers first made ten sheets from fine linen. One of the craftsmen then embroidered blue
Exodus36:9 Each sheet was exactly the same size--forty-two feet long and six feet wide.
Exodus36:10 Five of these sheets were joined together to make one set
Exodus36:11 Fifty blue loops were placed along the edge of the last sheet in each set.
Exodus36:12 The fifty loops along the edge of the first set of sheets matched the loops along the edge of the second set.
Exodus36:13 Then fifty gold clasps were made to connect the loops on the edge of each set. Thus the Tabernacle was joined together in one piece.
Exodus36:14 Above the Tabernacle
Exodus36:15 Each sheet was exactly the same size--forty-five feet long and six feet wide.
Exodus36:16 The craftsmen joined five of these sheets together to make one set
Exodus36:17 Then they made fifty loops along the edge of the last sheet in each set.
Exodus36:18 They also made fifty small bronze clasps to couple the loops
Exodus36:19 Then they made two more layers for the roof covering. The first was made of tanned ram skins
Exodus36:20 For the framework of the Tabernacle
Exodus36:21 Each frame was 15 feet high and 2 1/4 feet wide.
Exodus36:22 There were two pegs on each frame so they could be joined to the next frame. All the frames were made this way.
Exodus36:23 They made twenty frames to support the south side
Exodus36:24 along with forty silver bases
Exodus36:25 They also made twenty frames for the north side of the Tabernacle
Exodus36:26 along with forty silver bases
Exodus36:27 The west side of the Tabernacle
Exodus36:28 plus an extra frame at each corner.
Exodus36:29 These corner frames were connected at the bottom and firmly attached at the top with a single ring
Exodus36:30 So for the west side they made a total of eight frames
Exodus36:31 Then they made five crossbars from acacia wood to tie the frames on the south side together.
Exodus36:32 They made another five for the north side and five for the west side.
Exodus36:33 The middle crossbar of the five was halfway up the frames
Exodus36:34 The frames and crossbars were all overlaid with gold. The rings used to hold the crossbars were made of pure gold.
Exodus36:35 The inner curtain was made of fine linen cloth
Exodus36:36 This curtain was then attached to four gold hooks set into four posts of acacia wood. The posts were overlaid with gold and set into four silver bases.
Exodus36:37 Then they made another curtain for the entrance to the sacred tent. It was made of fine linen cloth and embroidered with blue
Exodus36:38 This curtain was connected by five hooks to five posts. The posts with their decorated tops and bands were overlaid with gold. The five bases were molded from bronze.
Exodus37:1 Next Bezalel made the Ark out of acacia wood. It was 3 3/4 feet long
Exodus37:2 It was overlaid with pure gold inside and out
Exodus37:3 Four gold rings were fastened to its four feet
Exodus37:4 Then he made poles from acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
Exodus37:5 He put the poles into the rings at the sides of the Ark to carry it.
Exodus37:6 Then
Exodus37:7 He made two figures of cherubim out of hammered gold and placed them at the two ends of the atonement cover.
Exodus37:8 They were made so they were actually a part of the atonement cover--it was all one piece.
Exodus37:9 The cherubim faced each other as they looked down on the atonement cover
Exodus37:10 Then he made a table out of acacia wood
Exodus37:11 It was overlaid with pure gold
Exodus37:12 A rim about 3 inches wide was attached along the edges of the table
Exodus37:13 Then he cast four rings of gold and attached them to the four table legs
Exodus37:14 next to the rim. These were made to hold the carrying poles in place.
Exodus37:15 He made the carrying poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
Exodus37:16 Next
Exodus37:17 Then he made the lampstand
Exodus37:18 The lampstand had six branches
Exodus37:19 Each of the six branches held a cup shaped like an almond blossom
Exodus37:20 The center stem of the lampstand was also decorated with four almond blossoms.
Exodus37:21 One blossom was set beneath each pair of branches
Exodus37:22 The decorations and branches were all one piece with the stem
Exodus37:23 He also made the seven lamps
Exodus37:24 The entire lampstand
Exodus37:25 The incense altar was made of acacia wood. It was eighteen inches square and three feet high
Exodus37:26 He overlaid the top
Exodus37:27 Two gold rings were placed on opposite sides
Exodus37:28 The carrying poles were made of acacia wood and were overlaid with gold.
Exodus37:29 Then he made the sacred oil
Exodus38:1 The altar for burning animal sacrifices also was constructed of acacia wood. It was 7 1/2 feet square at the top and 4 1/2 feet high.
Exodus38:2 There were four horns
Exodus38:3 Then he made all the bronze utensils to be used with the altar--the ash buckets
Exodus38:4 Next he made a bronze grating that rested on a ledge about halfway down into the firebox.
Exodus38:5 Four rings were cast for each side of the grating to support the carrying poles.
Exodus38:6 The carrying poles themselves were made of acacia wood and were overlaid with bronze.
Exodus38:7 These poles were inserted into the rings at the side of the altar. The altar was hollow and was made from planks.
Exodus38:8 The bronze washbasin and its bronze pedestal were cast from bronze mirrors donated by the women who served at the entrance of the Tabernacle.
Exodus38:9 Then he constructed the courtyard. The south wall was 150 feet long. It consisted of curtains made of fine linen.
Exodus38:10 There were twenty posts
Exodus38:11 The north wall was also 150 feet long
Exodus38:12 The west end was 75 feet wide. The walls were made from curtains supported by ten posts and bases and with silver hooks and rods.
Exodus38:13 The east end was also 75 feet wide.
Exodus38:14 The courtyard entrance was on the east side
Exodus38:15 The curtain on the left side was also 22 1/2 feet long and was supported by three posts set into three bases.
Exodus38:16 All the curtains used in the courtyard walls were made of fine linen.
Exodus38:17 Each post had a bronze base
Exodus38:18 The curtain that covered the entrance to the courtyard was made of fine linen cloth and embroidered with blue
Exodus38:19 It was supported by four posts set into four bronze bases. The tops of the posts were overlaid with silver
Exodus38:20 All the tent pegs used in the Tabernacle and courtyard were made of bronze.
Exodus38:21 Here is an inventory of the materials used in building the Tabernacle of the Covenant. Moses directed the Levites to compile the figures
Exodus38:22 Bezalel son of Uri
Exodus38:23 He was assisted by Oholiab son of Ahisamach
Exodus38:24 The people brought gifts of gold totaling about 2
Exodus38:25 The amount of silver that was given was about 7
Exodus38:26 It came from the tax of one-fifth of an ounce of silver collected from each of those registered in the census. This included all the men who were twenty years old or older
Exodus38:27 The 100 bases for the frames of the sanctuary walls and for the posts supporting the inner curtain required 7
Exodus38:28 The rest of the silver
Exodus38:29 The people also brought 5
Exodus38:30 which was used for casting the bases for the posts at the entrance to the Tabernacle
Exodus38:31 Bronze was also used to make the bases for the posts that supported the curtains around the courtyard
Exodus39:1 For the priests
Exodus39:2 The ephod was made from fine linen cloth and embroidered with gold thread and blue
Exodus39:3 A skilled craftsman made gold thread by beating gold into thin sheets and cutting it into fine strips. He then embroidered it into the linen with the blue
Exodus39:4 They made two shoulder-pieces for the ephod
Exodus39:5 They also made an elaborate woven sash of the same materials: fine linen cloth; blue
Exodus39:6 The two onyx stones
Exodus39:7 These stones served as reminders to the LORD concerning the people of Israel. All this was done just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Exodus39:8 The chestpiece was made in the same style as the ephod
Exodus39:9 It was doubled over to form a pouch
Exodus39:10 Four rows of gemstones were set across it. In the first row were a red carnelian
Exodus39:11 In the second row were a turquoise
Exodus39:12 In the third row were a jacinth
Exodus39:13 In the fourth row were a beryl
Exodus39:14 The stones were engraved like a seal
Exodus39:15 To attach the chestpiece to the ephod
Exodus39:16 They also made two gold rings and attached them to the top corners of the chestpiece.
Exodus39:17 The two gold cords were put through the gold rings on the chestpiece
Exodus39:18 and the ends of the cords were tied to the gold settings on the shoulder-pieces of the ephod.
Exodus39:19 Two more gold rings were attached to the lower inside corners of the chestpiece next to the ephod.
Exodus39:20 Then two gold rings were attached to the ephod near the sash.
Exodus39:21 Blue cords were used to attach the bottom rings of the chestpiece to the rings on the ephod. In this way
Exodus39:22 The robe of the ephod was woven entirely of blue yarn
Exodus39:23 with an opening for Aaron's head in the middle of it. The edge of this opening was reinforced with a woven collar
Exodus39:24 Pomegranates were attached to the bottom edge of the robe. These were finely crafted of blue
Exodus39:25 Bells of pure gold were placed between the pomegranates along the hem of the robe
Exodus39:26 with bells and pomegranates alternating all around the hem. This robe was to be worn when Aaron ministered to the LORD
Exodus39:27 Tunics were then made for Aaron and his sons from fine linen cloth.
Exodus39:28 The turban
Exodus39:29 The sashes were made of fine linen cloth and embroidered with blue
Exodus39:30 Finally
Exodus39:31 This medallion was tied to the turban with a blue cord
Exodus39:32 And so at last the Tabernacle was finished. The Israelites had done everything just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Exodus39:33 And they brought the entire Tabernacle to Moses: the sacred tent with all its furnishings
Exodus39:34 the layers of tanned ram skins and fine goatskin leather; the inner curtain that enclosed the Most Holy Place;
Exodus39:35 the Ark of the Covenant and its carrying poles; the Ark's cover--the place of atonement;
Exodus39:36 the table and all its utensils; the Bread of the Presence;
Exodus39:37 the gold lampstand and its accessories; the lamp cups and the oil for lighting;
Exodus39:38 the gold altar; the anointing oil; the fragrant incense; the curtain for the entrance of the sacred tent;
Exodus39:39 the bronze altar; the bronze grating; its poles and utensils; the large washbasin and its pedestal;
Exodus39:40 the curtains for the walls of the courtyard and the posts and bases holding them up; the curtain at the courtyard entrance; the cords and tent pegs; all the articles used in the operation of the Tabernacle;
Exodus39:41 the beautifully crafted garments to be worn while ministering in the Holy Place--the holy garments for Aaron the priest and for his sons to wear while on duty.
Exodus39:42 So the people of Israel followed all of the LORD's instructions to Moses.
Exodus39:43 Moses inspected all their work and blessed them because it had been done as the LORD had commanded him.
Exodus40:1 The LORD now said to Moses
Exodus40:2 Set up the Tabernacle on the first day of the new year.
Exodus40:3 Place the Ark of the Covenant inside
Exodus40:4 Then bring in the table
Exodus40:5 Place the incense altar just outside the inner curtain
Exodus40:6 Place the altar of burnt offering in front of the Tabernacle entrance.
Exodus40:7 Set the large washbasin between the Tabernacle and the altar and fill it with water.
Exodus40:8 Then set up the courtyard around the outside of the tent
Exodus40:9 Take the anointing oil and sprinkle it on the Tabernacle and on all its furnishings to make them holy.
Exodus40:10 Sprinkle the anointing oil on the altar of burnt offering and its utensils
Exodus40:11 Next anoint the large washbasin and its pedestal to make them holy.
Exodus40:12 Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance of the Tabernacle
Exodus40:13 Clothe Aaron with the holy garments and anoint him
Exodus40:14 Then bring his sons and dress them in their tunics.
Exodus40:15 Anoint them as you did their father
Exodus40:16 Moses proceeded to do everything as the LORD had commanded him.
Exodus40:17 So the Tabernacle was set up on the first day of the new year.
Exodus40:18 Moses put it together by setting its frames into their bases and attaching the crossbars and raising the posts.
Exodus40:19 Then he spread the coverings over the Tabernacle framework and put on the roof layers
Exodus40:20 He placed inside the Ark the stone tablets inscribed with the terms of the covenant
Exodus40:21 Then he brought the Ark of the Covenant into the Tabernacle and set up the inner curtain to shield it from view
Exodus40:22 Next he placed the table in the Tabernacle
Exodus40:23 And he arranged the Bread of the Presence on the table that stands before the LORD
Exodus40:24 He set the lampstand in the Tabernacle across from the table on the south side of the Holy Place.
Exodus40:25 Then he set up the lamps in the LORD's presence
Exodus40:26 He also placed the incense altar in the Tabernacle
Exodus40:27 On it he burned the fragrant incense made from sweet spices
Exodus40:28 He attached the curtain at the entrance of the Tabernacle
Exodus40:29 and he placed the altar of burnt offering near the Tabernacle entrance. On it he offered a burnt offering and a grain offering
Exodus40:30 Next he placed the large washbasin between the Tabernacle and the altar. He filled it with water so the priests could use it to wash themselves.
Exodus40:31 Moses and Aaron and Aaron's sons washed their hands and feet in the basin.
Exodus40:32 Whenever they walked past the altar to enter the Tabernacle
Exodus40:33 Then he hung the curtains forming the courtyard around the Tabernacle and the altar. And he set up the curtain at the entrance of the courtyard. So at last Moses finished the work.
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud covered the Tabernacle
Exodus40:35 Moses was no longer able to enter the Tabernacle because the cloud had settled down over it
Exodus40:36 Now whenever the cloud lifted from the Tabernacle and moved
Exodus40:37 But if the cloud stayed
Exodus40:38 The cloud of the LORD rested on the Tabernacle during the day
Leviticus1:1 The LORD called to Moses from the Tabernacle and said to him
Leviticus1:2 Give the following instructions to the Israelites: Whenever you present offerings to the LORD
Leviticus1:3 If your sacrifice for a whole burnt offering is from the herd
Leviticus1:4 Lay your hand on its head so the LORD will accept it as your substitute
Leviticus1:5 Then slaughter the animal in the LORD's presence
Leviticus1:6 When the animal has been skinned and cut into pieces
Leviticus1:7 the sons of Aaron the priest will build a wood fire on the altar.
Leviticus1:8 Aaron's sons will then put the pieces of the animal
Leviticus1:9 But the internal organs and legs must first be washed with water. Then the priests will burn the entire sacrifice on the altar. It is a whole burnt offering made by fire
Leviticus1:10 If your sacrifice for a whole burnt offering is from the flock
Leviticus1:11 Slaughter the animal on the north side of the altar in the LORD's presence. Aaron's sons
Leviticus1:12 Then you must cut the animal in pieces
Leviticus1:13 The internal organs and legs must first be washed with water. Then the priests will burn the entire sacrifice on the altar. It is a whole burnt offering made by fire
Leviticus1:14 If you bring a bird as a burnt offering to the LORD
Leviticus1:15 The priest will take the bird to the altar
Leviticus1:16 The priest must remove the crop and the feathers and throw them to the east side of the altar among the ashes.
Leviticus1:17 Then
Leviticus2:1 When you bring a grain offering to the LORD
Leviticus2:2 Bring this offering to one of Aaron's sons
Leviticus2:3 The rest of the flour will be given to Aaron and his sons. It will be considered a most holy part of the offerings given to the LORD by fire.
Leviticus2:4 When you present some kind of baked bread as a grain offering
Leviticus2:5 If your grain offering is cooked on a griddle
Leviticus2:6 Break it into pieces and pour oil on it; it is a kind of grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 If your offering is prepared in a pan
Leviticus2:8 No matter how a grain offering has been prepared before being offered to the LORD
Leviticus2:9 The priests will take a token portion of the grain offering and burn it on the altar as an offering made by fire
Leviticus2:10 The rest of the grain offering will be given to Aaron and his sons as their food. It will be considered a most holy part of the offerings given to the LORD by fire.
Leviticus2:11 Do not use yeast in any of the grain offerings you present to the LORD
Leviticus2:12 You may add yeast and honey to the offerings presented at harvesttime
Leviticus2:13 Season all your grain offerings with salt
Leviticus2:14 If you present a grain offering to the LORD from the first portion of your harvest
Leviticus2:15 Since it is a grain offering
Leviticus2:16 The priests will take a token portion of the roasted grain mixed with olive oil
Leviticus3:1 If you want to present a peace offering from the herd
Leviticus3:2 Lay your hand on the animal's head
Leviticus3:3 Part of this peace offering must be presented to the LORD as an offering made by fire. This includes the fat around the internal organs
Leviticus3:4 the two kidneys with the fat around them near the loins
Leviticus3:5 The sons of Aaron will burn these on the altar on top of the burnt offering on the wood fire. It is an offering made by fire
Leviticus3:6 If you present a peace offering to the LORD from the flock
Leviticus3:7 If you bring a sheep as your gift
Leviticus3:8 by laying your hand on its head and slaughtering it at the entrance of the Tabernacle. The sons of Aaron will then sprinkle the sheep's blood against the sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:9 Part of this peace offering must be presented to the LORD as an offering made by fire. This includes the fat of the entire tail cut off near the backbone
Leviticus3:10 the two kidneys with the fat around them near the loins
Leviticus3:11 The priest will burn them on the altar as food
Leviticus3:12 If you bring a goat as your offering to the LORD
Leviticus3:13 lay your hand on its head
Leviticus3:14 Part of this offering must be presented to the LORD as an offering made by fire. This part includes the fat around the internal organs
Leviticus3:15 the two kidneys with the fat around them near the loins
Leviticus3:16 The priest will burn them on the altar as food
Leviticus3:17 You must never eat any fat or blood. This is a permanent law for you and all your descendants
Leviticus4:1 Then the LORD said to Moses
Leviticus4:2 Give the Israelites the following instructions for dealing with those who sin unintentionally by doing anything forbidden by the LORD's commands.
Leviticus4:3 If the high priest sins
Leviticus4:4 He must present the bull to the LORD at the entrance of the Tabernacle
Leviticus4:5 The priest on duty will then take some of the animal's blood into the Tabernacle
Leviticus4:6 dip his finger into the blood
Leviticus4:7 The priest will put some of the blood on the horns of the incense altar that stands in the LORD's presence in the Tabernacle. The rest of the bull's blood must be poured out at the base of the altar of burnt offerings at the entrance of the Tabernacle.
Leviticus4:8 The priest must remove all the fat around the bull's internal organs
Leviticus4:9 the two kidneys with the fat around them near the loins
Leviticus4:10 Then he must burn them on the altar of burnt offerings
Leviticus4:11 But the rest of the bull--its hide
Leviticus4:12 must be carried away to a ceremonially clean place outside the camp
Leviticus4:13 If the entire Israelite community does something forbidden by the LORD and the matter escapes the community's notice
Leviticus4:14 When they discover their sin
Leviticus4:15 The leaders must then lay their hands on the bull's head and slaughter it there before the LORD.
Leviticus4:16 The priest will bring some of its blood into the Tabernacle
Leviticus4:17 dip his finger into the blood
Leviticus4:18 He will then put some of the blood on the horns of the incense altar that stands in the LORD's presence in the Tabernacle. The rest of the blood must then be poured out at the base of the altar of burnt offerings at the entrance of the Tabernacle.
Leviticus4:19 The priest must remove all the animal's fat and burn it on the altar
Leviticus4:20 following the same procedure as with the sin offering for the priest. In this way
Leviticus4:21 The priest must then take what is left of the bull outside the camp and burn it there
Leviticus4:22 If one of Israel's leaders does something forbidden by the LORD his God
Leviticus4:23 When he becomes aware of his sin
Leviticus4:24 He is to lay his hand on the goat's head and slaughter it before the LORD at the place where burnt offerings are slaughtered. This will be his sin offering.
Leviticus4:25 Then the priest will dip his finger into the blood of the sin offering
Leviticus4:26 He must burn all the goat's fat on the altar
Leviticus4:27 If any of the citizens of Israel do something forbidden by the LORD
Leviticus4:28 When they become aware of their sin
Leviticus4:29 They are to lay a hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter it at the place where burnt offerings are slaughtered.
Leviticus4:30 The priest will then dip his finger into the blood
Leviticus4:31 Those who are guilty must remove all the goat's fat
Leviticus4:32 If any of the people bring a sheep as their sin offering
Leviticus4:33 They are to lay a hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter it at the place where the burnt offerings are slaughtered.
Leviticus4:34 The priest will then dip his finger into the blood
Leviticus4:35 Those who are guilty must remove all the sheep's fat
Leviticus5:1 If any of the people are called to testify about something they have witnessed
Leviticus5:2 Or if they touch something that is ceremonially unclean
Leviticus5:3 Or if they come into contact with any source of human defilement
Leviticus5:4 Or if they make a rash vow of any kind
Leviticus5:5 When any of the people become aware of their guilt in any of these ways
Leviticus5:6 and bring to the LORD as their penalty a female from the flock
Leviticus5:7 If any of them cannot afford to bring a sheep
Leviticus5:8 They must bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 Then he will sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering against the sides of the altar
Leviticus5:10 The priest will offer the second bird as a whole burnt offering
Leviticus5:11 If any of the people cannot afford to bring young turtledoves or pigeons
Leviticus5:12 They must take the flour to the priest
Leviticus5:13 In this way
Leviticus5:14 Then the LORD said to Moses
Leviticus5:15 If any of the people sin by unintentionally defiling the LORD's sacred property
Leviticus5:16 They must then make restitution for whatever holy things they have defiled by paying for the loss
Leviticus5:17 If any of them sin by doing something forbidden by the LORD
Leviticus5:18 they must bring to the priest a ram from the flock as a guilt offering. The animal must have no physical defects
Leviticus5:19 This is a guilt offering
Leviticus6:1 And the LORD said to Moses
Leviticus6:2 Suppose some of the people sin against the LORD by falsely telling their neighbor that an item entrusted to their safekeeping has been lost or stolen. Or suppose they have been dishonest with regard to a security deposit
Leviticus6:3 Or suppose they find a lost item and lie about it
Leviticus6:4 If they have sinned in any of these ways and are guilty
Leviticus6:5 or anything gained by swearing falsely. When they realize their guilt
Leviticus6:6 They must then bring a guilt offering to the priest
Leviticus6:7 The priest will then make atonement for them before the LORD
Leviticus6:8 Then the LORD said to Moses
Leviticus6:9 Give Aaron and his sons the following instructions regarding the whole burnt offering. The burnt offering must be left on the altar until the next morning
Leviticus6:10 The next morning
Leviticus6:11 Then he must change back into his normal clothing and carry the ashes outside the camp to a place that is ceremonially clean.
Leviticus6:12 Meanwhile
Leviticus6:13 Remember
Leviticus6:14 These are the instructions regarding the grain offering. Aaron's sons must present this offering to the LORD in front of the altar.
Leviticus6:15 The priest on duty will take a handful of the choice flour that has been mixed with olive oil and sprinkled with incense. He will burn this token portion on the altar
Leviticus6:16 After burning this handful
Leviticus6:17 Remember
Leviticus6:18 Any of Aaron's male descendants
Leviticus6:19 And the LORD said to Moses
Leviticus6:20 On the day Aaron and his sons are anointed
Leviticus6:21 It must be cooked on a griddle with olive oil
Leviticus6:22 As the sons of the priests replace their fathers
Leviticus6:23 All such grain offerings of the priests must be entirely burned up. None of the flour may be eaten.
Leviticus6:24 Then the LORD said to Moses
Leviticus6:25 Give Aaron and his sons these further instructions regarding the sin offering. The animal given as a sin offering is most holy and must be slaughtered in the LORD's presence at the place where the burnt offerings are slaughtered.
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers the sacrifice may eat his portion in a sacred place within the courtyard of the Tabernacle.
Leviticus6:27 Anything or anyone who touches the sacrificial meat will become holy
Leviticus6:28 If a clay pot is used to boil the sacrificial meat
Leviticus6:29 Only males from a priest's family may eat of this offering
Leviticus6:30 If
Leviticus7:1 These are the instructions for the guilt offering
Leviticus7:2 The animal sacrificed as a guilt offering must be slaughtered where the burnt offerings are slaughtered
Leviticus7:3 The priest will then offer all its fat on the altar
Leviticus7:4 the two kidneys with the fat around them near the loins
Leviticus7:5 The priests will burn these parts on the altar as an offering to the LORD made by fire. It is a guilt offering.
Leviticus7:6 All males from a priest's family may eat the meat
Leviticus7:7 For both the sin offering and the guilt offering
Leviticus7:8 In the case of the whole burnt offering
Leviticus7:9 Any grain offering that has been baked in an oven
Leviticus7:10 All other grain offerings
Leviticus7:11 These are the instructions regarding the different kinds of peace offerings that may be presented to the LORD.
Leviticus7:12 If you present your peace offering as a thanksgiving offering
Leviticus7:13 This peace offering of thanksgiving must also be accompanied by loaves of yeast bread.
Leviticus7:14 One of each kind of bread must be presented as a gift to the LORD. This bread will then belong to the priest who sprinkles the altar with blood from the sacrificed animal.
Leviticus7:15 The animal's meat must be eaten on the same day it is offered. None of it may be saved for the next morning.
Leviticus7:16 However
Leviticus7:17 But anything left over until the third day must be completely burned up.
Leviticus7:18 If any of the meat from this peace offering is eaten on the third day
Leviticus7:19 Meat that touches anything ceremonially unclean may not be eaten; it must be completely burned up. And as for meat that may be eaten
Leviticus7:20 Anyone who is ceremonially unclean but eats meat from a peace offering that was presented to the LORD must be cut off from the community.
Leviticus7:21 If anyone touches anything that is unclean
Leviticus7:22 Then the LORD said to Moses
Leviticus7:23 Give the Israelites these instructions: You must never eat fat
Leviticus7:24 The fat of an animal found dead or killed by a wild animal may never be eaten
Leviticus7:25 Anyone who eats fat from an offering given to the LORD by fire must be cut off from the community.
Leviticus7:26 Even in your homes
Leviticus7:27 Anyone who eats blood must be cut off from the community.
Leviticus7:28 Then the LORD said to Moses
Leviticus7:29 Give these further instructions to the Israelites: When you present a peace offering to the LORD
Leviticus7:30 Present it to him with your own hands as an offering given to the LORD by fire. Bring the fat of the animal
Leviticus7:31 Then the priest will burn the fat on the altar
Leviticus7:32 You are to give the right thigh of your peace offering to the priest as a gift.
Leviticus7:33 The right thigh must always be given to the priest who sprinkles the blood and offers the fat of the peace offering.
Leviticus7:34 For I have designated the breast and the right thigh for the priests. It is their regular share of the peace offerings brought by the Israelites.
Leviticus7:35 This is their share. It has been set apart for Aaron and his descendants from the offerings given to the LORD by fire from the time they were appointed to serve the LORD as priests.
Leviticus7:36 The LORD commanded that the Israelites were to give these portions to the priests as their regular share from the time of the priests' anointing. This regulation applies throughout the generations to come.
Leviticus7:37 These are the instructions for the whole burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 The LORD gave these instructions to Moses on Mount Sinai when he commanded the Israelites to bring their offerings to the LORD in the wilderness of Sinai.
Leviticus8:1 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus8:2 Now bring Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:3 to the entrance of the Tabernacle. Then call the entire community of Israel to meet you there.
Leviticus8:4 So Moses followed the LORD's instructions
Leviticus8:5 Moses announced to them
Leviticus8:6 Then he presented Aaron and his sons and washed them with water.
Leviticus8:7 He clothed Aaron with the embroidered tunic and tied the sash around his waist. He dressed him in the robe of the ephod
Leviticus8:8 Then Moses placed the chestpiece on Aaron and put the Urim and the Thummim inside it.
Leviticus8:9 He placed on Aaron's head the turban with the gold medallion at its front
Leviticus8:10 Then Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the Tabernacle and everything in it
Leviticus8:11 He sprinkled the altar seven times
Leviticus8:12 Then he poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron's head
Leviticus8:13 Next Moses presented Aaron's sons and clothed them in their embroidered tunics
Leviticus8:14 Then Moses brought in the bull for the sin offering
Leviticus8:15 as Moses slaughtered it. Moses took some of the blood
Leviticus8:16 He took all the fat around the internal organs
Leviticus8:17 The rest of the bull
Leviticus8:18 Then Moses presented the ram to the LORD for the whole burnt offering
Leviticus8:19 as Moses slaughtered it. Then Moses took the ram's blood and sprinkled it against the sides of the altar.
Leviticus8:20 Next he cut the ram into pieces and burned the head
Leviticus8:21 After washing the internal organs and the legs with water
Leviticus8:22 Next Moses presented the second ram
Leviticus8:23 as Moses slaughtered it. Then Moses took some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron's right ear
Leviticus8:24 Next he presented Aaron's sons and put some of the blood on the lobe of their right ears
Leviticus8:25 Next he took the fat
Leviticus8:26 On top of these he placed a loaf of unleavened bread
Leviticus8:27 He gave all of these to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:28 Moses then took all the offerings back and burned them on the altar on top of the burnt offering as an ordination offering. It was an offering given to the LORD by fire
Leviticus8:29 Then Moses took the breast and lifted it up in the LORD's presence. This was Moses' share of the ram of ordination
Leviticus8:30 Next Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar
Leviticus8:31 Then Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 Any meat or bread that is left over must then be burned up.
Leviticus8:33 Do not leave the Tabernacle entrance for seven days
Leviticus8:34 What has been done today was commanded by the LORD in order to make atonement for you.
Leviticus8:35 Remember
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did everything the LORD had commanded through Moses.

Leviticus11:1 Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 Give the following instructions to the Israelites: The animals you may use for food
Leviticus11:3 include those that have completely divided hooves and chew the cud.
Leviticus11:4 You may not
Leviticus11:5 The same is true of the rock badger
Leviticus11:6 and the hare
Leviticus11:7 And the pig may not be eaten
Leviticus11:8 You may not eat the meat of these animals or touch their dead bodies. They are ceremonially unclean for you.
Leviticus11:9 As for marine animals
Leviticus11:10 You may not
Leviticus11:11 and they will always be forbidden to you. You must never eat their meat or even touch their dead bodies.
Leviticus11:12 I repeat
Leviticus11:13 These are the birds you must never eat because they are detestable for you: the eagle
Leviticus11:14 the buzzard
Leviticus11:15 ravens of all kinds
Leviticus11:16 the ostrich
Leviticus11:17 the little owl
Leviticus11:18 the white owl
Leviticus11:19 the stork
Leviticus11:20 You are to consider detestable all swarming insects that walk along the ground.
Leviticus11:21 However
Leviticus11:22 locusts of all varieties
Leviticus11:23 But you are to consider detestable all other swarming insects that walk or crawl.
Leviticus11:24 The following creatures make you ceremonially unclean. If you touch any of their dead bodies
Leviticus11:25 If you move the dead body of an unclean animal
Leviticus11:26 Any animal that has divided but unsplit hooves or that does not chew the cud is unclean for you. If you touch the dead body of such an animal
Leviticus11:27 Of the animals that walk on all fours
Leviticus11:28 If you pick up and move its carcass
Leviticus11:29 Of the small animals that scurry or creep on the ground
Leviticus11:30 the gecko
Leviticus11:31 All these small animals are unclean for you. If you touch the dead body of such an animal
Leviticus11:32 If such an animal dies and falls on something
Leviticus11:33 If such an animal dies and falls into a clay pot
Leviticus11:34 If the water used to cleanse an unclean object touches any food
Leviticus11:35 Any object on which the dead body of such an animal falls will be defiled. If it is a clay oven or cooking pot
Leviticus11:36 However
Leviticus11:37 If the dead body falls on seed grain to be planted in the field
Leviticus11:38 But if the seed is wet when the dead body falls on it
Leviticus11:39 If an animal that is permitted for eating dies and you touch its carcass
Leviticus11:40 If you eat any of its meat or carry away its carcass
Leviticus11:41 Consider detestable any animal that scurries along the ground; such animals may never be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 This includes all animals that slither along on their bellies
Leviticus11:43 Never defile yourselves by touching such animals.
Leviticus11:44 After all
Leviticus11:45 I
Leviticus11:46 These are the instructions regarding the land animals
Leviticus11:47 so you can distinguish between what is unclean and may not be eaten and what is clean and may be eaten.

Leviticus13:1 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 If some of the people notice a swelling or a rash or a shiny patch on their skin that develops into a contagious skin disease
Leviticus13:3 The priest will then examine the affected area of a person's skin. If the hair in the affected area has turned white and appears to be more than skin-deep
Leviticus13:4 But if the affected area of the skin is white but does not appear to be more than skin-deep
Leviticus13:5 On the seventh day the priest will make another examination. If the affected area has not changed or spread on the skin
Leviticus13:6 The priest will examine the skin again on the seventh day. If the affected area has faded and not spread
Leviticus13:7 But if the rash continues to spread after this examination and pronouncement by the priest
Leviticus13:8 If the priest notices that the rash has spread
Leviticus13:9 Anyone who develops a contagious skin disease must go to the priest for an examination.
Leviticus13:10 If the priest sees that some hair has turned white and an open sore appears in the affected area
Leviticus13:11 it is clearly a contagious skin disease
Leviticus13:12 Now suppose the priest discovers after his examination that a rash has broken out all over someone's skin
Leviticus13:13 In such cases
Leviticus13:14 But if any open sores appear
Leviticus13:15 The priest must make this pronouncement as soon as he sees an open sore because open sores indicate the presence of a contagious skin disease.
Leviticus13:16 However
Leviticus13:17 If
Leviticus13:18 If anyone has had a boil on the skin that has started to heal
Leviticus13:19 but a white swelling or a reddish white spot remains in its place
Leviticus13:20 If the priest finds the disease to be more than skin-deep
Leviticus13:21 But if the priest sees that there is no white hair in the affected area
Leviticus13:22 If during that time the affected area spreads on the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the area grows no larger and does not spread
Leviticus13:24 If anyone has suffered a burn on the skin and the burned area changes color
Leviticus13:25 then the priest must examine it. If the hair in the affected area turns white and the problem appears to be more than skin-deep
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest discovers that there is no white hair in the affected area and the problem appears to be no more than skin-deep and has faded
Leviticus13:27 If at the end of that time the affected area has spread on the skin
Leviticus13:28 But if the affected area has not moved or spread on the skin and has faded
Leviticus13:29 If anyone
Leviticus13:30 the priest must examine the infection. If it appears to be more than skin-deep and fine yellow hair is found in the affected area
Leviticus13:31 However
Leviticus13:32 If at the end of that time the affected area has not spread and no yellow hair has appeared
Leviticus13:33 the infected person must shave off all hair except the hair on the affected area. Then the priest must put the person in quarantine for another seven days
Leviticus13:34 and he will examine the infection again on the seventh day. If it has not spread and appears to be no more than skin-deep
Leviticus13:35 But if the infection begins to spread after the person is pronounced clean
Leviticus13:36 the priest must do another examination. If the infection has spread
Leviticus13:37 But if it appears that the infection has stopped spreading and black hair has grown in the affected area
Leviticus13:38 If anyone
Leviticus13:39 the priest must examine the affected area. If the patch is only a pale white
Leviticus13:40 If a man loses his hair and his head becomes bald
Leviticus13:41 And if he loses hair on his forehead
Leviticus13:42 However
Leviticus13:43 The priest must examine him
Leviticus13:44 the man is infected with a contagious skin disease and is unclean. The priest must pronounce him ceremonially unclean because of the infection.
Leviticus13:45 Those who suffer from any contagious skin disease must tear their clothing and allow their hair to hang loose. Then
Leviticus13:46 As long as the disease lasts
Leviticus13:47 Now suppose an infectious mildew contaminates some woolen or linen clothing
Leviticus13:48 some woolen or linen fabric
Leviticus13:49 If the affected area in the clothing
Leviticus13:50 After examining the affected spot
Leviticus13:51 On the seventh day the priest must inspect it again. If the affected area has spread
Leviticus13:52 The priest must burn the linen or wool clothing or the piece of leather because it has been contaminated by an infectious mildew. It must be completely destroyed by fire.
Leviticus13:53 But if the priest examines it again and the affected spot has not spread in the clothing
Leviticus13:54 the priest will order the contaminated object to be washed and then isolated for seven more days.
Leviticus13:55 Then the priest must inspect the object again. If he sees that the affected area has not changed appearance after being washed
Leviticus13:56 But if the priest sees that the affected area has faded after being washed
Leviticus13:57 If the spot reappears at a later time
Leviticus13:58 But if the spot disappears after the object is washed
Leviticus13:59 These are the instructions for dealing with infectious mildew in woolen or linen clothing or fabric

Leviticus15:2 Give these further instructions to the Israelites: Any man who has a genital discharge is ceremonially unclean because of it.
Leviticus15:3 This defilement applies whether the discharge continues or is stopped up. In either case the man is unclean.
Leviticus15:4 Any bedding on which he lies and anything on which he sits will be defiled.

Leviticus15:6 If you sit where the man with the discharge has sat

Leviticus15:9 Any blanket on which the man rides will be defiled.
Leviticus15:10 If you touch or carry anything that was under him
Leviticus15:11 If the man touches you without first rinsing his hands
Leviticus15:12 Any clay pot touched by the man with the discharge must be broken
Leviticus15:13 When the man's discharge heals

Leviticus15:20 Anything on which she lies or sits during that time will be defiled.
Leviticus15:21 If you touch her bed
Leviticus15:22 The same applies if you touch an object on which she sits
Leviticus15:23 whether it is her bedding or any piece of furniture.
Leviticus15:24 If a man has sexual intercourse with her during this time
Leviticus15:25 If the menstrual flow of blood continues for many days beyond the normal period
Leviticus15:26 Anything on which she lies or sits during that time will be defiled

Leviticus16:18 Then Aaron will go out to make atonement for the altar that stands before the LORD by smearing some of the blood from the bull and the goat on each of the altar's horns.
Leviticus16:19 Then he must dip his finger into the blood and sprinkle it seven times over the altar. In this way
Leviticus16:20 When Aaron has finished making atonement for the Most Holy Place

Leviticus16:27 The bull and goat given as sin offerings
Leviticus16:28 The man who does the burning must wash his clothes and bathe himself in water before returning to the camp.

Leviticus16:30 On this day
Leviticus16:31 It will be a Sabbath day of total rest
Leviticus16:32 In future generations
Leviticus16:33 and make atonement for the Most Holy Place

Leviticus19:8 If you eat it on the third day
Leviticus19:9 When you harvest your crops
Leviticus19:10 It is the same with your grape crop--do not strip every last bunch of grapes from the vines
Leviticus19:11 Do not steal. Do not cheat one another. Do not lie.
Leviticus19:12 Do not use my name to swear a falsehood and so profane the name of your God. I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:13 Do not cheat or rob anyone. Always pay your hired workers promptly.
Leviticus19:14 Show your fear of God by treating the deaf with respect and by not taking advantage of the blind. I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:15 Always judge your neighbors fairly
Leviticus19:16 Do not spread slanderous gossip among your people. Do not try to get ahead at the cost of your neighbor's life
Leviticus19:17 Do not nurse hatred in your heart for any of your relatives. Confront your neighbors directly so you will not be held guilty for their crimes.
Leviticus19:18 Never seek revenge or bear a grudge against anyone
Leviticus19:19 You must obey all my laws. Do not breed your cattle with other kinds of animals. Do not plant your field with two kinds of seed. Do not wear clothing woven from two different kinds of fabric.

Leviticus19:23 When you enter the land and plant fruit trees
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year the entire crop will be devoted to the LORD as an outburst of praise.
Leviticus19:25 Finally
Leviticus19:26 Never eat meat that has not been drained of its blood. Do not practice fortune-telling or witchcraft.
Leviticus19:27 Do not trim off the hair on your temples or clip the edges of your beards.
Leviticus19:28 Never cut your bodies in mourning for the dead or mark your skin with tattoos
Leviticus19:29 Do not defile your daughter by making her a prostitute
Leviticus19:30 Keep my Sabbath days of rest and show reverence toward my sanctuary
Leviticus19:31 Do not rely on mediums and psychics
Leviticus19:32 Show your fear of God by standing up in the presence of elderly people and showing respect for the aged. I am the LORD.

Leviticus26:32 Yes

Leviticus26:34 Then at last the land will make up for its missed Sabbath years as it lies desolate during your years of exile in the land of your enemies. Then the land will finally rest and enjoy its Sabbaths.

Leviticus26:36 And for those of you who survive
Leviticus26:37 Yes

Leviticus26:41 Finally
Leviticus26:42 Then I will remember my covenant with Jacob
Leviticus26:43 And the land will enjoy its years of Sabbath rest as it lies deserted. At last the people will receive the due punishment for their sins
Leviticus26:44 But despite all this
Leviticus26:45 I will remember my ancient covenant with their ancestors

Leviticus27:1 The LORD said to Moses
Leviticus27:2 Give the following instructions to the Israelites: If you make a special vow to dedicate someone to the LORD by paying the value of that person
Leviticus27:3 here is the scale of values to be used. A man between the ages of twenty and sixty is valued at fifty pieces of silver;
Leviticus27:4 a woman of that age is valued at thirty pieces of silver.
Leviticus27:5 A boy between five and twenty is valued at twenty pieces of silver; a girl of that age is valued at ten pieces of silver.
Leviticus27:6 A boy between the ages of one month and five years is valued at five pieces of silver; a girl of that age is valued at three pieces of silver.
Leviticus27:7 A man older than sixty is valued at fifteen pieces of silver; a woman older than sixty is valued at ten pieces of silver.
Leviticus27:8 If you desire to make such a vow but cannot afford to pay the prescribed amount
Leviticus27:9 If your vow involves giving a clean animal--one that is acceptable as an offering to the LORD--then your gift to the LORD will be considered holy.
Leviticus27:10 The animal should never be exchanged or substituted for another--neither a good animal for a bad one nor a bad animal for a good one. But if such an exchange is in fact made
Leviticus27:11 But if your vow involves an unclean animal--one that is not acceptable as an offering to the LORD--then you must bring the animal to the priest.
Leviticus27:12 He will assess its value
Leviticus27:13 If you want to redeem the animal
Leviticus27:14 If you dedicate a house to the LORD
Leviticus27:15 If you wish to redeem the house
Leviticus27:16 If you dedicate to the LORD a piece of your ancestral property
Leviticus27:17 If the field is dedicated to the LORD in the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:18 But if the field is dedicated after the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:19 If you decide to redeem the dedicated field
Leviticus27:20 But if you decide not to redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 When the field is released in the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:22 If you dedicate to the LORD a field that you have purchased but which is not part of your ancestral property
Leviticus27:23 the priest must assess its value based on the years until the next Year of Jubilee. You must then give the assessed value of the land as a sacred donation to the LORD.
Leviticus27:24 In the Year of Jubilee the field will be released to the original owner from whom you purchased it.
Leviticus27:25 All the value assessments must be measured in terms of the standard sanctuary shekel.
Leviticus27:26 You may not dedicate to the LORD the firstborn of your cattle or sheep because the firstborn of these animals already belong to him.
Leviticus27:27 However
Leviticus27:28 However
Leviticus27:29 A person specially set apart by the LORD for destruction cannot be redeemed. Such a person must be put to death.
Leviticus27:30 A tenth of the produce of the land
Leviticus27:31 If you want to redeem the LORD's tenth of the fruit or grain
Leviticus27:32 The LORD also owns every tenth animal counted off from your herds and flocks. They are set apart to him as holy.
Leviticus27:33 The tenth animal must not be selected on the basis of whether it is good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commands that the LORD gave to the Israelites through Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers1:1 One day in midspring
Numbers1:2 Take a census of the whole community of Israel by their clans and families. List the names of all the men
Numbers1:3 twenty years old or older who are able to go to war. You and Aaron are to direct the project
Numbers1:4 assisted by one family leader from each tribe.

Numbers1:54 So the Israelites did everything just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
