Numbers3:1 This is the family line of Aaron and Moses as it was recorded when the LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai:
Numbers3:2 Aaron's sons were Nadab (the firstborn)
Numbers3:3 They were anointed and set apart to minister as priests.
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu died in the LORD's presence in the wilderness of Sinai when they burned before the LORD a different kind of fire than he had commanded. Since they had no sons
Numbers3:5 Then the LORD said to Moses
Numbers3:6 Call forward the tribe of Levi and present them to Aaron the priest as his assistants.
Numbers3:7 They will serve Aaron and the whole community
Numbers3:8 They will also maintain all the furnishings of the sacred tent
Numbers3:9 Assign the Levites to Aaron and his sons as their assistants.
Numbers3:10 Appoint Aaron and his sons to carry out the duties of the priesthood. Anyone else who comes too near the sanctuary must be executed!
Numbers3:11 And the LORD said to Moses
Numbers3:12 I have chosen the Levites from among the Israelites as substitutes for all the firstborn sons of the people of Israel. The Levites are mine
Numbers3:13 because all the firstborn sons are mine. From the day I killed all the firstborn sons of the Egyptians
Numbers3:14 The LORD spoke again to Moses
Numbers3:15 Take a census of the tribe of Levi by its families and clans. Count every male who is one month old or older.
Numbers3:16 So Moses counted them
Numbers3:17 Levi had three sons
Numbers3:18 The clans descended from Gershon were named for two of his descendants
Numbers3:19 The clans descended from Kohath were named for four of his descendants
Numbers3:20 The clans descended from Merari were named for two of his descendants
Numbers3:21 The descendants of Gershon were composed of the clans descended from Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:22 There were 7
Numbers3:23 They were assigned the area to the west of the Tabernacle for their camp.
Numbers3:24 The leader of the Gershonite clans was Eliasaph son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 These two clans were responsible to care for the tent of the Tabernacle with its layers of coverings
Numbers3:26 the curtains of the courtyard that surrounded the Tabernacle and altar
Numbers3:27 The descendants of Kohath were composed of the clans descended from Amram
Numbers3:28 There were 8
Numbers3:29 They were assigned the area south of the Tabernacle for their camp.
Numbers3:30 The leader of the Kohathite clans was Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 These four clans were responsible for the care of the Ark
Numbers3:32 Eleazar the priest
Numbers3:33 The descendants of Merari were composed of the clans descended from Mahli and Mushi.
Numbers3:34 There were 6
Numbers3:35 They were assigned the area north of the Tabernacle for their camp. The leader of the Merarite clans was Zuriel son of Abihail.
Numbers3:36 These two clans were responsible for the care of the frames supporting the Tabernacle
Numbers3:37 They were also responsible for the posts of the courtyard and all their bases
Numbers3:38 The area in front of the Tabernacle in the east toward the sunrise was reserved for the tents of Moses and of Aaron and his sons
Numbers3:39 So among the Levite clans counted by Moses and Aaron at the LORD's command
Numbers3:40 Then the LORD said to Moses
Numbers3:41 The Levites will be reserved for me as substitutes for the firstborn sons of Israel; I am the LORD. And the Levites' livestock are mine as substitutes for the firstborn livestock of the whole nation of Israel.
Numbers3:42 So Moses counted the firstborn sons of the people of Israel
Numbers3:43 The total number of firstborn sons who were one month old or older was 22
Numbers3:44 Now the LORD said to Moses
Numbers3:45 Take the Levites in place of the firstborn sons of the people of Israel. And take the livestock of the Levites as substitutes for the firstborn livestock of the people of Israel. The Levites will be mine; I am the LORD.
Numbers3:46 To redeem the 273 firstborn sons of Israel who are in excess of the number of Levites
Numbers3:47 collect five pieces of silver for each person
Numbers3:48 Give the silver to Aaron and his sons as the redemption price for the extra firstborn sons.
Numbers3:49 So Moses collected redemption money for the firstborn sons of Israel who exceeded the number of Levites.
Numbers3:50 The silver collected on behalf of these firstborn sons of Israel came to about thirty-four pounds in weight.
Numbers3:51 And Moses gave the redemption money to Aaron and his sons as the LORD had commanded.
Numbers4:1 Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:2 Take a census of the clans and families of the Kohathite division of the Levite tribe.
Numbers4:3 Count all the men between the ages of thirty and fifty who qualify to work in the Tabernacle.
Numbers4:4 The duties of the Kohathites at the Tabernacle will relate to the most sacred objects.
Numbers4:5 When the camp moves
Numbers4:6 Then they must cover the inner curtain with fine goatskin leather
Numbers4:7 Next they must spread a blue cloth over the table
Numbers4:8 They must spread a scarlet cloth over that
Numbers4:9 Next they must cover the lampstand with a dark blue cloth
Numbers4:10 The lampstand with its utensils must then be covered with fine goatskin leather
Numbers4:11 Aaron and his sons must also spread a dark blue cloth over the gold altar and cover this cloth with a covering of fine goatskin leather. Then they are to attach the carrying poles to the altar.
Numbers4:12 All the remaining utensils of the sanctuary must be wrapped in a dark blue cloth
Numbers4:13 The ashes must be removed from the altar
Numbers4:14 All the altar utensils--the firepans
Numbers4:15 When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all the sacred utensils
Numbers4:16 Eleazar son of Aaron the priest will be responsible for the oil of the lampstand
Numbers4:17 Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:18 Don't let the Kohathite clans be destroyed from among the Levites!
Numbers4:19 This is what you must do so they will live and not die when they approach the most sacred objects. Aaron and his sons must always go in with them and assign a specific duty or load to each person.
Numbers4:20 Otherwise they must not approach the sanctuary and look at the sacred objects for even a moment
Numbers4:21 And the LORD said to Moses
Numbers4:22 Take a census of the clans and families of the Gershonite division of the tribe of Levi.
Numbers4:23 Count all the men between the ages of thirty and fifty who are eligible to serve in the Tabernacle.
Numbers4:24 The duties of the Gershonites will be in the areas of general service and carrying loads.
Numbers4:25 They must carry the curtains of the Tabernacle
Numbers4:26 They are also to carry the curtains for the courtyard walls that surround the Tabernacle and altar
Numbers4:27 Aaron and his sons will direct the Gershonites regarding their duties
Numbers4:28 So these are the duties assigned to the Gershonites at the Tabernacle. They will be directly responsible to Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
Numbers4:29 Now take a census of the clans and families of the Merarite division of the Levite tribe.
Numbers4:30 Count all the men between the ages of thirty and fifty who are eligible to serve in the Tabernacle.
Numbers4:31 Their duties at the Tabernacle will consist of carrying loads. They will be required to carry the frames of the Tabernacle
Numbers4:32 the posts for the courtyard walls with their bases
Numbers4:33 So these are the duties of the Merarites at the Tabernacle. They are directly responsible to Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
Numbers4:34 So Moses
Numbers4:35 The count included all the men between thirty and fifty years of age who were eligible for service in the Tabernacle
Numbers4:36 and the total number came to 2
Numbers4:37 So this was the total of all those from the Kohathite clans who were eligible to serve at the Tabernacle. Moses and Aaron counted them
Numbers4:38 The Gershonite division was also counted by its clans and families.
Numbers4:39 The count included all the men between thirty and fifty years of age who were eligible for service in the Tabernacle
Numbers4:40 and the total number came to 2
Numbers4:41 So this was the total of all those from the Gershonite clans who were eligible to serve at the Tabernacle. Moses and Aaron counted them
Numbers4:42 The Merarite division was also counted by its clans and families.
Numbers4:43 The count included all the men between thirty and fifty years of age who were eligible for service in the Tabernacle
Numbers4:44 and the total number came to 3
Numbers4:45 So this was the total of all those from the Merarite clans who were eligible for service. Moses and Aaron counted them
Numbers4:46 So Moses
Numbers4:47 All the men between thirty and fifty years of age who were eligible for service in the Tabernacle and for its transportation
Numbers4:48 numbered 8
Numbers4:49 Each man was assigned his task and told what to carry
Numbers5:1 The LORD gave these instructions to Moses:
Numbers5:2 Command the people of Israel to remove anyone from the camp who has a contagious skin disease or a discharge
Numbers5:3 This applies to men and women alike. Remove them so they will not defile the camp
Numbers5:4 So the Israelites did just as the LORD had commanded Moses and removed such people from the camp.
Numbers5:5 Then the LORD said to Moses
Numbers5:6 Give these instructions to the people of Israel: If any of the people--men or women--betray the LORD by doing wrong to another person
Numbers5:7 They must confess their sin and make full restitution for what they have done
Numbers5:8 But if the person who was wronged is dead
Numbers5:9 All the sacred gifts that the Israelites bring to a priest will belong to him.
Numbers5:10 Each priest may keep the sacred donations that he receives.
Numbers5:11 And the LORD said to Moses
Numbers5:12 Say to the people of Israel: `Suppose a man's wife goes astray and is unfaithful to her husband.
Numbers5:13 Suppose she sleeps with another man
Numbers5:14 If her husband becomes jealous and suspicious of his wife
Numbers5:15 the husband must bring his wife to the priest with an offering of two quarts of barley flour to be presented on her behalf. Do not mix it with olive oil or frankincense
Numbers5:16  `The priest must then present her before the LORD.
Numbers5:17 He must take some holy water in a clay jar and mix it with dust from the Tabernacle floor.
Numbers5:18 When he has presented her before the LORD
Numbers5:19 The priest will put the woman under oath and say to her
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray while under your husband's authority and defiled yourself by sleeping with another man--
Numbers5:21 at this point the priest must put the woman under this oath--then may the people see that the LORD's curse is upon you when he makes you infertile.
Numbers5:22 Now may this water that brings the curse enter your body and make you infertile. And the woman will be required to say
Numbers5:23 Then the priest will write these curses on a piece of leather and wash them off into the bitter water.
Numbers5:24 He will then make the woman drink the bitter water
Numbers5:25  `Then the priest will take the jealousy offering from the woman's hand
Numbers5:26 He will take a handful as a token portion and burn it on the altar. Then he will require the woman to drink the water.
Numbers5:27 If she has defiled herself by being unfaithful to her husband
Numbers5:28 But if she has not defiled herself and is pure
Numbers5:29  `This is the ritual law for dealing with jealousy. If a woman defiles herself by being unfaithful to her husband
Numbers5:30 or if a man is overcome with jealousy and suspicion that his wife has been unfaithful
Numbers5:31 The husband will be innocent of any guilt in this matter
Numbers6:1 Then the LORD said to Moses
Numbers6:2 If some of the people
Numbers6:3 they must give up wine and other alcoholic drinks. They must not use vinegar made from wine
Numbers6:4 As long as they are bound by their Nazirite vow
Numbers6:5 They must never cut their hair throughout the time of their vow
Numbers6:6 And they may not go near a dead body during the entire period of their vow to the LORD
Numbers6:7 even if their own father
Numbers6:8 This applies as long as they are set apart to the LORD.
Numbers6:9 If their hair is defiled because someone suddenly falls dead beside them
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day they must bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance of the Tabernacle.
Numbers6:11 The priest will offer one of the birds for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering. In this way
Numbers6:12 The days of their vow that were completed before their defilement no longer count. They must rededicate themselves to the LORD for the full term of their vow
Numbers6:13 This is the ritual law of the Nazirites. At the conclusion of their time of separation as Nazirites
Numbers6:14 and offer these sacrifices to the LORD: a one-year-old male lamb without defect for a burnt offering
Numbers6:15 a basket of bread made without yeast--cakes of choice flour mixed with olive oil and wafers spread with olive oil--along with their prescribed grain offerings and drink offerings.
Numbers6:16 The priest will present these offerings before the LORD: first the sin offering and the burnt offering;
Numbers6:17 then the ram for a peace offering
Numbers6:18 Then the Nazirites will shave their hair at the entrance of the Tabernacle and put it on the fire beneath the peace-offering sacrifice.
Numbers6:19 After each Nazirite's head has been shaved
Numbers6:20 The priest will then lift the gifts up before the LORD in a gesture of offering. These are holy portions for the priest
Numbers6:21 This is the ritual law of the Nazirites. If any Nazirites have vowed to give the LORD anything else beyond what is required by their normal Nazirite vow
Numbers6:22 Then the LORD said to Moses
Numbers6:23 Instruct Aaron and his sons to bless the people of Israel with this special blessing:
Numbers6:24 `May the LORD bless you and protect you.
Numbers6:25 May the LORD smile on you and be gracious to you.
Numbers6:26 May the LORD show you his favor and give you his peace.'
Numbers6:27 This is how Aaron and his sons will designate the Israelites as my people

Numbers8:1 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers8:2 Tell Aaron that when he sets up the seven lamps in the lampstand
Numbers8:3 So Aaron did this. He set up the seven lamps so they reflected their light forward
Numbers8:4 The entire lampstand
Numbers8:5 Then the LORD said to Moses
Numbers8:6 Now set the Levites apart from the rest of the people of Israel and make them ceremonially clean.
Numbers8:7 Do this by sprinkling them with the water of purification. And have them shave their entire body and wash their clothing. Then they will be ceremonially clean.
Numbers8:8 Have them bring a young bull and a grain offering of choice flour mixed with olive oil
Numbers8:9 Then assemble the whole community of Israel and present the Levites at the entrance of the Tabernacle.
Numbers8:10 When you bring the Levites before the LORD
Numbers8:11 Aaron must present the Levites to the LORD as a special offering from the people of Israel
Numbers8:12 Next the Levites will lay their hands on the heads of the young bulls and present them to the LORD. One will be for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering
Numbers8:13 Then have the Levites stand in front of Aaron and his sons

Numbers8:25 and they must retire at the age of fifty.

Numbers9:1 The LORD gave these instructions to Moses in early spring
Numbers9:2 Tell the Israelites to celebrate the Passover at the proper time
Numbers9:3 at twilight on the appointed day in early spring. Be sure to follow all my laws and regulations concerning this celebration.
Numbers9:4 So Moses told the people to celebrate the Passover
Numbers9:5 in the wilderness of Sinai as twilight fell on the appointed day. And they celebrated the festival there
Numbers9:6 But some of the men had been ceremonially defiled by touching a dead person
Numbers9:7 and said
Numbers9:8 Moses answered
Numbers9:9 This was the LORD's reply:
Numbers9:10 Say to the Israelites: `If any of the people now or in future generations are ceremonially unclean at Passover time because of touching a dead body
Numbers9:11 They must offer the Passover sacrifice one month later
Numbers9:12 They must not leave any of the lamb until the next morning
Numbers9:13  `But those who are ceremonially clean and not away on a trip
Numbers9:14 And if foreigners living among you want to celebrate the Passover to the LORD
Numbers9:15 The Tabernacle was set up
Numbers9:16 This was the regular pattern--at night the cloud changed to the appearance of fire.
Numbers9:17 When the cloud lifted from over the sacred tent
Numbers9:18 In this way
Numbers9:19 If the cloud remained over the Tabernacle for a long time
Numbers9:20 Sometimes the cloud would stay over the Tabernacle for only a few days
Numbers9:21 Sometimes the cloud stayed only overnight and moved on the next morning. But day or night
Numbers9:22 Whether the cloud stayed above the Tabernacle for two days
Numbers9:23 So they camped or traveled at the LORD's command
Numbers10:1 Now the LORD said to Moses
Numbers10:2 Make two trumpets of beaten silver to be used for summoning the people to assemble and for signaling the breaking of camp.
Numbers10:3 When both trumpets are blown
Numbers10:4 But if only one is blown
Numbers10:5 When you sound the signal to move on
Numbers10:6 When you sound the signal a second time
Numbers10:7 But when you call the people to an assembly
Numbers10:8 Only the priests
Numbers10:9 When you arrive in your own land and go to war against your enemies

Numbers15:6 If the sacrifice is a ram
Numbers15:7 and give two and a half pints of wine for a drink offering. This sacrifice will be very pleasing to the LORD.
Numbers15:8 When you present a young bull as a burnt offering or a sacrifice in fulfillment of a special vow or as a peace offering to the LORD
Numbers15:9 then the grain offering accompanying it must include five quarts of choice flour mixed with two quarts of olive oil
Numbers15:10 plus two quarts of wine for the drink offering. This will be an offering made by fire
Numbers15:11 These are the instructions for what is to accompany each sacrificial bull
Numbers15:12 Each of you must do this with each offering you present.
Numbers15:13 If you native Israelites want to present an offering by fire that is pleasing to the LORD
Numbers15:14 And if any foreigners living among you want to present an offering by fire
Numbers15:15 Native Israelites and foreigners are the same before the LORD and are subject to the same laws. This is a permanent law for you.
Numbers15:16 The same instructions and regulations will apply both to you and to the foreigners living among you.
Numbers15:17 The LORD also said to Moses at this time
Numbers15:18 Give the people of Israel the following instructions: When you arrive in the land where I am taking you
Numbers15:19 you will eat from the crops that grow there. But you must set some aside as a gift to the LORD.

Numbers15:21 Throughout the generations to come
Numbers15:22 But suppose some of you unintentionally fail to carry out all these commands that the LORD has given you through Moses.
Numbers15:23 And suppose some of your descendants in the future fail to do everything the LORD has commanded through Moses.
Numbers15:24 If the mistake was done unintentionally
Numbers15:25 With it the priest will make atonement for the whole community of Israel
Numbers15:26 The whole community of Israel will be forgiven
Numbers15:27 If the unintentional sin is committed by an individual
Numbers15:28 The priest will make atonement for the guilty person before the LORD
Numbers15:29 This same law applies both to native Israelites and the foreigners living among you.

Numbers19:1 The LORD said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 Here is another ritual law required by the LORD: Tell the people of Israel to bring you a red heifer that has no physical defects and has never been yoked to a plow.
Numbers19:3 Give it to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 Eleazar will take some of its blood on his finger and sprinkle it seven times toward the front of the Tabernacle.
Numbers19:5 As Eleazar watches
Numbers19:6 Eleazar the priest must then take cedarwood
Numbers19:7 Then the priest must wash his clothes and bathe himself in water. Afterward he may return to the camp
Numbers19:8 The man who burns the animal must also wash his clothes and bathe in water
Numbers19:9 Then someone who is ceremonially clean will gather up the ashes of the heifer and place them in a purified place outside the camp. They will be kept there for the people of Israel to use in the water for the purification ceremony. This ceremony is performed for the removal of sin.
Numbers19:10 The man who gathers up the ashes of the heifer must also wash his clothes
Numbers19:11 All those who touch a dead human body will be ceremonially unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 They must purify themselves on the third and seventh days with the water of purification; then they will be purified. But if they do not do this on the third and seventh days
Numbers19:13 All those who touch a dead body and do not purify themselves in the proper way defile the LORD's Tabernacle and will be cut off from the community of Israel. Since the water of purification was not sprinkled on them
Numbers19:14 This is the ritual law that applies when someone dies in a tent: Those who enter that tent
Numbers19:15 Any container in the tent that was not covered with a lid is also defiled.
Numbers19:16 And if someone outdoors touches the corpse of someone who was killed with a sword or who died a natural death
Numbers19:17 To remove the defilement
Numbers19:18 Then someone who is ceremonially clean must take a hyssop branch and dip it into the water. That person must sprinkle the water on the tent
Numbers19:19 On the third and seventh days the ceremonially clean person must sprinkle the water on those who are unclean. Then on the seventh day the people being cleansed must wash their clothes and bathe themselves
Numbers19:20 But those who become defiled and do not purify themselves will be cut off from the community
Numbers19:21 This is a permanent law. Those who sprinkle the water of purification must afterward wash their clothes
Numbers19:22 Anything and anyone that a defiled person touches will be ceremonially defiled until evening.

Numbers30:1 Now Moses summoned the leaders of the tribes of Israel and told them
Numbers30:2 A man who makes a vow to the LORD or makes a pledge under oath must never break it. He must do exactly what he said he would do.
Numbers30:3 If a young woman makes a vow to the LORD or a pledge under oath while she is still living at her father's home
Numbers30:4 and her father hears of the vow or pledge but says nothing
Numbers30:5 But if her father refuses to let her fulfill the vow or pledge on the day he hears of it
Numbers30:6 Now suppose a young woman takes a vow or makes an impulsive pledge and later marries.
Numbers30:7 If her husband learns of her vow or pledge and raises no objections on the day he hears of it
Numbers30:8 But if her husband refuses to accept her vow or impulsive pledge on the day he hears of it
Numbers30:9 If
Numbers30:10 Suppose a woman is married and living in her husband's home when she makes a vow or pledge.
Numbers30:11 If her husband hears of it and does nothing to stop her
Numbers30:12 But if her husband refuses to accept it on the day he hears of it
Numbers30:13 So her husband may either confirm or nullify any vows or pledges she makes to deny herself.
Numbers30:14 But if he says nothing on the day he hears of it
Numbers30:15 If he waits more than a day and then tries to nullify a vow or pledge
Numbers30:16 These are the regulations the LORD gave Moses concerning relationships between a man and his wife

Numbers33:1 This is the itinerary the Israelites followed as they marched out of Egypt under the leadership of Moses and Aaron.

Numbers33:5 After leaving Rameses

Numbers33:7 They left Etham and turned back toward Pi-hahiroth
Numbers33:8 They left Pi-hahiroth and crossed the Red Sea into the wilderness beyond. Then they traveled for three days into the Etham wilderness and camped at Marah.
Numbers33:9 They left Marah and camped at Elim
Numbers33:10 They left Elim and camped beside the Red Sea.
Numbers33:11 They left the Red Sea and camped in the Sin Desert.

Numbers33:13 They left Dophkah and camped at Alush.
Numbers33:14 They left Alush and camped at Rephidim
Numbers33:15 They left Rephidim and camped in the wilderness of Sinai.
Numbers33:16 They left the wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth-hattaavah.
Numbers33:17 They left Kibroth-hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth.
Numbers33:18 They left Hazeroth and camped at Rithmah.
Numbers33:19 They left Rithmah and camped at Rimmon-perez.

Numbers33:21 They left Libnah and camped at Rissah.

Numbers33:23 They left Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shepher.

Numbers33:25 They left Haradah and camped at Makheloth.
Numbers33:26 They left Makheloth and camped at Tahath.
Numbers33:27 They left Tahath and camped at Terah.

Deuteronomy8:14 that is the time to be careful. Do not become proud at that time and forget the LORD your God
Deuteronomy8:15 Do not forget that he led you through the great and terrifying wilderness with poisonous snakes and scorpions
Deuteronomy8:16 He fed you with manna in the wilderness
Deuteronomy8:17 He did it so you would never think that it was your own strength and energy that made you wealthy.
Deuteronomy8:18 Always remember that it is the LORD your God who gives you power to become rich

Deuteronomy9:5 It is not at all because you are such righteous

Deuteronomy9:17 So I raised the stone tablets and dashed them to the ground. I smashed them before your very eyes.
Deuteronomy9:18 Then for forty days and nights I lay prostrate before the LORD
Deuteronomy9:19 How I feared for you
Deuteronomy9:20 The LORD was so angry with Aaron that he wanted to destroy him. But I prayed for Aaron
Deuteronomy9:21 I took your sin--the calf you had made--and I melted it in the fire and ground it into fine dust. I threw the dust into the stream that cascades down the mountain.
Deuteronomy9:22 You also made the LORD angry at Taberah
Deuteronomy9:23 And at Kadesh-barnea the LORD sent you out with this command: `Go up and take the land I have given you.' But you rebelled against the command of the LORD your God and refused to trust him or obey him.

Deuteronomy28:1 If you fully obey the LORD your God by keeping all the commands I am giving you today
Deuteronomy28:2 You will experience all these blessings if you obey the LORD your God:
Deuteronomy28:3 You will be blessed in your towns and in the country.
Deuteronomy28:4 You will be blessed with many children and productive fields. You will be blessed with fertile herds and flocks.
Deuteronomy28:5 You will be blessed with baskets overflowing with fruit
Deuteronomy28:6 You will be blessed wherever you go
Deuteronomy28:7 The LORD will conquer your enemies when they attack you. They will attack you from one direction
Deuteronomy28:8 The LORD will bless everything you do and will fill your storehouses with grain. The LORD your God will bless you in the land he is giving you.
Deuteronomy28:9 If you obey the commands of the LORD your God and walk in his ways
Deuteronomy28:10 Then all the nations of the world will see that you are a people claimed by the LORD
Deuteronomy28:11 The LORD will give you an abundance of good things in the land he swore to give your ancestors--many children
Deuteronomy28:12 The LORD will send rain at the proper time from his rich treasury in the heavens to bless all the work you do. You will lend to many nations
Deuteronomy28:13 If you listen to these commands of the LORD your God and carefully obey them
Deuteronomy28:14 You must not turn away from any of the commands I am giving you today to follow after other gods and worship them.
Deuteronomy28:15 But if you refuse to listen to the LORD your God and do not obey all the commands and laws I am giving you today
Deuteronomy28:16 You will be cursed in your towns and in the country.
Deuteronomy28:17 You will be cursed with baskets empty of fruit
Deuteronomy28:18 You will be cursed with few children and barren fields. You will be cursed with infertile herds and flocks.
Deuteronomy28:19 You will be cursed wherever you go
Deuteronomy28:20 The LORD himself will send against you curses
Deuteronomy28:21 The LORD will send diseases among you until none of you are left in the land you are about to enter and occupy.
Deuteronomy28:22 The LORD will strike you with wasting disease
Deuteronomy28:23 The skies above will be as unyielding as bronze
Deuteronomy28:24 The LORD will turn your rain into sand and dust
Deuteronomy28:25 The LORD will cause you to be defeated by your enemies. You will attack your enemies from one direction
Deuteronomy28:26 Your dead bodies will be food for the birds and wild animals
Deuteronomy28:27 The LORD will afflict you with the boils of Egypt and with tumors
Deuteronomy28:28 The LORD will strike you with madness
Deuteronomy28:29 You will grope around in broad daylight
Deuteronomy28:30 You will be engaged to a woman
Deuteronomy28:31 Your ox will be butchered before your eyes
Deuteronomy28:32 You will watch as your sons and daughters are taken away as slaves. Your heart will break as you long for them
Deuteronomy28:33 A foreign nation you have never heard about will eat the crops you worked so hard to grow. You will suffer under constant oppression and harsh treatment.
Deuteronomy28:34 You will go mad because of all the tragedy around you.
Deuteronomy28:35 The LORD will cover you from head to foot with incurable boils.
Deuteronomy28:36 The LORD will exile you and the king you crowned to a nation unknown to you and your ancestors. Then in exile you will worship gods of wood and stone!
Deuteronomy28:37 You will become an object of horror
Deuteronomy28:38 You will plant much but harvest little
Deuteronomy28:39 You will plant vineyards and care for them
Deuteronomy28:40 You will grow olive trees throughout your land
Deuteronomy28:41 You will have sons and daughters
Deuteronomy28:42 Swarms of insects will destroy your trees and crops.
Deuteronomy28:43 The foreigners living among you will become stronger and stronger
Deuteronomy28:44 They will lend money to you
Deuteronomy28:45 If you refuse to listen to the LORD your God and to obey the commands and laws he has given you
Deuteronomy28:46 These horrors will serve as a sign and warning among you and your descendants forever.
Deuteronomy28:47 Because you have not served the LORD your God with joy and enthusiasm for the abundant benefits you have received
Deuteronomy28:48 you will serve your enemies whom the LORD will send against you. You will be left hungry
Deuteronomy28:49 The LORD will bring a distant nation against you from the end of the earth
Deuteronomy28:50 a fierce and heartless nation that shows no respect for the old and no pity for the young.
Deuteronomy28:51 Its armies will devour your livestock and crops
Deuteronomy28:52 They will lay siege to your cities until all the fortified walls in your land--the walls you trusted to protect you--are knocked down. They will attack all the towns in the land the LORD your God has given you.
Deuteronomy28:53 The siege will be so severe that you will eat the flesh of your own sons and daughters
Deuteronomy28:54 The most tenderhearted man among you will have no compassion for his own brother
Deuteronomy28:55 He will refuse to give them a share of the flesh he is devouring--the flesh of one of his own children--because he has nothing else to eat during the siege that your enemy will inflict on all your towns.
Deuteronomy28:56 The most tender and delicate woman among you--so delicate she would not so much as touch her feet to the ground--will be cruel to the husband she loves and to her own son or daughter.
Deuteronomy28:57 She will hide from them the afterbirth and the new baby she has borne
Deuteronomy28:58 If you refuse to obey all the terms of this law that are written in this book
Deuteronomy28:59 then the LORD will overwhelm both you and your children with indescribable plagues. These plagues will be intense and without relief
Deuteronomy28:60 He will bring against you all the diseases of Egypt that you feared so much
Deuteronomy28:61 The LORD will bring against you every sickness and plague there is
Deuteronomy28:62 Though you are as numerous as the stars in the sky

2Samuel22:1 David sang this song to the LORD after the LORD had rescued him from all his enemies and from Saul.
2Samuel22:2 These are the words he sang: The LORD is my rock
2Samuel22:3 my God is my rock
2Samuel22:4 I will call on the LORD
2Samuel22:5 The waves of death surrounded me; the floods of destruction swept over me.
2Samuel22:6 The grave wrapped its ropes around me; death itself stared me in the face.
2Samuel22:7 But in my distress I cried out to the LORD; yes
2Samuel22:8 Then the earth quaked and trembled; the foundations of the heavens shook; they quaked because of his anger.
2Samuel22:9 Smoke poured from his nostrils; fierce flames leaped from his mouth; glowing coals flamed forth from him.
2Samuel22:10 He opened the heavens and came down; dark storm clouds were beneath his feet.
2Samuel22:11 Mounted on a mighty angel
2Samuel22:12 He shrouded himself in darkness
2Samuel22:13 A great brightness shone before him
2Samuel22:14 The LORD thundered from heaven; the Most High gave a mighty shout.
2Samuel22:15 He shot his arrows and scattered his enemies; his lightning flashed
2Samuel22:16 Then at the command of the LORD
2Samuel22:17 He reached down from heaven and rescued me; he drew me out of deep waters.
2Samuel22:18 He delivered me from my powerful enemies
2Samuel22:19 They attacked me at a moment when I was weakest
2Samuel22:20 He led me to a place of safety; he rescued me because he delights in me.
2Samuel22:21 The LORD rewarded me for doing right; he compensated me because of my innocence.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the LORD; I have not turned from my God to follow evil.
2Samuel22:23 For all his laws are constantly before me; I have never abandoned his principles.
2Samuel22:24 I am blameless before God; I have kept myself from sin.
2Samuel22:25 The LORD rewarded me for doing right
2Samuel22:26 To the faithful you show yourself faithful; to those with integrity you show integrity.
2Samuel22:27 To the pure you show yourself pure
2Samuel22:28 You rescue those who are humble
2Samuel22:29 O LORD
2Samuel22:30 In your strength I can crush an army; with my God I can scale any wall.
2Samuel22:31 As for God
2Samuel22:32 For who is God except the LORD? Who but our God is a solid rock?
2Samuel22:33 God is my strong fortress; he has made my way safe.
2Samuel22:34 He makes me as surefooted as a deer
2Samuel22:35 He prepares me for battle; he strengthens me to draw a bow of bronze.
2Samuel22:36 You have given me the shield of your salvation; your help has made me great.
2Samuel22:37 You have made a wide path for my feet to keep them from slipping.
2Samuel22:38 I chased my enemies and destroyed them; I did not stop until they were conquered.
2Samuel22:39 I consumed them; I struck them down so they could not get up; they fell beneath my feet.
2Samuel22:40 You have armed me with strength for the battle; you have subdued my enemies under my feet.
2Samuel22:41 You made them turn and run; I have destroyed all who hated me.
2Samuel22:42 They called for help
2Samuel22:43 I ground them as fine as the dust of the earth; I swept them into the gutter like dirt.
2Samuel22:44 You gave me victory over my accusers. You preserved me as the ruler over nations; people I don't even know now serve me.
2Samuel22:45 Foreigners cringe before me; as soon as they hear of me
2Samuel22:46 They all lose their courage and come trembling from their strongholds.
2Samuel22:47 The LORD lives! Blessed be my rock! May God
2Samuel22:48 He is the God who pays back those who harm me; he subdues the nations under me
2Samuel22:49 and rescues me from my enemies. You hold me safe beyond the reach of my enemies; you save me from violent opponents.
2Samuel22:50 For this
2Samuel22:51 You give great victories to your king; you show unfailing love to your anointed

1Kings6:33 Then he made four-sided doorposts of olive wood for the entrance to the Temple.
1Kings6:34 There were two folding doors of cypress wood
1Kings6:35 These doors were decorated with carvings of cherubim
1Kings6:36 The walls of the inner courtyard were built so that there was one layer of cedar beams after every three layers of hewn stone.
1Kings6:37 The foundation of the LORD's Temple was laid in midspring of the fourth year of Solomon's reign.
1Kings6:38 The entire building was completed in every detail by midautumn of the eleventh year of his reign. So it took seven years to build the Temple.
1Kings7:1 Solomon also built a palace for himself
1Kings7:2 One of Solomon's buildings was called the Palace of the Forest of Lebanon. It was 150 feet long
1Kings7:3 It had a cedar roof supported by forty-five rafters that rested on three rows of pillars
1Kings7:4 On each of the side walls there were three rows of windows facing each other.
1Kings7:5 All the doorways were rectangular in frame; they were in sets of three
1Kings7:6 He also built the Hall of Pillars
1Kings7:7 There was also the Hall of the Throne
1Kings7:8 Solomon's living quarters surrounded a courtyard behind this hall; they were built the same way. He also built similar living quarters for Pharaoh's daughter
1Kings7:9 All these buildings were built entirely from huge
1Kings7:10 Some of the huge foundation stones were 15 feet long
1Kings7:11 The costly blocks of stone used in the walls were also cut to measure
1Kings7:12 The walls of the great courtyard were built so that there was one layer of cedar beams after every three layers of hewn stone
1Kings7:13 King Solomon then asked for a man named Huram to come from Tyre
1Kings7:14 for he was a craftsman skilled in bronze work. He was half Israelite
1Kings7:15 Huram cast two bronze pillars
1Kings7:16 For the tops of the pillars he made capitals of molded bronze
1Kings7:17 Each capital was decorated with seven sets of latticework and interwoven chains.
1Kings7:18 He also made two rows of pomegranates that encircled the latticework to decorate the capitals over the pillars.
1Kings7:19 The capitals on the columns inside the foyer were shaped like lilies
1Kings7:20 Each capital on the two pillars had two hundred pomegranates in two rows around them
1Kings7:21 Huram set the pillars at the entrance of the Temple
1Kings7:22 The capitals on the pillars were shaped like lilies. And so the work on the pillars was finished.
1Kings7:23 Then Huram cast a large round tank
1Kings7:24 The Sea was encircled just below its rim by two rows of decorative gourds. There were about six gourds per foot all the way around
1Kings7:25 The Sea rested on a base of twelve bronze oxen
1Kings7:26 The walls of the Sea were about three inches thick
1Kings7:27 Huram also made ten bronze water carts
1Kings7:28 They were constructed with side panels braced with crossbars.
1Kings7:29 Both the panels and the crossbars were decorated with carved lions
1Kings7:30 Each of these carts had four bronze wheels and bronze axles. At each corner of the carts were supporting posts for the bronze basins; these supports were decorated with carvings of wreaths on each side.
1Kings7:31 The top of each cart had a circular frame for the basin. It projected 1 1/2 feet above the cart's top like a round pedestal
1Kings7:32 Under the panels were four wheels that were connected to axles that had been cast as one unit with the cart. The wheels were 2 1/4 feet in diameter
1Kings7:33 and were similar to chariot wheels. The axles
1Kings7:34 There were supports at each of the four corners of the carts
1Kings7:35 Around the top of each cart there was a rim 9 inches wide. The supports and side panels were cast as one unit with the cart.
1Kings7:36 Carvings of cherubim
1Kings7:37 All ten water carts were the same size and were made alike
1Kings7:38 Huram also made ten bronze basins
1Kings7:39 He arranged five water carts on the south side of the Temple and five on the north side. The Sea was placed at the southeast corner of the Temple.
1Kings7:40 He also made the necessary pots
1Kings7:41 two pillars
1Kings7:42 four hundred pomegranates that hung from the chains on the capitals (two rows of pomegranates for each of the chain networks that were hung around the capitals on top of the pillars)
1Kings7:43 the ten water carts holding the ten basins
1Kings7:44 the Sea and the twelve oxen under it
1Kings7:45 the pots
1Kings7:46 The king had them cast in clay molds in the Jordan Valley between Succoth and Zarethan.
1Kings7:47 Solomon did not weigh all the utensils because there were so many; the weight of the bronze could not be measured.

1Kings7:50 the cups
1Kings7:51 So King Solomon finished all his work on the Temple of the LORD. Then Solomon brought all the gifts his father

1Kings8:7 The cherubim spread their wings over the Ark
1Kings8:8 These poles were so long that their ends could be seen from the front entrance of the Temple's main room--the Holy Place--but not from outside it. They are still there to this day.

1Kings8:33 If your people Israel are defeated by their enemies because they have sinned against you
1Kings8:34 then hear from heaven and forgive their sins and return them to this land you gave their ancestors.

1Kings8:38 and if your people offer a prayer concerning their troubles or sorrow
1Kings8:39 then hear from heaven where you live
1Kings8:40 Then they will fear you and walk in your ways as long as they live in the land you gave to our ancestors.
1Kings8:41 And when foreigners hear of you and come from distant lands to worship your great name--
1Kings8:42 for they will hear of you and of your mighty miracles and your power--and when they pray toward this Temple
1Kings8:43 then hear from heaven where you live
1Kings8:44 If your people go out at your command to fight their enemies
1Kings8:45 then hear their prayers from heaven and uphold their cause.
1Kings8:46 If they sin against you--and who has never sinned?--you may become angry with them and let their enemies conquer them and take them captive to a foreign land far or near.
1Kings8:47 But in that land of exile
1Kings8:48 Then if they turn to you with their whole heart and soul and pray toward the land you gave to their ancestors
1Kings8:49 then hear their prayers from heaven where you live. Uphold their cause
1Kings8:50 and forgive your people who have sinned against you. Make their captors merciful to them
1Kings8:51 for they are your people--your special possession--whom you brought out of the iron-smelting furnace of Egypt.
1Kings8:52 May your eyes be open to my requests and to the requests of your people Israel. Hear and answer them whenever they cry out to you.
1Kings8:53 For when you brought our ancestors out of Egypt

1Kings8:58 May he give us the desire to do his will in everything and to obey all the commands
1Kings8:59 And may these words that I have prayed in the presence of the LORD be before him constantly
1Kings8:60 May people all over the earth know that the LORD is God and that there is no other god.
1Kings8:61 And may you

A large crowd had gathered from as far away as Lebo-hamath in the north to the brook of Egypt in the south. 2Kings17:6 Finally
2Kings17:7 This disaster came upon the nation of Israel because the people worshiped other gods
2Kings17:8 They had imitated the practices of the pagan nations the LORD had driven from the land before them
2Kings17:9 The people of Israel had also secretly done many things that were not pleasing to the LORD their God. They built pagan shrines for themselves in all their towns
2Kings17:10 They set up sacred pillars and Asherah poles at the top of every hill and under every green tree.
2Kings17:11 They burned incense at the shrines
2Kings17:12 Yes
2Kings17:13 Again and again the LORD had sent his prophets and seers to warn both Israel and Judah: Turn from all your evil ways. Obey my commands and laws
2Kings17:14 But the Israelites would not listen. They were as stubborn as their ancestors and refused to believe in the LORD their God.
2Kings17:15 They rejected his laws and the covenant he had made with their ancestors
2Kings17:16 They defied all the commands of the LORD their God and made two calves from metal. They set up an Asherah pole and worshiped Baal and all the forces of heaven.
2Kings17:17 They even sacrificed their own sons and daughters in the fire. They consulted fortune-tellers and used sorcery and sold themselves to evil
2Kings17:18 And because the LORD was angry
2Kings17:19 But even the people of Judah refused to obey the commands of the LORD their God. They walked down the same evil paths that Israel had established.
2Kings17:20 So the LORD rejected all the descendants of Israel. He punished them by handing them over to their attackers until they were destroyed.
2Kings17:21 For when the LORD tore Israel away from the kingdom of David
2Kings17:22 And the people of Israel persisted in all the evil ways of Jeroboam. They did not turn from these sins of idolatry
2Kings17:23 until the LORD finally swept them away
2Kings17:24 And the king of Assyria transported groups of people from Babylon
2Kings17:25 But since these foreign settlers did not worship the LORD when they first arrived
2Kings17:26 So a message was sent to the king of Assyria: The people whom you have resettled in the towns of Israel do not know how to worship the God of the land. He has sent lions among them to destroy them because they have not worshiped him correctly.
2Kings17:27 The king of Assyria then commanded
2Kings17:28 So one of the priests who had been exiled from Samaria returned to Bethel and taught the new residents how to worship the LORD.
2Kings17:29 But these various groups of foreigners also continued to worship their own gods. In town after town where they lived
2Kings17:30 Those from Babylon worshiped idols of their god Succoth-benoth. Those from Cuthah worshiped their god Nergal. And those from Hamath worshiped Ashima.
2Kings17:31 The Avvites worshiped their gods Nibhaz and Tartak. And the people from Sepharvaim even burned their own children as sacrifices to Adrammelech and Anammelech.
2Kings17:32 These new residents worshiped the LORD
2Kings17:33 And though they worshiped the LORD
2Kings17:34 And this is still going on among them today. They follow their former practices instead of truly worshiping the LORD and obeying the laws
2Kings17:35 For the LORD had made a covenant with the descendants of Jacob and commanded them: Do not worship any other gods or bow before them or serve them or offer sacrifices to them.
2Kings17:36 Worship only the LORD
2Kings17:37 Be careful to obey all the laws
2Kings17:38 Do not forget the covenant I made with you
2Kings17:39 You must worship only the LORD your God. He is the one who will rescue you from all your enemies.
2Kings17:40 But the people would not listen and continued to follow their old ways.
2Kings17:41 So while these new residents worshiped the LORD

1Chronicles10:4 Saul groaned to his armor bearer

1Chronicles10:6 So Saul and his three sons died there together
1Chronicles10:7 When the Israelites in the Jezreel Valley saw that their army had been routed and that Saul and his sons were dead
1Chronicles10:8 The next day when the Philistines went out to strip the dead
1Chronicles10:9 So they stripped off Saul's armor and cut off his head. Then they proclaimed the news of Saul's death before their idols and to the people throughout the land of Philistia.
1Chronicles10:10 They placed his armor in the temple of their gods
1Chronicles10:11 But when the people of Jabesh-gilead heard what the Philistines had done to Saul
1Chronicles10:12 their warriors went out and brought the bodies of Saul and his three sons back to Jabesh. Then they buried their remains beneath the oak tree at Jabesh
1Chronicles10:13 So Saul died because he was unfaithful to the LORD. He failed to obey the LORD's command
1Chronicles10:14 instead of asking the LORD for guidance. So the LORD killed him and turned his kingdom over to David son of Jesse.

1Chronicles26:3 Elam (the fifth)
1Chronicles26:4 The sons of Obed-edom
1Chronicles26:5 Ammiel (the sixth)
1Chronicles26:6 Obed-edom's son Shemaiah had sons with great ability who earned positions of great authority in the clan.
1Chronicles26:7 Their names were Othni
1Chronicles26:8 All of these descendants of Obed-edom
1Chronicles26:9 Meshelemiah's eighteen sons and relatives were also very capable men.
1Chronicles26:10 Hosah
1Chronicles26:11 His other sons included Hilkiah (the second)
1Chronicles26:12 These divisions of the gatekeepers were named for their family leaders
1Chronicles26:13 They were assigned by families for guard duty at the various gates
1Chronicles26:14 The responsibility for the east gate went to Meshelemiah and his group. The north gate was assigned to his son Zechariah
1Chronicles26:15 The south gate went to Obed-edom
1Chronicles26:16 Shuppim and Hosah were assigned the west gate and the gateway leading up to the Temple. Guard duties were divided evenly.
1Chronicles26:17 Six Levites were assigned each day to the east gate
1Chronicles26:18 Six were assigned each day to the west gate
1Chronicles26:19 These were the divisions of the gatekeepers from the clans of Korah and Merari.

1Chronicles26:24 From the clan of Amram
1Chronicles26:25 His relatives through Eliezer were Rehabiah
1Chronicles26:26 Shelomoth and his relatives were in charge of the treasuries that held all the things dedicated to the LORD by King David
1Chronicles26:27 These men had dedicated some of the plunder they had gained in battle to maintain the house of the LORD.
1Chronicles26:28 Shelomoth and his relatives also cared for the items dedicated to the LORD by Samuel the seer
1Chronicles26:29 From the clan of Izhar came Kenaniah. He and his sons were appointed to serve as public administrators and judges throughout Israel.
1Chronicles26:30 From the clan of Hebron came Hashabiah. He and his relatives--seventeen hundred capable men--were put in charge of the Israelite lands west of the Jordan River. They were responsible for all matters related to the things of the LORD and the service of the king in that area.
1Chronicles26:31 Also from the clan of Hebron came Jeriah
1Chronicles26:32 There were twenty-seven hundred capable men among the relatives of Jeriah. King David sent them to the east side of the Jordan River and put them in charge of the tribes of Reuben and Gad and the half-tribe of Manasseh. They were responsible for all matters related to the things of God and the service of the king.

1Chronicles27:23 When David took his census
1Chronicles27:24 Joab began the census but never finished it because the anger of God broke out against Israel. The final total was never recorded in King David's official records.

1Chronicles27:30 Obil the Ishmaelite was in charge of the camels. Jehdeiah from Meronoth was in charge of the donkeys.

1Chronicles27:32 Jonathan
1Chronicles27:33 Ahithophel was the royal adviser. Hushai the Arkite was the king's friend.
1Chronicles27:34 Ahithophel was succeeded by Jehoiada son of Benaiah and by Abiathar. Joab was commander of the Israelite army.

2Chronicles2:3 Solomon also sent this message to King Hiram at Tyre: Send me cedar logs like the ones that were supplied to my father
2Chronicles2:4 I am about to build a Temple to honor the name of the LORD my God. It will be a place set apart to burn incense and sweet spices before him
2Chronicles2:5 This will be a magnificent Temple because our God is an awesome God
2Chronicles2:6 But who can really build him a worthy home? Not even the highest heavens can contain him! So who am I to consider building a Temple for him
2Chronicles2:7 So send me a master craftsman who can work with gold
2Chronicles2:8 Also send me cedar
2Chronicles2:9 An immense amount of timber will be needed
2Chronicles2:10 I will pay your men 100
2Chronicles2:11 King Hiram sent this letter of reply to Solomon: It is because the LORD loves his people that he has made you their king!
2Chronicles2:12 Blessed be the LORD
2Chronicles2:13 I am sending you a master craftsman named Huram-abi. He is a brilliant man
2Chronicles2:14 the son of a woman from Dan in Israel; his father is from Tyre. He is skillful at making things from gold
2Chronicles2:15 Send along the wheat
2Chronicles2:16 We will cut whatever timber you need from the Lebanon mountains and will float the logs in rafts down the coast of the Mediterranean Sea to Joppa. From there you can transport the logs up to Jerusalem.

2Chronicles3:1 So Solomon began to build the Temple of the LORD in Jerusalem on Mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 The construction began in midspring
2Chronicles3:3 The foundation for the Temple of God was ninety feet long and thirty feet wide.
2Chronicles3:4 The foyer at the front of the Temple was thirty feet wide
2Chronicles3:5 The main room of the Temple was paneled with cypress wood
2Chronicles3:6 The walls of the Temple were decorated with beautiful jewels and with pure gold from the land of Parvaim.
2Chronicles3:7 All the walls
2Chronicles3:8 The Most Holy Place was thirty feet wide
2Chronicles3:9 They used gold nails that weighed about twenty ounces each. The walls of the upper rooms were also overlaid with pure gold.
2Chronicles3:10 Solomon made two figures shaped like cherubim and overlaid them with gold. These were placed in the Most Holy Place.
2Chronicles3:11 The total wingspan of the two cherubim standing side by side was 30 feet. One wing of the first figure was 7 1/2 feet long
2Chronicles3:12 In the same way
2Chronicles3:13 So the wingspan of both cherubim together was 30 feet. They both stood and faced out toward the main room of the Temple.
2Chronicles3:14 Across the entrance of the Most Holy Place
2Chronicles3:15 For the front of the Temple
2Chronicles3:16 He made a network of interwoven chains and used them to decorate the tops of the pillars. He also made one hundred decorative pomegranates and attached them to the chains.
2Chronicles3:17 Then he set up the two pillars at the entrance of the Temple
2Chronicles4:1 Solomon also made a bronze altar 30 feet long
2Chronicles4:2 Then he cast a large round tank
2Chronicles4:3 The Sea was encircled just below its rim by two rows of figures that resembled oxen. There were about six oxen per foot all the way around
2Chronicles4:4 The Sea rested on a base of twelve bronze oxen
2Chronicles4:5 The walls of the Sea were about three inches thick
2Chronicles4:6 He also made ten basins for water to wash the offerings
2Chronicles4:7 Solomon then cast ten gold lampstands according to the specifications that had been given and put them in the Temple. Five were placed against the south wall
2Chronicles4:8 He also built ten tables and placed them in the Temple
2Chronicles4:9 Solomon also built a courtyard for the priests and the large outer courtyard. He made doors for the courtyard entrances and overlaid them with bronze.
2Chronicles4:10 The Sea was placed near the southeast corner of the Temple.
2Chronicles4:11 Huram-abi also made the necessary pots
2Chronicles4:12 two pillars
2Chronicles4:13 four hundred pomegranates that hung from the chains on the capitals (two rows of pomegranates for each of the chain networks that were hung around the capitals on top of the pillars)
2Chronicles4:14 the water carts holding the basins
2Chronicles4:15 the Sea and the twelve oxen under it
2Chronicles4:16 the pots
2Chronicles4:17 The king had them cast in clay molds in the Jordan Valley between Succoth and Zarethan.
2Chronicles4:18 Such great quantities of bronze were used that its weight could not be determined.
2Chronicles4:19 So Solomon made all the furnishings for the Temple of God: the gold altar; the tables for the Bread of the Presence;
2Chronicles4:20 the lampstands and their lamps of pure gold to burn in front of the Most Holy Place as prescribed;
2Chronicles4:21 the flower decorations

2Chronicles5:3 They all assembled before the king at the annual Festival of Shelters in early autumn.

2Chronicles5:5 along with the special tent and all its sacred utensils. The Levitical priests carried them all up to the Temple.

2Chronicles5:7 Then the priests carried the Ark of the LORD's covenant into the inner sanctuary of the Temple--the Most Holy Place--and placed it beneath the wings of the cherubim.
2Chronicles5:8 The cherubim spread their wings out over the Ark
2Chronicles5:9 These poles were so long that their ends could be seen from the front entrance of the Temple's main room--the Holy Place--but not from outside it. They are still there to this day.
2Chronicles5:10 Nothing was in the Ark except the two stone tablets that Moses had placed there at Mount Sinai
2Chronicles5:11 Then the priests left the Holy Place. All the priests who were present had purified themselves
2Chronicles5:12 And the Levites who were musicians--Asaph
2Chronicles5:13 The trumpeters and singers performed together in unison to praise and give thanks to the LORD. Accompanied by trumpets
2Chronicles5:14 The priests could not continue their work because the glorious presence of the LORD filled the Temple of God.

2Chronicles6:21 May you hear the humble and earnest requests from me and your people Israel when we pray toward this place. Yes
2Chronicles6:22 If someone wrongs another person and is required to take an oath of innocence in front of the altar at this Temple
2Chronicles6:23 then hear from heaven and judge between your servants--the accuser and the accused. Punish the guilty party
2Chronicles6:24 If your people Israel are defeated by their enemies because they have sinned against you
2Chronicles6:25 then hear from heaven and forgive their sins and return them to this land you gave their ancestors.
2Chronicles6:26 If the skies are shut up and there is no rain because your people have sinned against you
2Chronicles6:27 then hear from heaven and forgive the sins of your servants
2Chronicles6:28 If there is a famine in the land
2Chronicles6:29 and if your people offer a prayer concerning their troubles or sorrow
2Chronicles6:30 then hear from heaven where you live
2Chronicles6:31 Then they will fear you and walk in your ways as long as they live in the land you gave to our ancestors.
2Chronicles6:32 And when foreigners hear of you and your mighty miracles
2Chronicles6:33 then hear from heaven where you live
2Chronicles6:34 If your people go out at your command to fight their enemies
2Chronicles6:35 then hear their prayers from heaven and uphold their cause.
2Chronicles6:36 If they sin against you--and who has never sinned?--you may become angry with them and let their enemies conquer them and take them captive to a foreign land far or near.
2Chronicles6:37 But in that land of exile
2Chronicles6:38 Then if they turn to you with their whole heart and soul and pray toward the land you gave to their ancestors
2Chronicles6:39 then hear their prayers from heaven where you live. Uphold their cause and forgive your people who have sinned against you.

2Chronicles6:41 And now
2Chronicles6:42 O LORD God

2Chronicles8:1 It was now twenty years since Solomon had become king
2Chronicles8:2 Solomon now turned his attention to rebuilding the towns that King Hiram had given him
2Chronicles8:3 It was at this time
2Chronicles8:4 He rebuilt Tadmor in the desert and built towns in the region of Hamath as supply centers.
2Chronicles8:5 He fortified the cities of Upper Beth-horon and Lower Beth-horon
2Chronicles8:6 He also rebuilt Baalath and other supply centers at this time and constructed cities where his chariots and horses could be kept. He built to his heart's content in Jerusalem and Lebanon and throughout the entire realm.
2Chronicles8:7 There were still some people living in the land who were not Israelites
2Chronicles8:8 These were descendants of the nations that Israel had not completely destroyed. So Solomon conscripted them for his labor force
2Chronicles8:9 But Solomon did not conscript any of the Israelites for forced labor. Instead
2Chronicles8:10 King Solomon also appointed 250 of them to supervise the various projects.
2Chronicles8:11 Solomon moved his wife
2Chronicles8:12 Then Solomon sacrificed burnt offerings to the LORD on the altar he had built in front of the foyer of the Temple.
2Chronicles8:13 The number of sacrifices varied from day to day according to the commands Moses had given. Extra sacrifices were offered on the Sabbaths
2Chronicles8:14 In assigning the priests to their duties
2Chronicles8:15 Solomon did not deviate in any way from David's commands concerning the priests and Levites and the treasuries.
2Chronicles8:16 So Solomon made sure that all the work related to building the Temple of the LORD was carried out
2Chronicles8:17 Later Solomon went to Ezion-geber and Elath
2Chronicles8:18 Hiram sent him ships commanded by his own officers and manned by experienced crews of sailors. These ships sailed to the land of Ophir with Solomon's men and brought back to Solomon almost seventeen tons of gold.
2Chronicles9:1 When the queen of Sheba heard of Solomon's reputation
2Chronicles9:2 When she met with Solomon
2Chronicles9:3 When the queen of Sheba realized how wise Solomon was
2Chronicles9:4 she was breathless. She was also amazed at the food on his tables
2Chronicles9:5 She exclaimed to the king
2Chronicles9:6 I didn't believe it until I arrived here and saw it with my own eyes. Truly I had not heard the half of it! Your wisdom is far greater than what I was told.
2Chronicles9:7 How happy these people must be! What a privilege for your officials to stand here day after day
2Chronicles9:8 The LORD your God is great indeed! He delights in you and has placed you on the throne to rule for him. Because God loves Israel so much and desires this kingdom to last forever
2Chronicles9:9 Then she gave the king a gift of nine thousand pounds of gold
2Chronicles9:10 (When the crews of Hiram and Solomon brought gold from Ophir
2Chronicles9:11 The king used the almug wood to make steps for the Temple of the LORD and the royal palace
2Chronicles9:12 King Solomon gave the queen of Sheba whatever she asked for--gifts of greater value than the gifts she had given him. Then she and all her attendants left and returned to their own land.
2Chronicles9:13 Each year Solomon received about 25 tons of gold.
2Chronicles9:14 This did not include the additional revenue he received from merchants and traders. All the kings of Arabia and the governors of the land also brought gold and silver to Solomon.
2Chronicles9:15 King Solomon made two hundred large shields of hammered gold
2Chronicles9:16 He also made three hundred smaller shields of hammered gold
2Chronicles9:17 Then the king made a huge ivory throne and overlaid it with pure gold.
2Chronicles9:18 The throne had six steps
2Chronicles9:19 Solomon made twelve other lion figures
2Chronicles9:20 All of King Solomon's drinking cups were solid gold
2Chronicles9:21 The king had a fleet of trading ships manned by the sailors sent by Hiram. Once every three years the ships returned
2Chronicles9:22 So King Solomon became richer and wiser than any other king in all the earth.
2Chronicles9:23 Kings from every nation came to visit him and to hear the wisdom God had given him.
2Chronicles9:24 Year after year
2Chronicles9:25 Solomon had four thousand stalls for his chariot horses and twelve thousand horses. He stationed many of them in the chariot cities
2Chronicles9:26 He ruled over all the kings from the Euphrates River to the land of the Philistines and the border of Egypt.
2Chronicles9:27 The king made silver as plentiful in Jerusalem as stones. And valuable cedarwood was as common as the sycamore wood that grows in the foothills of Judah.
2Chronicles9:28 Solomon's horses were imported from Egypt and many other countries.
2Chronicles9:29 The rest of the events of Solomon's reign
2Chronicles9:30 Solomon ruled in Jerusalem over all Israel for forty years.
2Chronicles9:31 When he died

2Chronicles10:2 When Jeroboam son of Nebat heard of Solomon's death
2Chronicles10:3 The leaders of Israel sent for Jeroboam

Ezra3:1 Now in early autumn
Ezra3:2 Then Jeshua son of Jehozadak with his fellow priests and Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel with his family began to rebuild the altar of the God of Israel so they could sacrifice burnt offerings on it

Ezra3:5 They also offered the regular burnt offerings and the offerings required for the new moon celebrations and the other annual festivals to the LORD. Freewill offerings were also sacrificed to the LORD by the people.
Ezra3:6 Fifteen days before the Festival of Shelters began
Ezra3:7 Then they hired masons and carpenters and bought cedar logs from the people of Tyre and Sidon

Ezra4:1 The enemies of Judah and Benjamin heard that the exiles were rebuilding a Temple to the LORD
Ezra4:2 So they approached Zerubbabel and the other leaders and said
Ezra4:3 But Zerubbabel
Ezra4:4 Then the local residents tried to discourage and frighten the people of Judah to keep them from their work.
Ezra4:5 They bribed agents to work against them and to frustrate their aims. This went on during the entire reign of King Cyrus of Persia and lasted until King Darius of Persia took the throne.
Ezra4:6 Years later when Xerxes began his reign
Ezra4:7 And even later
Ezra4:8 Rehum the governor and Shimshai the court secretary wrote the letter
Ezra4:9 They greeted the king for all their colleagues--the judges and local leaders
Ezra4:10 They also sent greetings from the rest of the people whom the great and noble Ashurbanipal had deported and relocated in Samaria and throughout the neighboring lands of the province west of the Euphrates River.
Ezra4:11 This is a copy of the letter they sent him: To Artaxerxes
Ezra4:12 Please be informed that the Jews who came here to Jerusalem from Babylon are rebuilding this rebellious and evil city. They have already laid the foundation for its walls and will soon complete them.
Ezra4:13 But we wish you to know that if this city is rebuilt and its walls are completed
Ezra4:14 Since we are loyal to you as your subjects and we do not want to see you dishonored in this way
Ezra4:15 We suggest that you search your ancestors' records
Ezra4:16 We declare that if this city is rebuilt and its walls are completed
Ezra4:17 Then Artaxerxes made this reply: To Rehum the governor
Ezra4:18 Greetings. The letter you sent has been translated and read to me.
Ezra4:19 I have ordered a search to be made of the records and have indeed found that Jerusalem has in times past been a hotbed of insurrection against many kings. In fact
Ezra4:20 Powerful kings have ruled over Jerusalem and the entire province west of the Euphrates River and have received vast tribute
Ezra4:21 Therefore
Ezra4:22 Do not delay
Ezra4:23 When this letter from King Artaxerxes was read to Rehum
Ezra4:24 The work on the Temple of God in Jerusalem had stopped

Ezra6:1 So King Darius issued orders that a search be made in the Babylonian archives
Ezra6:2 But it was at the fortress at Ecbatana in the province of Media that a scroll was found. This is what it said:
Ezra6:3 Memorandum: In the first year of King Cyrus's reign
Ezra6:4 Every three layers of specially prepared stones will be topped by a layer of timber. All expenses will be paid by the royal treasury.
Ezra6:5 And the gold and silver utensils
Ezra6:6 So King Darius sent this message: To Tattenai
Ezra6:7 Do not disturb the construction of the Temple of God. Let it be rebuilt on its former site
Ezra6:8 Moreover I hereby decree that you are to help these leaders of the Jews as they rebuild this Temple of God. You must pay the full construction costs without delay from my taxes collected in your province so that the work will not be discontinued.
Ezra6:9 Give the priests in Jerusalem whatever is needed in the way of young bulls
Ezra6:10 Then they will be able to offer acceptable sacrifices to the God of heaven and pray for me and my sons.
Ezra6:11 Those who violate this decree in any way will have a beam pulled from their house. Then they will be tied to it and flogged
Ezra6:12 May the God who has chosen the city of Jerusalem as the place to honor his name destroy any king or nation that violates this command and destroys this Temple. I
Ezra6:13 Tattenai
Ezra6:14 So the Jewish leaders continued their work
Ezra6:15 The Temple was completed on March 12
Ezra6:16 The Temple of God was then dedicated with great joy by the people of Israel
Ezra6:17 During the dedication ceremony for the Temple of God
Ezra6:18 Then the priests and Levites were divided into their various divisions to serve at the Temple of God in Jerusalem
Ezra6:19 On April 21 the returned exiles celebrated Passover.
Ezra6:20 The priests and Levites had purified themselves and were ceremonially clean. So they slaughtered the Passover lamb for all the returned exiles
Ezra6:21 The Passover meal was eaten by the people of Israel who had returned from exile and by the others in the land who had turned from their immoral customs to worship the LORD
Ezra6:22 They ate the Passover meal and celebrated the Festival of Unleavened Bread for seven days. There was great joy throughout the land because the LORD had changed the attitude of the king of Assyria toward them

Ezra9:1 But then the Jewish leaders came to me and said
Ezra9:2 For the men of Israel have married women from these people and have taken them as wives for their sons. So the holy race has become polluted by these mixed marriages. To make matters worse
Ezra9:3 When I heard this
Ezra9:4 Then all who trembled at the words of the God of Israel came and sat with me because of this unfaithfulness of his people. And I sat there utterly appalled until the time of the evening sacrifice.
Ezra9:5 At the time of the sacrifice
Ezra9:6 I prayed
Ezra9:7 Our whole history has been one of great sin. That is why we and our kings and our priests have been at the mercy of the pagan kings of the land. We have been killed
Ezra9:8 But now we have been given a brief moment of grace
Ezra9:9 For we were slaves
Ezra9:10 And now
Ezra9:11 Your servants the prophets warned us that the land we would possess was totally defiled by the detestable practices of the people living there. From one end to the other
Ezra9:12 You told us not to let our daughters marry their sons
Ezra9:13 Now we are being punished because of our wickedness and our great guilt. But we have actually been punished far less than we deserve
Ezra9:14 But now we are again breaking your commands and intermarrying with people who do these detestable things. Surely your anger will destroy us until even this little remnant no longer survives.
Ezra9:15 O LORD

Nehemiah3:1 Then Eliashib the high priest and the other priests started to rebuild at the Sheep Gate. They dedicated it and set up its doors
Nehemiah3:2 People from the city of Jericho worked next to them
Nehemiah3:3 The Fish Gate was built by the sons of Hassenaah. They did the whole thing--laid the beams
Nehemiah3:4 Meremoth son of Uriah and grandson of Hakkoz repaired the next section of wall. Beside him were Meshullam son of Berekiah and grandson of Meshezabel
Nehemiah3:5 Next were the people from Tekoa

Nehemiah3:8 Next was Uzziel son of Harhaiah
Nehemiah3:9 Rephaiah son of Hur

Nehemiah3:11 Then came Malkijah son of Harim and Hasshub son of Pahath-moab

Nehemiah3:14 The Dung Gate was repaired by Malkijah son of Recab
Nehemiah3:15 Shallum son of Col-hozeh
Nehemiah3:16 Next to him was Nehemiah son of Azbuk

Nehemiah3:23 After them

Nehemiah4:1 Sanballat was very angry when he learned that we were rebuilding the wall. He flew into a rage and mocked the Jews
Nehemiah4:2 saying in front of his friends and the Samarian army officers
Nehemiah4:3 Tobiah the Ammonite
Nehemiah4:4 Then I prayed
Nehemiah4:5 Do not ignore their guilt. Do not blot out their sins
Nehemiah4:6 At last the wall was completed to half its original height around the entire city
Nehemiah4:7 But when Sanballat and Tobiah and the Arabs
Nehemiah4:8 They all made plans to come and fight against Jerusalem and to bring about confusion there.
Nehemiah4:9 But we prayed to our God and guarded the city day and night to protect ourselves.
Nehemiah4:10 Then the people of Judah began to complain that the workers were becoming tired. There was so much rubble to be moved that we could never get it done by ourselves.
Nehemiah4:11 Meanwhile
Nehemiah4:12 The Jews who lived near the enemy came and told us again and again
Nehemiah4:13 So I placed armed guards behind the lowest parts of the wall in the exposed areas. I stationed the people to stand guard by families
Nehemiah4:14 Then as I looked over the situation
Nehemiah4:15 When our enemies heard that we knew of their plans and that God had frustrated them
Nehemiah4:16 But from then on
Nehemiah4:17 who were building the wall. The common laborers carried on their work with one hand supporting their load and one hand holding a weapon.
Nehemiah4:18 All the builders had a sword belted to their side. The trumpeter stayed with me to sound the alarm.
Nehemiah4:19 Then I explained to the nobles and officials and all the people
Nehemiah4:20 When you hear the blast of the trumpet
Nehemiah4:21 We worked early and late
Nehemiah4:22 I also told everyone living outside the walls to move into Jerusalem. That way they and their servants could go on guard duty at night as well as work during the day.
Nehemiah4:23 During this time
Nehemiah5:1 About this time some of the men and their wives raised a cry of protest against their fellow Jews.
Nehemiah5:2 They were saying
Nehemiah5:3 Others said
Nehemiah5:4 And others said
Nehemiah5:5 We belong to the same family

Nehemiah5:8 At the meeting I said to them
Nehemiah5:9 Then I pressed further
Nehemiah5:10 I myself
Nehemiah5:11 You must restore their fields
Nehemiah5:12 Then they replied
Nehemiah5:13 I shook out the fold of my robe and said
Nehemiah5:14 I would like to mention that for the entire twelve years that I was governor of Judah--from the twentieth until the thirty-second year of the reign of King Artaxerxes--neither I nor my officials drew on our official food allowance.
Nehemiah5:15 This was quite a contrast to the former governors who had laid heavy burdens on the people
Nehemiah5:16 I devoted myself to working on the wall and refused to acquire any land. And I required all my officials to spend time working on the wall.
Nehemiah5:17 I asked for nothing
Nehemiah5:18 The provisions required at my expense for each day were one ox
Nehemiah5:19 Remember
Nehemiah6:1 When Sanballat
Nehemiah6:2 Sanballat and Geshem sent me a message asking me to meet them at one of the villages in the plain of Ono. But I realized they were plotting to harm me
Nehemiah6:3 so I replied by sending this message to them: I am doing a great work! I cannot stop to come and meet with you.
Nehemiah6:4 Four times they sent the same message
Nehemiah6:5 The fifth time
Nehemiah6:6 and this is what it said: Geshem tells me that everywhere he goes he hears that you and the Jews are planning to rebel and that is why you are building the wall. According to his reports
Nehemiah6:7 He also reports that you have appointed prophets to prophesy about you in Jerusalem
Nehemiah6:8 My reply was
Nehemiah6:9 They were just trying to intimidate us

Nehemiah6:11 But I replied
Nehemiah6:12 I realized that God had not spoken to him
Nehemiah6:13 They were hoping to intimidate me and make me sin by following his suggestion. Then they would be able to accuse and discredit me.
Nehemiah6:14 Remember
Nehemiah6:15 So on October 2 the wall was finally finished--just fifty-two days after we had begun.

Nehemiah6:17 During those fifty-two days
Nehemiah6:18 For many in Judah had sworn allegiance to him because his father-in-law was Shecaniah son of Arah and because his son Jehohanan was married to the daughter of Meshullam son of Berekiah.
Nehemiah6:19 They kept telling me what a wonderful man Tobiah was
