Nehemiah8:1 all the people assembled together as one person at the square just inside the Water Gate. They asked Ezra the scribe to bring out the Book of the Law of Moses
Nehemiah8:2 So on October 8 Ezra the priest brought the scroll of the law before the assembly
Nehemiah8:3 He faced the square just inside the Water Gate from early morning until noon and read aloud to everyone who could understand. All the people paid close attention to the Book of the Law.
Nehemiah8:4 Ezra the scribe stood on a high wooden platform that had been made for the occasion. To his right stood Mattithiah
Nehemiah8:5 Ezra stood on the platform in full view of all the people. When they saw him open the book
Nehemiah8:6 Then Ezra praised the LORD
Nehemiah8:7 Now the Levites--Jeshua
Nehemiah8:8 They read from the Book of the Law of God and clearly explained the meaning of what was being read
Nehemiah8:9 Then Nehemiah the governor
Nehemiah8:10 And Nehemiah continued
Nehemiah8:11 And the Levites
Nehemiah8:12 So the people went away to eat and drink at a festive meal
Nehemiah8:13 On October 9 the family leaders and the priests and Levites met with Ezra to go over the law in greater detail.
Nehemiah8:14 As they studied the law
Nehemiah8:15 He had said that a proclamation should be made throughout their towns and especially in Jerusalem
Nehemiah8:16 So the people went out and cut branches and used them to build shelters on the roofs of their houses
Nehemiah8:17 So everyone who had returned from captivity lived in these shelters for the seven days of the festival
Nehemiah8:18 Ezra read from the Book of the Law of God on each of the seven days of the festival. Then on October 15 they held a solemn assembly
Nehemiah9:1 On October 31 the people returned for another observance. This time they fasted and dressed in sackcloth and sprinkled dust on their heads.
Nehemiah9:2 Those of Israelite descent separated themselves from all foreigners as they confessed their own sins and the sins of their ancestors.
Nehemiah9:3 The Book of the Law of the LORD their God was read aloud to them for about three hours. Then for three more hours they took turns confessing their sins and worshiping the LORD their God.
Nehemiah9:4 Some of the Levites were standing on the stairs
Nehemiah9:5 Then the leaders of the Levites--Jeshua
Nehemiah9:6 You alone are the LORD. You made the skies and the heavens and all the stars. You made the earth and the seas and everything in them. You preserve and give life to everything
Nehemiah9:7 You are the LORD God
Nehemiah9:8 When he had proved himself faithful
Nehemiah9:9 You saw the sufferings and sorrows of our ancestors in Egypt
Nehemiah9:10 You displayed miraculous signs and wonders against Pharaoh
Nehemiah9:11 You divided the sea for your people so they could walk through on dry land! And then you hurled their enemies into the depths of the sea. They sank like stones beneath the mighty waters.
Nehemiah9:12 You led our ancestors by a pillar of cloud during the day and a pillar of fire at night so that they could find their way.
Nehemiah9:13 You came down on Mount Sinai and spoke to them from heaven. You gave them regulations and instructions that were just
Nehemiah9:14 You instructed them concerning the laws of your holy Sabbath. And you commanded them
Nehemiah9:15 You gave them bread from heaven when they were hungry and water from the rock when they were thirsty. You commanded them to go and take possession of the land you had sworn to give them.
Nehemiah9:16 But our ancestors were a proud and stubborn lot
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to listen and did not remember the miracles you had done for them. Instead
Nehemiah9:18 even though they made an idol shaped like a calf and said
Nehemiah9:19 But in your great mercy you did not abandon them to die in the wilderness. The pillar of cloud still led them forward by day
Nehemiah9:20 You sent your good Spirit to instruct them
Nehemiah9:21 For forty years you sustained them in the wilderness. They lacked nothing in all that time. Their clothes did not wear out
Nehemiah9:22 Then you helped our ancestors conquer great kingdoms and many nations
Nehemiah9:23 You made their descendants as numerous as the stars in the sky and brought them into the land you had promised to their ancestors.
Nehemiah9:24 They went in and took possession of the land. You subdued whole nations before them. Even the kings and the Canaanites
Nehemiah9:25 Our ancestors captured fortified cities and fertile land. They took over houses full of good things
Nehemiah9:26 But despite all this
Nehemiah9:27 So you handed them over to their enemies. But in their time of trouble they cried to you
Nehemiah9:28 But when all was going well
Nehemiah9:29 You warned them to return to your law
Nehemiah9:30 In your love
Nehemiah9:31 But in your great mercy
Nehemiah9:32 And now
Nehemiah9:33 Every time you punished us you were being just. We have sinned greatly
Nehemiah9:34 Our kings
Nehemiah9:35 Even while they had their own kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 So now today we are slaves here in the land of plenty that you gave to our ancestors! We are slaves among all this abundance!
Nehemiah9:37 The lush produce of this land piles up in the hands of the kings whom you have set over us because of our sins. They have power over us and our cattle. We serve them at their pleasure
Nehemiah9:38 Yet in spite of all this

Nehemiah10:30 We promise not to let our daughters marry the pagan people of the land

Nehemiah10:34 We have cast sacred lots to determine when--at regular times each year--the families of the priests
Nehemiah10:35 We promise always to bring the first part of every harvest to the LORD's Temple--whether it be a crop from the soil or from our fruit trees.
Nehemiah10:36 We agree to give to God our oldest sons and the firstborn of all our herds and flocks
Nehemiah10:37 We will store the produce in the storerooms of the Temple of our God. We will bring the best of our flour and other grain offerings
Nehemiah10:38 A priest--a descendant of Aaron--will be with the Levites as they receive these tithes. And a tenth of all that is collected as tithes will be delivered by the Levites to the Temple of our God and placed in the storerooms.
Nehemiah10:39 The people and the Levites must bring these offerings of grain

Esther1:1 This happened in the days of King Xerxes
Esther1:2 At that time he ruled his empire from his throne at the fortress of Susa.
Esther1:3 In the third year of his reign
Esther1:4 The celebration lasted six months--a tremendous display of the opulent wealth and glory of his empire.
Esther1:5 When it was all over
Esther1:6 The courtyard was decorated with beautifully woven white and blue linen hangings
Esther1:7 Drinks were served in gold goblets of many designs
Esther1:8 The only restriction on the drinking was that no one should be compelled to take more than he wanted. But those who wished could have as much as they pleased
Esther1:9 Queen Vashti gave a banquet for the women of the palace at the same time.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day of the feast
Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti to him with the royal crown on her head. He wanted all the men to gaze on her beauty
Esther1:12 But when they conveyed the king's order to Queen Vashti
Esther1:13 He immediately consulted with his advisers
Esther1:14 The names of these men were Carshena
Esther1:15 What must be done to Queen Vashti? the king demanded. What penalty does the law provide for a queen who refuses to obey the king's orders
Esther1:16 Memucan answered the king and his princes
Esther1:17 Women everywhere will begin to despise their husbands when they learn that Queen Vashti has refused to appear before the king.
Esther1:18 Before this day is out
Esther1:19 So if it please the king
Esther1:20 When this decree is published throughout your vast empire
Esther1:21 The king and his princes thought this made good sense
Esther1:22 He sent letters to all parts of the empire

Esther2:6 His family had been exiled from Jerusalem to Babylon by King Nebuchadnezzar
Esther2:7 This man had a beautiful and lovely young cousin
Esther2:8 As a result of the king's decree
Esther2:9 Hegai was very impressed with Esther and treated her kindly. He quickly ordered a special menu for her and provided her with beauty treatments. He also assigned her seven maids specially chosen from the king's palace
Esther2:10 Esther had not told anyone of her nationality and family background
Esther2:11 Every day Mordecai would take a walk near the courtyard of the harem to ask about Esther and to find out what was happening to her.
Esther2:12 Before each young woman was taken to the king's bed

Esther2:16 When Esther was taken to King Xerxes at the royal palace in early winter of the seventh year of his reign
Esther2:17 the king loved her more than any of the other young women. He was so delighted with her that he set the royal crown on her head and declared her queen instead of Vashti.

Esther2:22 But Mordecai heard about the plot and passed the information on to Queen Esther. She then told the king about it and gave Mordecai credit for the report.

Esther4:1 When Mordecai learned what had been done
Esther4:2 He stood outside the gate of the palace
Esther4:3 And as news of the king's decree reached all the provinces
Esther4:4 When Queen Esther's maids and eunuchs came and told her about Mordecai
Esther4:5 Then Esther sent for Hathach
Esther4:6 So Hathach went out to Mordecai in the square in front of the palace gate.
Esther4:7 Mordecai told him the whole story and told him how much money Haman had promised to pay into the royal treasury for the destruction of the Jews.
Esther4:8 Mordecai gave Hathach a copy of the decree issued in Susa that called for the death of all Jews
Esther4:9 So Hathach returned to Esther with Mordecai's message.
Esther4:10 Then Esther told Hathach to go back and relay this message to Mordecai:
Esther4:11 The whole world knows that anyone who appears before the king in his inner court without being invited is doomed to die unless the king holds out his gold scepter. And the king has not called for me to come to him in more than a month.
Esther4:12 So Hathach gave Esther's message to Mordecai.
Esther4:13 Mordecai sent back this reply to Esther: Don't think for a moment that you will escape there in the palace when all other Jews are killed.
Esther4:14 If you keep quiet at a time like this
Esther4:15 Then Esther sent this reply to Mordecai:
Esther4:16 Go and gather together all the Jews of Susa and fast for me. Do not eat or drink for three days
Esther4:17 So Mordecai went away and did as Esther told him.

Esther8:9 So on June 25 the king's secretaries were summoned. As Mordecai dictated
Esther8:10 Mordecai wrote in the name of King Xerxes and sealed the message with the king's signet ring. He sent the letters by swift messengers
Esther8:11 The king's decree gave the Jews in every city authority to unite to defend their lives. They were allowed to kill
Esther8:12 The day chosen for this event throughout all the provinces of King Xerxes was March 7 of the next year.
Esther8:13 A copy of this decree was to be recognized as law in every province and proclaimed to all the people. That way the Jews would be ready on that day to take revenge on their enemies.
Esther8:14 So urged on by the king's command
Esther8:15 Then Mordecai put on the royal robe of blue and white and the great crown of gold
Esther8:16 The Jews were filled with joy and gladness and were honored everywhere.
Esther8:17 In every city and province
Esther9:1 So on March 7 the two decrees of the king were put into effect. On that day
Esther9:2 The Jews gathered in their cities throughout all the king's provinces to defend themselves against anyone who might try to harm them. But no one could make a stand against them
Esther9:3 And all the commanders of the provinces
Esther9:4 For Mordecai had been promoted in the king's palace
Esther9:5 But the Jews went ahead on the appointed day and struck down their enemies with the sword. They killed and annihilated their enemies and did as they pleased with those who hated them.
Esther9:6 They killed five hundred people in the fortress of Susa.
Esther9:7 They also killed Parshandatha
Esther9:8 Poratha
Esther9:9 Parmashta
Esther9:10 the ten sons of Haman son of Hammedatha
Esther9:11 That evening
Esther9:12 he called for Queen Esther and said
Esther9:13 And Esther said
Esther9:14 So the king agreed
Esther9:15 Then the Jews at Susa gathered together on March 8 and killed three hundred more people
Esther9:16 Meanwhile
Esther9:17 Throughout the provinces this was done on March 7. Then on the following day they rested
Esther9:18 But the Jews at Susa continued killing their enemies on the second day also
Esther9:19 So to this day
Esther9:20 Mordecai recorded these events and sent letters to the Jews near and far
Esther9:21 encouraging them to celebrate an annual festival on these two days.
Esther9:22 He told them to celebrate these days with feasting and gladness and by giving gifts to each other and to the poor. This would commemorate a time when the Jews gained relief from their enemies
Esther9:23 So the Jews adopted Mordecai's suggestion and began this annual custom.
Esther9:24 Haman son of Hammedatha the Agagite
Esther9:25 But when Esther came before the king
Esther9:26 (That is why this celebration is called Purim
Esther9:27 the Jews throughout the realm agreed to inaugurate this tradition and to pass it on to their descendants and to all who became Jews. They declared they would never fail to celebrate these two prescribed days at the appointed time each year.
Esther9:28 These days would be remembered and kept from generation to generation and celebrated by every family throughout the provinces and cities of the empire. These days would never cease to be celebrated among the Jews
Esther9:29 Then Queen Esther
Esther9:30 In addition
Esther9:31 These letters established the Festival of Purim--an annual celebration of these days at the appointed time
Esther9:32 So the command of Esther confirmed the practices of Purim

Esther10:2 His great achievements and the full account of the greatness of Mordecai
Esther10:3 Mordecai the Jew became the prime minister
Job1:1 There was a man named Job who lived in the land of Uz. He was blameless
Job1:2 He had seven sons and three daughters.
Job1:3 He owned seven thousand sheep
Job1:4 Every year when Job's sons had birthdays
Job1:5 When these celebrations ended--and sometimes they lasted several days--Job would purify his children. He would get up early in the morning and offer a burnt offering for each of them. For Job said to himself
Job1:6 One day the angels came to present themselves before the LORD
Job1:7 Where have you come from? the LORD asked Satan. And Satan answered the LORD
Job1:8 Then the LORD asked Satan
Job1:9 Satan replied to the LORD
Job1:10 You have always protected him and his home and his property from harm. You have made him prosperous in everything he does. Look how rich he is!
Job1:11 But take away everything he has
Job1:12 All right
Job1:13 One day when Job's sons and daughters were dining at the oldest brother's house
Job1:14 a messenger arrived at Job's home with this news: Your oxen were plowing
Job1:15 when the Sabeans raided us. They stole all the animals and killed all the farmhands. I am the only one who escaped to tell you.
Job1:16 While he was still speaking
Job1:17 While he was still speaking
Job1:18 While he was still speaking
Job1:19 Suddenly
Job1:20 Job stood up and tore his robe in grief. Then he shaved his head and fell to the ground before God.
Job1:21 He said
Job1:22 In all of this

Job2:2 Where have you come from? the LORD asked Satan. And Satan answered the LORD
Job2:3 Then the LORD asked Satan
Job2:4 Satan replied to the LORD
Job2:5 But take away his health
Job2:6 All right
Job2:7 So Satan left the LORD's presence

Job3:1 At last Job spoke
Job3:2 He said:
Job3:3 Cursed be the day of my birth
Job3:4 Let that day be turned to darkness. Let it be lost even to God on high
Job3:5 Yes
Job3:6 Let that night be blotted off the calendar
Job3:7 Let that night be barren. Let it have no joy.
Job3:8 Let those who are experts at cursing--those who are ready to rouse the sea monster--curse that day.
Job3:9 Let its morning stars remain dark. Let it hope for light
Job3:10 Curse it for its failure to shut my mother's womb
Job3:11 Why didn't I die at birth as I came from the womb?
Job3:12 Why did my mother let me live? Why did she nurse me at her breasts?
Job3:13 For if I had died at birth
Job3:14 I would rest with the world's kings and prime ministers
Job3:15 I would rest with wealthy princes whose palaces were filled with gold and silver.
Job3:16 Why was I not buried like a stillborn child
Job3:17 For in death the wicked cease from troubling
Job3:18 Even prisoners are at ease in death
Job3:19 Rich and poor are there alike
Job3:20 Oh
Job3:21 They long for death
Job3:22 It is a blessed relief when they finally die
Job3:23 Why is life given to those with no future
Job3:24 I cannot eat for sighing; my groans pour out like water.
Job3:25 What I always feared has happened to me. What I dreaded has come to be.
Job3:26 I have no peace
Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied to Job:
Job4:2 Will you be patient and let me say a word? For who could keep from speaking out?
Job4:3 In the past you have encouraged many a troubled soul to trust in God; you have supported those who were weak.
Job4:4 Your words have strengthened the fallen; you steadied those who wavered.
Job4:5 But now when trouble strikes
Job4:6 Does your reverence for God give you no confidence? Shouldn't you believe that God will care for those who are upright?
Job4:7 Stop and think! Does the innocent person perish? When has the upright person been destroyed?
Job4:8 My experience shows that those who plant trouble and cultivate evil will harvest the same.
Job4:9 They perish by a breath from God. They vanish in a blast of his anger.
Job4:10 Though they are fierce young lions
Job4:11 The fierce lion will starve
Job4:12 This truth was given me in secret
Job4:13 It came in a vision at night as others slept.
Job4:14 Fear gripped me; I trembled and shook with terror.
Job4:15 A spirit swept past my face. Its wind sent shivers up my spine.
Job4:16 It stopped
Job4:17 `Can a mortal be just and upright before God? Can a person be pure before the Creator?'
Job4:18 If God cannot trust his own angels and has charged some of them with folly
Job4:19 how much less will he trust those made of clay! Their foundation is dust
Job4:20 They are alive in the morning
Job4:21 Their tent collapses; they die in ignorance.
Job5:1 You may cry for help
Job5:2 Surely resentment destroys the fool
Job5:3 From my experience
Job5:4 Their children are abandoned far from help
Job5:5 Their harvests are stolen
Job5:6 But evil does not spring from the soil
Job5:7 People are born for trouble as predictably as sparks fly upward from a fire.
Job5:8 My advice to you is this: Go to God and present your case to him.
Job5:9 For he does great works too marvelous to understand. He performs miracles without number.
Job5:10 He gives rain for the earth. He sends water for the fields.
Job5:11 He gives prosperity to the poor and humble
Job5:12 He frustrates the plans of the crafty
Job5:13 He catches those who think they are wise in their own cleverness
Job5:14 They grope in the daylight as though they were blind; they see no better in the daytime than at night.
Job5:15 He rescues the poor from the cutting words of the strong. He saves them from the clutches of the powerful.
Job5:16 And so at last the poor have hope
Job5:17 But consider the joy of those corrected by God! Do not despise the chastening of the Almighty when you sin.
Job5:18 For though he wounds
Job5:19 He will rescue you again and again so that no evil can touch you.
Job5:20 He will save you from death in time of famine
Job5:21 You will be safe from slander and will have no fear of destruction when it comes.
Job5:22 You will laugh at destruction and famine; wild animals will not terrify you.
Job5:23 You will be at peace with the stones of the field
Job5:24 You will know that your home is kept safe. When you visit your pastures
Job5:25 Your children will be many; your descendants will be as plentiful as grass!
Job5:26 You will live to a good old age. You will not be harvested until the proper time!
Job5:27 We have found from experience that all this is true. Listen to my counsel

Job6:2 If my sadness could be weighed and my troubles be put on the scales
Job6:3 they would be heavier than all the sands of the sea. That is why I spoke so rashly.

Job6:5 Don't I have a right to complain? Wild donkeys bray when they find no green grass
Job6:6 People complain when there is no salt in their food. And how tasteless is the uncooked white of an egg!
Job6:7 My appetite disappears when I look at it; I gag at the thought of eating it!
Job6:8 Oh

Job6:13 No

Job6:15 My brother

Job6:27 You would even send an orphan into slavery or sell a friend.
Job6:28 Look at me! Would I lie to your face?
Job6:29 Stop assuming my guilt
Job6:30 Do you think I am lying? Don't I know the difference between right and wrong?

Job8:6 if you are pure and live with complete integrity

Job18:2 How long before you stop talking? Speak sense if you want us to answer!

Job18:4 You may tear your hair out in anger
Job18:5 The truth remains that the light of the wicked will be snuffed out. The sparks of their fire will not glow.
Job18:6 The light in their tent will grow dark. The lamp hanging above them will be quenched.
Job18:7 The confident stride of the wicked will be shortened. Their own schemes will be their downfall.
Job18:8 The wicked walk into a net. They fall into a pit that's been dug in the path.
Job18:9 A trap grabs them by the heel. A noose tightens around them.
Job18:10 A snare lies hidden in the ground. A rope lies coiled on their path.
Job18:11 Terrors surround the wicked and trouble them at every step.
Job18:12 Their vigor is depleted by hunger
Job18:13 Disease eats their skin; death devours their limbs.
Job18:14 They are torn from the security of their tent
Job18:15 The home of the wicked will disappear beneath a fiery barrage of burning sulfur.
Job18:16 Their roots will dry up
Job18:17 All memory of their existence will perish from the earth. No one will remember them.
Job18:18 They will be thrust from light into darkness
Job18:19 They will have neither children nor grandchildren
Job18:20 People in the west are appalled at their fate; people in the east are horrified.
Job18:21 They will say
Job19:1 Then Job spoke again:
Job19:2 How long will you torture me? How long will you try to break me with your words?
Job19:3 Ten times now you have meant to insult me. You should be ashamed of dealing with me so harshly.
Job19:4 And even if I have sinned
Job19:5 You are trying to overcome me
Job19:6 but it is God who has wronged me. I cannot defend myself
Job19:7 I cry out for help
Job19:8 God has blocked my way and plunged my path into darkness.
Job19:9 He has stripped me of my honor and removed the crown from my head.
Job19:10 He has demolished me on every side
Job19:11 His fury burns against me; he counts me as an enemy.
Job19:12 His troops advance. They build up roads to attack me. They camp all around my tent.
Job19:13 My relatives stay far away
Job19:14 My neighbors and my close friends are all gone.
Job19:15 The members of my household have forgotten me. The servant girls consider me a stranger. I am like a foreigner to them.
Job19:16 I call my servant
Job19:17 My breath is repulsive to my wife. I am loathsome to my own family.
Job19:18 Even young children despise me. When I stand to speak
Job19:19 My close friends abhor me. Those I loved have turned against me.
Job19:20 I have been reduced to skin and bones and have escaped death by the skin of my teeth.
Job19:21 Have mercy on me
Job19:22 Why must you persecute me as God does? Why aren't you satisfied with my anguish?
Job19:23 Oh
Job19:24 carved with an iron chisel and filled with lead

Job19:26 And after my body has decayed
Job19:27 I will see him for myself. Yes
Job19:28 How dare you go on persecuting me
Job19:29 I warn you
Job20:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite replied:
Job20:2 I must reply because I am greatly disturbed.
Job20:3 I have had to endure your insults
Job20:4 Don't you realize that ever since people were first placed on the earth
Job20:5 the triumph of the wicked has been short-lived and the joy of the godless has been only temporary?
Job20:6 Though the godless man's pride reaches to the heavens and though his head touches the clouds
Job20:7 yet he will perish forever
Job20:8 He will fade like a dream and not be found. He will vanish like a vision in the night.
Job20:9 Neither his friends nor his family will ever see him again.
Job20:10 His children will beg from the poor
Job20:11 He was just a young man
Job20:12 He enjoyed the taste of his wickedness
Job20:13 He savored it
Job20:14 But suddenly
Job20:15 He will vomit the wealth he swallowed. God won't let him keep it down.
Job20:16 He will suck the poison of snakes. The viper will kill him.
Job20:17 He will never again enjoy abundant streams of olive oil or rivers of milk and honey.
Job20:18 His labors will not be rewarded. His wealth will bring him no joy.
Job20:19 For he oppressed the poor and left them destitute. He foreclosed on their homes.
Job20:20 He was always greedy but never satisfied. Of all the things he dreamed about
Job20:21 Nothing is left after he finishes gorging himself; therefore
Job20:22 In the midst of plenty
Job20:23 May God give him a bellyful of trouble. May God rain down his anger upon him.
Job20:24 He will try to escape
Job20:25 The arrow is pulled from his body
Job20:26 His treasures will be lost in deepest darkness. A wildfire will devour his goods
Job20:27 The heavens will reveal his guilt
Job20:28 A flood will sweep away his house. God's anger will descend on him in torrents.
Job20:29 This is the fate that awaits the wicked. It is the inheritance decreed by God.
Job21:1 Then Job spoke again:
Job21:2 Listen closely to what I am saying. You can console me by listening to me.
Job21:3 Bear with me
Job21:4 My complaint is with God
Job21:5 Look at me and be stunned. Put your hand over your mouth in shock.
Job21:6 When I think about what I am saying
Job21:7 The truth is that the wicked live to a good old age. They grow old and wealthy.
Job21:8 They live to see their children grow to maturity
Job21:9 Their homes are safe from every fear
Job21:10 Their bulls never fail to breed. Their cows bear calves without miscarriage.
Job21:11 Their children skip about like lambs in a flock of sheep.
Job21:12 They sing with tambourine and harp. They make merry to the sound of the flute.
Job21:13 They spend their days in prosperity; then they go down to the grave in peace.
Job21:14 All this
Job21:15 Who is the Almighty
Job21:16 But their prosperity is not of their own doing
Job21:17 Yet the wicked get away with it time and time again. They rarely have trouble
Job21:18 Are they driven before the wind like straw? Are they carried away by the storm? Not at all!
Job21:19  `Well
Job21:20 Let their own eyes see their destruction. Let them drink deeply of the anger of the Almighty.
Job21:21 For when they are dead
Job21:22 But who can teach a lesson to God
Job21:23 One person dies in prosperity and security
Job21:24 the very picture of good health.
Job21:25 Another person dies in bitter poverty
Job21:26 Both alike are buried in the same dust
Job21:27 Look
Job21:28 You will tell me of rich and wicked people who came to disaster because of their sins.
Job21:29 But I tell you to ask those who have been around
Job21:30 Evil people are spared in times of calamity and are allowed to escape.
Job21:31 No one rebukes them openly. No one repays them for what they have done.
Job21:32 When they are carried to the grave
Job21:33 A great funeral procession goes to the cemetery. Many pay their respects as the body is laid to rest and the earth gives sweet repose.
Job21:34 How can you comfort me? All your explanations are wrong!
Job22:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:
Job22:2 Can a person's actions be of benefit to God? Can even a wise person be helpful to him?

Job22:5 Not at all! It is because of your wickedness! Your guilt has no limit!

Job28:1 People know how to mine silver and refine gold.
Job28:2 They know how to dig iron from the earth and smelt copper from stone.
Job28:3 They know how to put light into darkness and explore the farthest
Job28:4 They sink a mine shaft into the earth far from where anyone lives. They descend on ropes
Job28:5 Bread comes from the earth
Job28:6 People know how to find sapphires and gold dust--
Job28:7 treasures that no bird of prey can see
Job28:8 for they are deep within the mines. No wild animal has ever walked upon those treasures; no lion has set his paw there.
Job28:9 People know how to tear apart flinty rocks and overturn the roots of mountains.
Job28:10 They cut tunnels in the rocks and uncover precious stones.
Job28:11 They dam up the trickling streams and bring to light the hidden treasures.
Job28:12 But do people know where to find wisdom? Where can they find understanding?
Job28:13 No one knows where to find it
Job28:14 `It is not here
Job28:15 It cannot be bought for gold or silver.
Job28:16 Its value is greater than all the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Wisdom is far more valuable than gold and crystal. It cannot be purchased with jewels mounted in fine gold.
Job28:18 Coral and valuable rock crystal are worthless in trying to get it. The price of wisdom is far above pearls.
Job28:19 Topaz from Ethiopia cannot be exchanged for it. Its value is greater than the purest gold.
Job28:20 But do people know where to find wisdom? Where can they find understanding?
Job28:21 For it is hidden from the eyes of all humanity. Even the sharp-eyed birds in the sky cannot discover it.
Job28:22 But Destruction and Death say
Job28:23 God surely knows where it can be found
Job28:24 for he looks throughout the whole earth
Job28:25 He made the winds blow and determined how much rain should fall.
Job28:26 He made the laws of the rain and prepared a path for the lightning.
Job28:27 Then
Job28:28 And this is what he says to all humanity: `The fear of the Lord is true wisdom; to forsake evil is real understanding.'

Job29:14 All I did was just and honest. Righteousness covered me like a robe

Job31:12 It is a devastating fire that destroys to hell. It would wipe out everything I own.

Job36:4 I am telling you the honest truth
Job36:5 God is mighty
Job36:6 He does not let the wicked live but gives justice to the afflicted.
Job36:7 His eyes never leave the innocent
Job36:8 If troubles come upon them and they are enslaved and afflicted
Job36:9 he takes the trouble to show them the reason. He shows them their sins
Job36:10 He gets their attention and says they must turn away from evil.
Job36:11 If they listen and obey God
Job36:12 But if they refuse to listen to him
Job36:13 For the godless are full of resentment. Even when he punishes them
Job36:14 They die young after wasting their lives in immoral living.
Job36:15 But by means of their suffering
Job36:16 God has led you away from danger
Job36:17 But you are too obsessed with judgment on the godless. Don't worry
Job36:18 But watch out
Job36:19 Could all your wealth and mighty efforts keep you from distress?
Job36:20 Do not long for the cover of night
Job36:21 Be on guard! Turn back from evil
Job36:22 Look
Job36:23 No one can tell him what to do. No one can say to him
Job36:24 Instead
Job36:25 Everyone has seen these things
Job36:26 Look
Job36:27 He draws up the water vapor and then distills it into rain.
Job36:28 The rain pours down from the clouds
Job36:29 Can anyone really understand the spreading of the clouds and the thunder that rolls forth from heaven?
Job36:30 See how he spreads the lightning around him and how it lights up the depths of the sea.
Job36:31 By his mighty acts he governs the people
Job36:32 He fills his hands with lightning bolts. He hurls each at its target.
Job36:33 The thunder announces his presence; the storm announces his indignant anger.
Job37:1 My heart pounds as I think of this. It leaps within me.
Job37:2 Listen carefully to the thunder of God's voice as it rolls from his mouth.
Job37:3 It rolls across the heavens
Job37:4 Then comes the roaring of the thunder--the tremendous voice of his majesty. He does not restrain the thunder when he speaks.
Job37:5 God's voice is glorious in the thunder. We cannot comprehend the greatness of his power.
Job37:6 He directs the snow to fall on the earth and tells the rain to pour down.
Job37:7 Everyone stops working at such a time so they can recognize his power.
Job37:8 The wild animals hide in the rocks or in their dens.
Job37:9 The stormy wind comes from its chamber
Job37:10 God's breath sends the ice
Job37:11 He loads the clouds with moisture
Job37:12 The clouds turn around and around under his direction. They do whatever he commands throughout the earth.
Job37:13 He causes things to happen on earth
Job37:14 Listen
Job37:15 Do you know how God controls the storm and causes the lightning to flash forth from his clouds?
Job37:16 Do you understand how he balances the clouds with wonderful perfection and skill?
Job37:17 When you are sweltering in your clothes and the south wind dies down and everything is still
Job37:18 he makes the skies reflect the heat like a giant mirror. Can you do that?
Job37:19 You think you know so much
Job37:20 Should God be told that I want to speak? Can we speak when we are confused?
Job37:21 We cannot look at the sun
Job37:22 Golden splendor comes from the mountain of God. He is clothed in dazzling splendor.
Job37:23 We cannot imagine the power of the Almighty
Job37:24 No wonder people everywhere fear him. People who are truly wise show him reverence.
Job38:1 Then the LORD answered Job from the whirlwind:
Job38:2 Who is this that questions my wisdom with such ignorant words?
Job38:3 Brace yourself
Job38:4 Where were you when I laid the foundations of the earth? Tell me
Job38:5 Do you know how its dimensions were determined and who did the surveying?
Job38:6 What supports its foundations
Job38:7 as the morning stars sang together and all the angels shouted for joy?
Job38:8 Who defined the boundaries of the sea as it burst from the womb
Job38:9 and as I clothed it with clouds and thick darkness?
Job38:10 For I locked it behind barred gates
Job38:11 I said
Job38:12 Have you ever commanded the morning to appear and caused the dawn to rise in the east?
Job38:13 Have you ever told the daylight to spread to the ends of the earth
Job38:14 For the features of the earth take shape as the light approaches
Job38:15 The light disturbs the haunts of the wicked
Job38:16 Have you explored the springs from which the seas come? Have you walked about and explored their depths?
Job38:17 Do you know where the gates of death are located? Have you seen the gates of utter gloom?
Job38:18 Do you realize the extent of the earth? Tell me about it if you know!
Job38:19 Where does the light come from
Job38:20 Can you take it to its home? Do you know how to get there?
Job38:21 But of course you know all this! For you were born before it was all created
Job38:22 Have you visited the treasuries of the snow? Have you seen where the hail is made and stored?
Job38:23 I have reserved it for the time of trouble
Job38:24 Where is the path to the origin of light? Where is the home of the east wind?
Job38:25 Who created a channel for the torrents of rain? Who laid out the path for the lightning?
Job38:26 Who makes the rain fall on barren land
Job38:27 Who sends the rain that satisfies the parched ground and makes the tender grass spring up?
Job38:28 Does the rain have a father? Where does dew come from?
Job38:29 Who is the mother of the ice? Who gives birth to the frost from the heavens?
Job38:30 For the water turns to ice as hard as rock
Job38:31 Can you hold back the movements of the stars? Are you able to restrain the Pleiades or Orion?
Job38:32 Can you ensure the proper sequence of the seasons or guide the constellation of the Bear with her cubs across the heavens?
Job38:33 Do you know the laws of the universe and how God rules the earth?
Job38:34 Can you shout to the clouds and make it rain?
Job38:35 Can you make lightning appear and cause it to strike as you direct it?
Job38:36 Who gives intuition and instinct?
Job38:37 Who is wise enough to count all the clouds? Who can tilt the water jars of heaven
Job38:38 turning the dry dust to clumps of mud?
Job38:39 Can you stalk prey for a lioness and satisfy the young lions' appetites
Job38:40 as they lie in their dens or crouch in the thicket?
Job38:41 Who provides food for the ravens when their young cry out to God as they wander about in hunger?
Job39:1 Do you know when the mountain goats give birth? Have you watched as the wild deer are born?
Job39:2 Do you know how many months they carry their young? Are you aware of the time of their delivery?
Job39:3 They crouch down to give birth to their young and deliver their offspring.
Job39:4 Their young grow up in the open fields
Job39:5 Who makes the wild donkey wild?
Job39:6 I have placed it in the wilderness; its home is the wasteland.
Job39:7 It hates the noise of the city
Job39:8 The mountains are its pastureland
Job39:9 Will the wild ox consent to being tamed? Will it stay in your stall?
Job39:10 Can you hitch a wild ox to a plow? Will it plow a field for you?
Job39:11 Since it is so strong
Job39:12 Can you rely on it to return
Job39:13 The ostrich flaps her wings grandly
Job39:14 She lays her eggs on top of the earth
Job39:15 She doesn't worry that a foot might crush them or that wild animals might destroy them.
Job39:16 She is harsh toward her young
Job39:17 for God has deprived her of wisdom. He has given her no understanding.
Job39:18 But whenever she jumps up to run
Job39:19 Have you given the horse its strength or clothed its neck with a flowing mane?
Job39:20 Did you give it the ability to leap forward like a locust? Its majestic snorting is something to hear!
Job39:21 It paws the earth and rejoices in its strength. When it charges to war
Job39:22 it is unafraid. It does not run from the sword.
Job39:23 The arrows rattle against it
Job39:24 Fiercely it paws the ground and rushes forward into battle when the trumpet blows.
Job39:25 It snorts at the sound of the bugle. It senses the battle even at a distance. It quivers at the noise of battle and the shout of the captain's commands.
Job39:26 Are you the one who makes the hawk soar and spread its wings to the south?
Job39:27 Is it at your command that the eagle rises to the heights to make its nest?
Job39:28 It lives on the cliffs
Job39:29 From there it hunts its prey
Job39:30 Its nestlings gulp down blood
Job40:1 Then the LORD said to Job
Job40:2 Do you still want to argue with the Almighty? You are God's critic
Job40:3 Then Job replied to the LORD
Job40:4 I am nothing--how could I ever find the answers? I will put my hand over my mouth in silence.
Job40:5 I have said too much already. I have nothing more to say.
Job40:6 Then the LORD answered Job from the whirlwind:
Job40:7 Brace yourself
Job40:8 Are you going to discredit my justice and condemn me so you can say you are right?
Job40:9 Are you as strong as God
Job40:10 All right then
Job40:11 Give vent to your anger. Let it overflow against the proud.
Job40:12 Humiliate the proud with a glance; walk on the wicked where they stand.
Job40:13 Bury them in the dust. Imprison them in the world of the dead.
Job40:14 Then even I would praise you
Job40:15 Take a look at the mighty hippopotamus. I made it
Job40:16 See its powerful loins and the muscles of its belly.
Job40:17 Its tail is as straight as a cedar. The sinews of its thighs are tightly knit together.
Job40:18 Its bones are tubes of bronze. Its limbs are bars of iron.
Job40:19 It is a prime example of God's amazing handiwork. Only its Creator can threaten it.
Job40:20 The mountains offer it their best food
Job40:21 It lies down under the lotus plants
Job40:22 The lotus plants give it shade among the willows beside the stream.
Job40:23 It is not disturbed by raging rivers
Job40:24 No one can catch it off guard or put a ring in its nose and lead it away.
Job41:1 Can you catch a crocodile with a hook or put a noose around its jaw?
Job41:2 Can you tie it with a rope through the nose or pierce its jaw with a spike?
Job41:3 Will it beg you for mercy or implore you for pity?
Job41:4 Will it agree to work for you? Can you make it be your slave for life?
Job41:5 Can you make it a pet like a bird
Job41:6 Will merchants try to buy it? Will they sell it in their shops?
Job41:7 Will its hide be hurt by darts
Job41:8 If you lay a hand on it
Job41:9 No
Job41:10 And since no one dares to disturb the crocodile
Job41:11 Who will confront me and remain safe? Everything under heaven is mine.
Job41:12 I want to emphasize the tremendous strength in the crocodile's limbs and throughout its enormous frame.
Job41:13 Who can strip off its hide
Job41:14 Who could pry open its jaws? For its teeth are terrible!
Job41:15 The overlapping scales on its back make a shield.
Job41:16 They are close together so no air can get between them.
Job41:17 They lock together so nothing can penetrate them.
Job41:18 When it sneezes
Job41:19 Fire and sparks leap from its mouth.
Job41:20 Smoke streams from its nostrils like steam from a boiling pot on a fire of dry rushes.
Job41:21 Yes
Job41:22 The tremendous strength in its neck strikes terror wherever it goes.
Job41:23 Its flesh is hard and firm
Job41:24 Its heart is as hard as rock
Job41:25 When it rises
Job41:26 No sword can stop it
Job41:27 To the crocodile
Job41:28 Arrows cannot make it flee. Stones shot from a sling are as ineffective as straw.
Job41:29 Clubs do no good
Job41:30 Its belly is covered with scales as sharp as glass. They tear up the ground as it drags through the mud.
Job41:31 The crocodile makes the water boil with its commotion. It churns the depths.
Job41:32 The water glistens in its wake. One would think the sea had turned white.
Job41:33 There is nothing else so fearless anywhere on earth.
Job41:34 Of all the creatures
Job42:1 Then Job replied to the LORD:
Job42:2 I know that you can do anything
Job42:3 You ask
Job42:4 You said
Job42:5 I had heard about you before
Job42:6 I take back everything I said
Job42:7 After the LORD had finished speaking to Job
Job42:8 Now take seven young bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and offer a burnt offering for yourselves. My servant Job will pray for you
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite
Job42:10 When Job prayed for his friends
Job42:11 Then all his brothers
Job42:12 So the LORD blessed Job in the second half of his life even more than in the beginning. For now he had fourteen thousand sheep
Job42:13 He also gave Job seven more sons and three more daughters.
Job42:14 He named his first daughter Jemimah
Job42:15 In all the land there were no other women as lovely as the daughters of Job. And their father put them into his will along with their brothers.
Job42:16 Job lived 140 years after that
Job42:17 Then he died

Psalms19:1 The heavens tell of the glory of God. The skies display his marvelous craftsmanship.
Psalms19:2 Day after day they continue to speak; night after night they make him known.
Psalms19:3 They speak without a sound or a word; their voice is silent in the skies;
Psalms19:4 yet their message has gone out to all the earth
Psalms19:5 It bursts forth like a radiant bridegroom after his wedding. It rejoices like a great athlete eager to run the race.
Psalms19:6 The sun rises at one end of the heavens and follows its course to the other end. Nothing can hide from its heat.
Psalms19:7 The law of the LORD is perfect
Psalms19:8 The commandments of the LORD are right
Psalms19:9 Reverence for the LORD is pure
Psalms19:10 They are more desirable than gold
Psalms19:11 They are a warning to those who hear them; there is great reward for those who obey them.
Psalms19:12 How can I know all the sins lurking in my heart? Cleanse me from these hidden faults.
Psalms19:13 Keep me from deliberate sins! Don't let them control me. Then I will be free of guilt and innocent of great sin.
Psalms19:14 May the words of my mouth and the thoughts of my heart be pleasing to you
Psalms20:1 In times of trouble
Psalms20:2 May he send you help from his sanctuary and strengthen you from Jerusalem.
Psalms20:3 May he remember all your gifts and look favorably on your burnt offerings.
Psalms20:4 May he grant your heart's desire and fulfill all your plans.
Psalms20:5 May we shout for joy when we hear of your victory
Psalms20:6 Now I know that the LORD saves his anointed king. He will answer him from his holy heaven and rescue him by his great power.
Psalms20:7 Some nations boast of their armies and weapons
Psalms20:8 Those nations will fall down and collapse
Psalms20:9 Give victory to our king
Psalms21:1 How the king rejoices in your strength
Psalms21:2 For you have given him his heart's desire; you have held back nothing that he requested.
Psalms21:3 You welcomed him back with success and prosperity. You placed a crown of finest gold on his head.
Psalms21:4 He asked you to preserve his life
Psalms21:5 Your victory brings him great honor
Psalms21:6 You have endowed him with eternal blessings. You have given him the joy of being in your presence.
Psalms21:7 For the king trusts in the LORD. The unfailing love of the Most High will keep him from stumbling.
Psalms21:8 You will capture all your enemies. Your strong right hand will seize all those who hate you.
Psalms21:9 You will destroy them as in a flaming furnace when you appear. The LORD will consume them in his anger; fire will devour them.
Psalms21:10 You will wipe their children from the face of the earth; they will never have descendants.
Psalms21:11 Although they plot against you
Psalms21:12 For they will turn and run when they see your arrows aimed at them.
Psalms21:13 We praise you
Psalms22:1 My God
Psalms22:2 Every day I call to you
Psalms22:3 Yet you are holy. The praises of Israel surround your throne.
Psalms22:4 Our ancestors trusted in you
Psalms22:5 You heard their cries for help and saved them. They put their trust in you and were never disappointed.
Psalms22:6 But I am a worm and not a man. I am scorned and despised by all!
Psalms22:7 Everyone who sees me mocks me. They sneer and shake their heads
Psalms22:8 Is this the one who relies on the LORD? Then let the LORD save him! If the LORD loves him so much
Psalms22:9 Yet you brought me safely from my mother's womb and led me to trust you when I was a nursing infant.
Psalms22:10 I was thrust upon you at my birth. You have been my God from the moment I was born.
Psalms22:11 Do not stay so far from me
Psalms22:12 My enemies surround me like a herd of bulls; fierce bulls of Bashan have hemmed me in!
Psalms22:13 Like roaring lions attacking their prey
Psalms22:14 My life is poured out like water
Psalms22:15 My strength has dried up like sunbaked clay. My tongue sticks to the roof of my mouth. You have laid me in the dust and left me for dead.
Psalms22:16 My enemies surround me like a pack of dogs; an evil gang closes in on me. They have pierced my hands and feet.
Psalms22:17 I can count every bone in my body. My enemies stare at me and gloat.
Psalms22:18 They divide my clothes among themselves and throw dice for my garments.
Psalms22:19 O LORD
Psalms22:20 Rescue me from a violent death; spare my precious life from these dogs.
Psalms22:21 Snatch me from the lions' jaws
Psalms22:22 Then I will declare the wonder of your name to my brothers and sisters. I will praise you among all your people.
Psalms22:23 Praise the LORD
Psalms22:24 For he has not ignored the suffering of the needy. He has not turned and walked away. He has listened to their cries for help.
Psalms22:25 I will praise you among all the people; I will fulfill my vows in the presence of those who worship you.
Psalms22:26 The poor will eat and be satisfied. All who seek the LORD will praise him. Their hearts will rejoice with everlasting joy.
Psalms22:27 The whole earth will acknowledge the LORD and return to him. People from every nation will bow down before him.
Psalms22:28 For the LORD is king! He rules all the nations.
Psalms22:29 Let the rich of the earth feast and worship. Let all mortals--those born to die--bow down in his presence.
Psalms22:30 Future generations will also serve him. Our children will hear about the wonders of the Lord.
Psalms22:31 His righteous acts will be told to those yet unborn. They will hear about everything he has done.

Psalms23:2 He lets me rest in green meadows; he leads me beside peaceful streams.
Psalms23:3 He renews my strength. He guides me along right paths
Psalms23:4 Even when I walk through the dark valley of death
Psalms23:5 You prepare a feast for me in the presence of my enemies. You welcome me as a guest
Psalms23:6 Surely your goodness and unfailing love will pursue me all the days of my life

Psalms26:2 Put me on trial
Psalms26:3 For I am constantly aware of your unfailing love
Psalms26:4 I do not spend time with liars or go along with hypocrites.
Psalms26:5 I hate the gatherings of those who do evil
Psalms26:6 I wash my hands to declare my innocence. I come to your altar
Psalms26:7 singing a song of thanksgiving and telling of all your miracles.

Psalms27:1 The LORD is my light and my salvation--so why should I be afraid? The LORD protects me from danger--so why should I tremble?
Psalms27:2 When evil people come to destroy me
Psalms27:3 Though a mighty army surrounds me
Psalms27:4 The one thing I ask of the LORD--the thing I seek most--is to live in the house of the LORD all the days of my life
Psalms27:5 For he will conceal me there when troubles come; he will hide me in his sanctuary. He will place me out of reach on a high rock.
Psalms27:6 Then I will hold my head high
Psalms27:7 Listen to my pleading
Psalms27:8 My heart has heard you say
Psalms27:9 Do not hide yourself from me. Do not reject your servant in anger. You have always been my helper. Don't leave me now; don't abandon me
Psalms27:10 Even if my father and mother abandon me
Psalms27:11 Teach me how to live
Psalms27:12 Do not let me fall into their hands. For they accuse me of things I've never done and breathe out violence against me.
Psalms27:13 Yet I am confident that I will see the LORD's goodness while I am here in the land of the living.
Psalms27:14 Wait patiently for the LORD. Be brave and courageous. Yes

Psalms28:2 Listen to my prayer for mercy as I cry out to you for help
Psalms28:3 Don't drag me away with the wicked--with those who do evil--those who speak friendly words to their neighbors while planning evil in their hearts.

Psalms28:5 They care nothing for what the LORD has done or for what his hands have made. So he will tear them down like old buildings

Psalms29:10 The LORD rules over the floodwaters. The LORD reigns as king forever.
Psalms29:11 The LORD gives his people strength. The LORD blesses them with peace.

Psalms44:1 O God
Psalms44:2 You drove out the pagan nations and gave all the land to our ancestors; you crushed their enemies
Psalms44:3 They did not conquer the land with their swords; it was not their own strength that gave them victory. It was by your mighty power that they succeeded; it was because you favored them and smiled on them.
Psalms44:4 You are my King and my God. You command victories for your people.
Psalms44:5 Only by your power can we push back our enemies; only in your name can we trample our foes.
Psalms44:6 I do not trust my bow; I do not count on my sword to save me.
Psalms44:7 It is you who gives us victory over our enemies; it is you who humbles those who hate us.
Psalms44:8 O God
Psalms44:9 But now you have tossed us aside in dishonor. You no longer lead our armies to battle.
Psalms44:10 You make us retreat from our enemies and allow them to plunder our land.
Psalms44:11 You have treated us like sheep waiting to be slaughtered; you have scattered us among the nations.
Psalms44:12 You sold us--your precious people--for a pittance. You valued us at nothing at all.
Psalms44:13 You have caused all our neighbors to mock us. We are an object of scorn and derision to the nations around us.
Psalms44:14 You have made us the butt of their jokes; we are scorned by the whole world.
Psalms44:15 We can't escape the constant humiliation; shame is written across our faces.
Psalms44:16 All we hear are the taunts of our mockers. All we see are our vengeful enemies.
Psalms44:17 All this has happened despite our loyalty to you. We have not violated your covenant.
Psalms44:18 Our hearts have not deserted you. We have not strayed from your path.
Psalms44:19 Yet you have crushed us in the desert. You have covered us with darkness and death.
Psalms44:20 If we had turned away from worshiping our God or spread our hands in prayer to foreign gods
Psalms44:21 God would surely have known it
Psalms44:22 For your sake we are killed every day; we are being slaughtered like sheep.

Psalms44:24 Why do you look the other way? Why do you ignore our suffering and oppression?

Psalms72:1 Give justice to the king
Psalms72:2 Help him judge your people in the right way; let the poor always be treated fairly.
Psalms72:3 May the mountains yield prosperity for all
Psalms72:4 Help him to defend the poor
Psalms72:5 May he live as long as the sun shines
Psalms72:6 May his reign be as refreshing as the springtime rains--like the showers that water the earth.
Psalms72:7 May all the godly flourish during his reign. May there be abundant prosperity until the end of time.
Psalms72:8 May he reign from sea to sea
Psalms72:9 Desert nomads will bow before him; his enemies will fall before him in the dust.
Psalms72:10 The western kings of Tarshish and the islands will bring him tribute. The eastern kings of Sheba and Seba will bring him gifts.
Psalms72:11 All kings will bow before him
Psalms72:12 He will rescue the poor when they cry to him; he will help the oppressed
Psalms72:13 He feels pity for the weak and the needy
Psalms72:14 He will save them from oppression and from violence
Psalms72:15 Long live the king! May the gold of Sheba be given to him. May the people always pray for him and bless him all day long.
Psalms72:16 May there be abundant crops throughout the land
Psalms72:17 May the king's name endure forever; may it continue as long as the sun shines. May all nations be blessed through him and bring him praise.
Psalms72:18 Bless the LORD God
Psalms72:19 Bless his glorious name forever! Let the whole earth be filled with his glory. Amen and amen!
Psalms72:20 (This ends the prayers of David son of Jesse.)
Psalms73:1 Truly God is good to Israel
Psalms73:2 But as for me
Psalms73:3 For I envied the proud when I saw them prosper despite their wickedness.
Psalms73:4 They seem to live such a painless life; their bodies are so healthy and strong.
Psalms73:5 They aren't troubled like other people or plagued with problems like everyone else.
Psalms73:6 They wear pride like a jeweled necklace
Psalms73:7 These fat cats have everything their hearts could ever wish for!
Psalms73:8 They scoff and speak only evil; in their pride they seek to crush others.
Psalms73:9 They boast against the very heavens
Psalms73:10 And so the people are dismayed and confused
Psalms73:11 Does God realize what is going on? they ask. Is the Most High even aware of what is happening?
Psalms73:12 Look at these arrogant people--enjoying a life of ease while their riches multiply.
Psalms73:13 Was it for nothing that I kept my heart pure and kept myself from doing wrong?
Psalms73:14 All I get is trouble all day long; every morning brings me pain.

Psalms74:12 You

Psalms74:15 You caused the springs and streams to gush forth

Psalms78:1 O my people
Psalms78:2 for I will speak to you in a parable. I will teach you hidden lessons from our past--
Psalms78:3 stories we have heard and know
Psalms78:4 We will not hide these truths from our children but will tell the next generation about the glorious deeds of the LORD. We will tell of his power and the mighty miracles he did.
Psalms78:5 For he issued his decree to Jacob; he gave his law to Israel. He commanded our ancestors to teach them to their children
Psalms78:6 so the next generation might know them--even the children not yet born--that they in turn might teach their children.
Psalms78:7 So each generation can set its hope anew on God
Psalms78:8 Then they will not be like their ancestors--stubborn
Psalms78:9 The warriors of Ephraim
Psalms78:10 They did not keep God's covenant
Psalms78:11 They forgot what he had done--the wonderful miracles he had shown them
Psalms78:12 the miracles he did for their ancestors in Egypt
Psalms78:13 For he divided the sea before them and led them through! The water stood up like walls beside them!
Psalms78:14 In the daytime he led them by a cloud
Psalms78:15 He split open the rocks in the wilderness to give them plenty of water
Psalms78:16 He made streams pour from the rock
Psalms78:17 Yet they kept on with their sin
Psalms78:18 They willfully tested God in their hearts
Psalms78:19 They even spoke against God himself
Psalms78:20 Yes
Psalms78:21 When the LORD heard them
Psalms78:22 for they did not believe God or trust him to care for them.
Psalms78:23 But he commanded the skies to open--he opened the doors of heaven--
Psalms78:24 and rained down manna for them to eat. He gave them bread from heaven.
Psalms78:25 They ate the food of angels! God gave them all they could hold.
Psalms78:26 He released the east wind in the heavens and guided the south wind by his mighty power.
Psalms78:27 He rained down meat as thick as dust--birds as plentiful as the sands along the seashore!
Psalms78:28 He caused the birds to fall within their camp and all around their tents.
Psalms78:29 The people ate their fill. He gave them what they wanted.
Psalms78:30 But before they finished eating this food they had craved
Psalms78:31 the anger of God rose against them
Psalms78:32 But in spite of this
Psalms78:33 So he ended their lives in failure and gave them years of terror.
Psalms78:34 When God killed some of them
Psalms78:35 Then they remembered that God was their rock
Psalms78:36 But they followed him only with their words; they lied to him with their tongues.
Psalms78:37 Their hearts were not loyal to him. They did not keep his covenant.
Psalms78:38 Yet he was merciful and forgave their sins and didn't destroy them all. Many a time he held back his anger and did not unleash his fury!
Psalms78:39 For he remembered that they were merely mortal
Psalms78:40 Oh
Psalms78:41 Again and again they tested God's patience and frustrated the Holy One of Israel.
Psalms78:42 They forgot about his power and how he rescued them from their enemies.
Psalms78:43 They forgot his miraculous signs in Egypt
Psalms78:44 For he turned their rivers into blood
Psalms78:45 He sent vast swarms of flies to consume them and hordes of frogs to ruin them.
Psalms78:46 He gave their crops to caterpillars; their harvest was consumed by locusts.
Psalms78:47 He destroyed their grapevines with hail and shattered their sycamores with sleet.
Psalms78:48 He abandoned their cattle to the hail
Psalms78:49 He loosed on them his fierce anger--all his fury
Psalms78:50 He turned his anger against them; he did not spare the Egyptians' lives but handed them over to the plague.
Psalms78:51 He killed the oldest son in each Egyptian family
Psalms78:52 But he led his own people like a flock of sheep
Psalms78:53 He kept them safe so they were not afraid; but the sea closed in upon their enemies.
Psalms78:54 He brought them to the border of his holy land
Psalms78:55 He drove out the nations before them; he gave them their inheritance by lot. He settled the tribes of Israel into their homes.
Psalms78:56 Yet though he did all this for them
Psalms78:57 They turned back and were as faithless as their parents had been. They were as useless as a crooked bow.
Psalms78:58 They made God angry by building altars to other gods; they made him jealous with their idols.
Psalms78:59 When God heard them
Psalms78:60 Then he abandoned his dwelling at Shiloh
Psalms78:61 He allowed the Ark of his might to be captured; he surrendered his glory into enemy hands.
Psalms78:62 He gave his people over to be butchered by the sword
Psalms78:63 Their young men were killed by fire; their young women died before singing their wedding songs.
Psalms78:64 Their priests were slaughtered
Psalms78:65 Then the Lord rose up as though waking from sleep
Psalms78:66 He routed his enemies and sent them to eternal shame.
Psalms78:67 But he rejected Joseph's descendants; he did not choose the tribe of Ephraim.
Psalms78:68 He chose instead the tribe of Judah
Psalms78:69 There he built his towering sanctuary
Psalms78:70 He chose his servant David
Psalms78:71 He took David from tending the ewes and lambs and made him the shepherd of Jacob's descendants--God's own people
Psalms78:72 He cared for them with a true heart and led them with skillful hands.

Psalms90:2 Before the mountains were created
Psalms90:3 You turn people back to dust
Psalms90:4 For you
Psalms90:5 You sweep people away like dreams that disappear or like grass that springs up in the morning.
Psalms90:6 In the morning it blooms and flourishes
Psalms90:7 We wither beneath your anger; we are overwhelmed by your fury.
Psalms90:8 You spread out our sins before you--our secret sins--and you see them all.
Psalms90:9 We live our lives beneath your wrath. We end our lives with a groan.
Psalms90:10 Seventy years are given to us! Some may even reach eighty. But even the best of these years are filled with pain and trouble; soon they disappear
Psalms90:11 Who can comprehend the power of your anger? Your wrath is as awesome as the fear you deserve.
Psalms90:12 Teach us to make the most of our time
Psalms90:13 O LORD
Psalms90:14 Satisfy us in the morning with your unfailing love

Psalms139:1 O LORD
Psalms139:2 You know when I sit down or stand up. You know my every thought when far away.
Psalms139:3 You chart the path ahead of me and tell me where to stop and rest. Every moment you know where I am.
Psalms139:4 You know what I am going to say even before I say it
Psalms139:5 You both precede and follow me. You place your hand of blessing on my head.
Psalms139:6 Such knowledge is too wonderful for me
Psalms139:7 I can never escape from your spirit! I can never get away from your presence!
Psalms139:8 If I go up to heaven
Psalms139:9 If I ride the wings of the morning
Psalms139:10 even there your hand will guide me
Psalms139:11 I could ask the darkness to hide me and the light around me to become night--
Psalms139:12 but even in darkness I cannot hide from you. To you the night shines as bright as day. Darkness and light are both alike to you.
Psalms139:13 You made all the delicate
Psalms139:14 Thank you for making me so wonderfully complex! Your workmanship is marvelous--and how well I know it.
Psalms139:15 You watched me as I was being formed in utter seclusion
Psalms139:16 You saw me before I was born. Every day of my life was recorded in your book. Every moment was laid out before a single day had passed.
Psalms139:17 How precious are your thoughts about me
Psalms139:18 I can't even count them; they outnumber the grains of sand! And when I wake up in the morning

Psalms139:20 They blaspheme you; your enemies take your name in vain.

Psalms139:22 Yes
Psalms139:23 Search me
Psalms139:24 Point out anything in me that offends you

Proverbs1:1 These are the proverbs of Solomon
Proverbs1:2 The purpose of these proverbs is to teach people wisdom and discipline
Proverbs1:3 Through these proverbs
Proverbs1:4 These proverbs will make the simpleminded clever. They will give knowledge and purpose to young people.
Proverbs1:5 Let those who are wise listen to these proverbs and become even wiser. And let those who understand receive guidance
Proverbs1:6 by exploring the depth of meaning in these proverbs
Proverbs1:7 Fear of the LORD is the beginning of knowledge. Only fools despise wisdom and discipline.
Proverbs1:8 Listen
Proverbs1:9 What you learn from them will crown you with grace and clothe you with honor.
Proverbs1:10 My child
Proverbs1:11 They may say
Proverbs1:12 Let's swallow them alive as the grave swallows its victims. Though they are in the prime of life
Proverbs1:13 And the loot we'll get! We'll fill our houses with all kinds of things!
Proverbs1:14 Come on
Proverbs1:15 Don't go along with them
Proverbs1:16 They rush to commit crimes. They hurry to commit murder.
Proverbs1:17 When a bird sees a trap being set
Proverbs1:18 But not these people! They set an ambush for themselves; they booby-trap their own lives!
Proverbs1:19 Such is the fate of all who are greedy for gain. It ends up robbing them of life.
Proverbs1:20 Wisdom shouts in the streets. She cries out in the public square.
Proverbs1:21 She calls out to the crowds along the main street
Proverbs1:22 You simpletons! she cries. How long will you go on being simpleminded? How long will you mockers relish your mocking? How long will you fools fight the facts?
Proverbs1:23 Come here and listen to me! I'll pour out the spirit of wisdom upon you and make you wise.
Proverbs1:24 I called you so often
Proverbs1:25 You ignored my advice and rejected the correction I offered.
Proverbs1:26 So I will laugh when you are in trouble! I will mock you when disaster overtakes you--
Proverbs1:27 when calamity overcomes you like a storm
Proverbs1:28 I will not answer when they cry for help. Even though they anxiously search for me
Proverbs1:29 For they hated knowledge and chose not to fear the LORD.
Proverbs1:30 They rejected my advice and paid no attention when I corrected them.
Proverbs1:31 That is why they must eat the bitter fruit of living their own way. They must experience the full terror of the path they have chosen.
Proverbs1:32 For they are simpletons who turn away from me--to death. They are fools
Proverbs1:33 But all who listen to me will live in peace and safety
Proverbs2:1 My child
Proverbs2:2 Tune your ears to wisdom
Proverbs2:3 Cry out for insight and understanding.
Proverbs2:4 Search for them as you would for lost money or hidden treasure.
Proverbs2:5 Then you will understand what it means to fear the LORD
Proverbs2:6 For the LORD grants wisdom! From his mouth come knowledge and understanding.
Proverbs2:7 He grants a treasure of good sense to the godly. He is their shield
Proverbs2:8 He guards the paths of justice and protects those who are faithful to him.
Proverbs2:9 Then you will understand what is right
Proverbs2:10 For wisdom will enter your heart
Proverbs2:11 Wise planning will watch over you. Understanding will keep you safe.
Proverbs2:12 Wisdom will save you from evil people
Proverbs2:13 These people turn from right ways to walk down dark and evil paths.
Proverbs2:14 They rejoice in doing wrong
Proverbs2:15 What they do is crooked
Proverbs2:16 Wisdom will save you from the immoral woman
Proverbs2:17 She has abandoned her husband and ignores the covenant she made before God.
Proverbs2:18 Entering her house leads to death; it is the road to hell.
Proverbs2:19 The man who visits her is doomed. He will never reach the paths of life.
Proverbs2:20 Follow the steps of good men instead
Proverbs2:21 For only the upright will live in the land
Proverbs2:22 But the wicked will be removed from the land

Proverbs3:26 for the LORD is your security. He will keep your foot from being caught in a trap.

Proverbs6:35 There is no compensation or bribe that will satisfy him.

Proverbs8:3 At the entrance to the city
Proverbs8:4 I call to you
Proverbs8:5 How naive you are! Let me give you common sense. O foolish ones
Proverbs8:6 Listen to me! For I have excellent things to tell you. Everything I say is right
Proverbs8:7 for I speak the truth and hate every kind of deception.
Proverbs8:8 My advice is wholesome and good. There is nothing crooked or twisted in it.
Proverbs8:9 My words are plain to anyone with understanding
Proverbs8:10 Choose my instruction rather than silver
Proverbs8:11 For wisdom is far more valuable than rubies. Nothing you desire can be compared with it.
Proverbs8:12 I
Proverbs8:13 All who fear the LORD will hate evil. That is why I hate pride
Proverbs8:14 Good advice and success belong to me. Insight and strength are mine.

Proverbs8:23 I was appointed in ages past

Proverbs8:25 Before the mountains and the hills were formed
Proverbs8:26 before he had made the earth and fields and the first handfuls of soil.

Proverbs8:28 I was there when he set the clouds above
Proverbs8:29 I was there when he set the limits of the seas

Proverbs8:31 And how happy I was with what he created--his wide world and all the human family!
Proverbs8:32 And so
Proverbs8:33 Listen to my counsel and be wise. Don't ignore it.
Proverbs8:34 Happy are those who listen to me
Proverbs8:35 For whoever finds me finds life and wins approval from the LORD.
Proverbs8:36 But those who miss me have injured themselves. All who hate me love death.
