Ecclesiastes1:1 These are the words of the Teacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 Everything is meaningless
Ecclesiastes1:3 What do people get for all their hard work?
Ecclesiastes1:4 Generations come and go
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun rises and sets and hurries around to rise again.
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind blows south and north
Ecclesiastes1:7 The rivers run into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 Everything is so weary and tiresome! No matter how much we see
Ecclesiastes1:9 History merely repeats itself. It has all been done before. Nothing under the sun is truly new.
Ecclesiastes1:10 What can you point to that is new? How do you know it didn't already exist long ago?
Ecclesiastes1:11 We don't remember what happened in those former times. And in future generations
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 I devoted myself to search for understanding and to explore by wisdom everything being done in the world. I soon discovered that God has dealt a tragic existence to the human race.
Ecclesiastes1:14 Everything under the sun is meaningless
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is wrong cannot be righted. What is missing cannot be recovered.
Ecclesiastes1:16 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes1:17 So I worked hard to distinguish wisdom from foolishness. But now I realize that even this was like chasing the wind.
Ecclesiastes1:18 For the greater my wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:1 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes2:2 It is silly to be laughing all the time
Ecclesiastes2:3 After much thought
Ecclesiastes2:4 I also tried to find meaning by building huge homes for myself and by planting beautiful vineyards.
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made gardens and parks
Ecclesiastes2:6 I built reservoirs to collect the water to irrigate my many flourishing groves.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought slaves
Ecclesiastes2:8 I collected great sums of silver and gold
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I became greater than any of the kings who ruled in Jerusalem before me. And with it all
Ecclesiastes2:10 Anything I wanted
Ecclesiastes2:11 But as I looked at everything I had worked so hard to accomplish
Ecclesiastes2:12 So I decided to compare wisdom and folly
Ecclesiastes2:13 Wisdom is of more value than foolishness
Ecclesiastes2:14 For the wise person sees
Ecclesiastes2:15 Both of them die. Just as the fool will die
Ecclesiastes2:16 For the wise person and the fool both die
Ecclesiastes2:17 So now I hate life because everything done here under the sun is so irrational. Everything is meaningless
Ecclesiastes2:18 I am disgusted that I must leave the fruits of my hard work to others.
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who can tell whether my successors will be wise or foolish? And yet they will control everything I have gained by my skill and hard work. How meaningless!
Ecclesiastes2:20 So I turned in despair from hard work. It was not the answer to my search for satisfaction in this life.
Ecclesiastes2:21 For though I do my work with wisdom
Ecclesiastes2:22 So what do people get for all their hard work?
Ecclesiastes2:23 Their days of labor are filled with pain and grief; even at night they cannot rest. It is all utterly meaningless.
Ecclesiastes2:24 So I decided there is nothing better than to enjoy food and drink and to find satisfaction in work. Then I realized that this pleasure is from the hand of God.
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who can eat or enjoy anything apart from him?
Ecclesiastes2:26 God gives wisdom
Ecclesiastes3:1 1 There is a time for everything
Ecclesiastes3:2 A time to be born and a time to die. A time to plant and a time to harvest.
Ecclesiastes3:3 A time to kill and a time to heal. A time to tear down and a time to rebuild.
Ecclesiastes3:4 A time to cry and a time to laugh. A time to grieve and a time to dance.
Ecclesiastes3:5 A time to scatter stones and a time to gather stones. A time to embrace and a time to turn away.
Ecclesiastes3:6 A time to search and a time to lose. A time to keep and a time to throw away.
Ecclesiastes3:7 A time to tear and a time to mend. A time to be quiet and a time to speak up.
Ecclesiastes3:8 A time to love and a time to hate. A time for war and a time for peace.
Ecclesiastes3:9 What do people really get for all their hard work?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have thought about this in connection with the various kinds of work God has given people to do.
Ecclesiastes3:11 God has made everything beautiful for its own time. He has planted eternity in the human heart
Ecclesiastes3:12 So I concluded that there is nothing better for people than to be happy and to enjoy themselves as long as they can.
Ecclesiastes3:13 And people should eat and drink and enjoy the fruits of their labor
Ecclesiastes3:14 And I know that whatever God does is final. Nothing can be added to it or taken from it. God's purpose in this is that people should fear him.
Ecclesiastes3:15 Whatever exists today and whatever will exist in the future has already existed in the past. For God calls each event back in its turn.
Ecclesiastes3:16 I also noticed that throughout the world there is evil in the courtroom. Yes
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said to myself
Ecclesiastes3:18 Then I realized that God allows people to continue in their sinful ways so he can test them. That way
Ecclesiastes3:19 For humans and animals both breathe the same air
Ecclesiastes3:20 Both go to the same place--the dust from which they came and to which they must return.
Ecclesiastes3:21 For who can prove that the human spirit goes upward and the spirit of animals goes downward into the earth?
Ecclesiastes3:22 So I saw that there is nothing better for people than to be happy in their work. That is why they are here! No one will bring them back from death to enjoy life in the future.
Ecclesiastes4:1 Again I observed all the oppression that takes place in our world. I saw the tears of the oppressed
Ecclesiastes4:2 So I concluded that the dead are better off than the living.
Ecclesiastes4:3 And most fortunate of all are those who were never born. For they have never seen all the evil that is done in our world.
Ecclesiastes4:4 Then I observed that most people are motivated to success by their envy of their neighbors. But this
Ecclesiastes4:5 Foolish people refuse to work and almost starve.
Ecclesiastes4:6 They feel it is better to be lazy and barely survive than to work hard
Ecclesiastes4:7 I observed yet another example of meaninglessness in our world.
Ecclesiastes4:8 This is the case of a man who is all alone
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two people can accomplish more than twice as much as one; they get a better return for their labor.
Ecclesiastes4:10 If one person falls
Ecclesiastes4:11 And on a cold night
Ecclesiastes4:12 A person standing alone can be attacked and defeated
Ecclesiastes4:13 It is better to be a poor but wise youth than to be an old and foolish king who refuses all advice.
Ecclesiastes4:14 Such a youth could come from prison and succeed. He might even become king
Ecclesiastes4:15 Everyone is eager to help such a youth
Ecclesiastes4:16 He might become the leader of millions and be very popular. But then the next generation grows up and rejects him! So again
Ecclesiastes5:1 As you enter the house of God
Ecclesiastes5:2 And don't make rash promises to God
Ecclesiastes5:3 Just as being too busy gives you nightmares
Ecclesiastes5:4 So when you make a promise to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 It is better to say nothing than to promise something that you don't follow through on.
Ecclesiastes5:6 In such cases
Ecclesiastes5:7 Dreaming all the time instead of working is foolishness. And there is ruin in a flood of empty words. Fear God instead.
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see a poor person being oppressed by the powerful and justice being miscarried throughout the land
Ecclesiastes5:9 Even the king milks the land for his own profit!
Ecclesiastes5:10 Those who love money will never have enough. How absurd to think that wealth brings true happiness!
Ecclesiastes5:11 The more you have
Ecclesiastes5:12 People who work hard sleep well
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is another serious problem I have seen in the world. Riches are sometimes hoarded to the harm of the saver
Ecclesiastes5:14 or they are put into risky investments that turn sour
Ecclesiastes5:15 People who live only for wealth come to the end of their lives as naked and empty-handed as on the day they were born.
Ecclesiastes5:16 And this
Ecclesiastes5:17 Throughout their lives
Ecclesiastes5:18 Even so
Ecclesiastes5:19 And it is a good thing to receive wealth from God and the good health to enjoy it. To enjoy your work and accept your lot in life--that is indeed a gift from God.
Ecclesiastes5:20 People who do this rarely look with sorrow on the past
Ecclesiastes6:1 There is another serious tragedy I have seen in our world.
Ecclesiastes6:2 God gives great wealth and honor to some people and gives them everything they could ever want
Ecclesiastes6:3 A man might have a hundred children and live to be very old. But if he finds no satisfaction in life and in the end does not even get a decent burial
Ecclesiastes6:4 I realize that his birth would have been meaningless and ended in darkness. He wouldn't even have had a name
Ecclesiastes6:5 and he would never have seen the sun or known of its existence. Yet he would have had more peace than he has in growing up to be an unhappy man.
Ecclesiastes6:6 He might live a thousand years twice over but not find contentment. And since he must die like everyone else--well
Ecclesiastes6:7 All people spend their lives scratching for food
Ecclesiastes6:8 Considering this
Ecclesiastes6:9 Enjoy what you have rather than desiring what you don't have. Just dreaming about nice things is meaningless; it is like chasing the wind.
Ecclesiastes6:10 Everything has already been decided. It was known long ago what each person would be. So there's no use arguing with God about your destiny.
Ecclesiastes6:11 The more words you speak
Ecclesiastes6:12 In the few days of our empty lives
Ecclesiastes7:1 A good reputation is more valuable than the most expensive perfume. In the same way
Ecclesiastes7:2 It is better to spend your time at funerals than at festivals. For you are going to die
Ecclesiastes7:3 Sorrow is better than laughter
Ecclesiastes7:4 A wise person thinks much about death
Ecclesiastes7:5 It is better to be criticized by a wise person than to be praised by a fool!
Ecclesiastes7:6 Indeed
Ecclesiastes7:7 Extortion turns wise people into fools
Ecclesiastes7:8 Finishing is better than starting. Patience is better than pride.
Ecclesiastes7:9 Don't be quick-tempered
Ecclesiastes7:10 Don't long for the good old days
Ecclesiastes7:11 Being wise is as good as being rich; in fact
Ecclesiastes7:12 Wisdom or money can get you almost anything
Ecclesiastes7:13 Notice the way God does things; then fall into line. Don't fight the ways of God
Ecclesiastes7:14 Enjoy prosperity while you can. But when hard times strike
Ecclesiastes7:15 In this meaningless life
Ecclesiastes7:16 So don't be too good or too wise! Why destroy yourself?
Ecclesiastes7:17 On the other hand
Ecclesiastes7:18 So try to walk a middle course--but those who fear God will succeed either way.
Ecclesiastes7:19 A wise person is stronger than the ten leading citizens of a town!
Ecclesiastes7:20 There is not a single person in all the earth who is always good and never sins.
Ecclesiastes7:21 Don't eavesdrop on others--you may hear your servant laughing at you.
Ecclesiastes7:22 For you know how often you yourself have laughed at others.
Ecclesiastes7:23 All along I have tried my best to let wisdom guide my thoughts and actions. I said to myself
Ecclesiastes7:24 Wisdom is always distant and very difficult to find.
Ecclesiastes7:25 I searched everywhere
Ecclesiastes7:26 I discovered that a seductive woman is more bitter than death. Her passion is a trap
Ecclesiastes7:27 This is my conclusion
Ecclesiastes7:28 Just one out of every thousand men I interviewed can be said to be upright
Ecclesiastes7:29 I discovered that God created people to be upright
Ecclesiastes8:1 How wonderful to be wise
Ecclesiastes8:2 Obey the king because you have vowed before God to do this.
Ecclesiastes8:3 Don't try to avoid doing your duty
Ecclesiastes8:4 The king's command is backed by great power. No one can resist or question it.
Ecclesiastes8:5 Those who obey him will not be punished. Those who are wise will find a time and a way to do what is right.
Ecclesiastes8:6 Yes
Ecclesiastes8:7 Indeed
Ecclesiastes8:8 None of us can hold back our spirit from departing. None of us has the power to prevent the day of our death. There is no escaping that obligation
Ecclesiastes8:9 I have thought deeply about all that goes on here in the world
Ecclesiastes8:10 I have seen wicked people buried with honor. How strange that they were the very ones who frequented the Temple and are praised in the very city where they committed their crimes!
Ecclesiastes8:11 When a crime is not punished
Ecclesiastes8:12 But even though a person sins a hundred times and still lives a long time
Ecclesiastes8:13 The wicked will never live long
Ecclesiastes8:14 And this is not all that is meaningless in our world. In this life
Ecclesiastes8:15 So I recommend having fun
Ecclesiastes8:16 In my search for wisdom
Ecclesiastes8:17 This reminded me that no one can discover everything God has created in our world
Ecclesiastes9:1 This
Ecclesiastes9:2 The same destiny ultimately awaits everyone
Ecclesiastes9:3 It seems so tragic that one fate comes to all. That is why people are not more careful to be good. Instead
Ecclesiastes9:4 There is hope only for the living. For as they say
Ecclesiastes9:5 The living at least know they will die
Ecclesiastes9:6 Whatever they did in their lifetime--loving
Ecclesiastes9:7 So go ahead. Eat your food and drink your wine with a happy heart
Ecclesiastes9:8 Wear fine clothes
Ecclesiastes9:9 Live happily with the woman you love through all the meaningless days of life that God has given you in this world. The wife God gives you is your reward for all your earthly toil.
Ecclesiastes9:10 Whatever you do
Ecclesiastes9:11 I have observed something else in this world of ours. The fastest runner doesn't always win the race
Ecclesiastes9:12 People can never predict when hard times might come. Like fish in a net or birds in a snare
Ecclesiastes9:13 Here is another bit of wisdom that has impressed me as I have watched the way our world works.
Ecclesiastes9:14 There was a small town with only a few people living in it
Ecclesiastes9:15 There was a poor
Ecclesiastes9:16 Then I realized that though wisdom is better than strength
Ecclesiastes9:17 But even so
Ecclesiastes9:18 A wise person can overcome weapons of war
Ecclesiastes10:1 Dead flies will cause even a bottle of perfume to stink! Yes
Ecclesiastes10:2 The hearts of the wise lead them to do right
Ecclesiastes10:3 You can identify fools just by the way they walk down the street!
Ecclesiastes10:4 If your boss is angry with you
Ecclesiastes10:5 There is another evil I have seen as I have watched the world go by. Kings and rulers make a grave mistake
Ecclesiastes10:6 if they give foolish people great authority
Ecclesiastes10:7 I have even seen servants riding like princes--and princes walking like servants.
Ecclesiastes10:8 When you dig a well
Ecclesiastes10:9 When you work in a quarry
Ecclesiastes10:10 Since a dull ax requires great strength
Ecclesiastes10:11 It does no good to charm a snake after it has bitten you.
Ecclesiastes10:12 It is pleasant to listen to wise words
Ecclesiastes10:13 Since fools base their thoughts on foolish premises
Ecclesiastes10:14 Foolish people claim to know all about the future and tell everyone the details! But who can really know what is going to happen?
Ecclesiastes10:15 Fools are so exhausted by a little work that they have no strength for even the simplest tasks.
Ecclesiastes10:16 Destruction is certain for the land whose king is a child and whose leaders feast in the morning.
Ecclesiastes10:17 Happy is the land whose king is a nobleman and whose leaders feast only to gain strength for their work
Ecclesiastes10:18 Laziness lets the roof leak
Ecclesiastes10:19 A party gives laughter
Ecclesiastes10:20 Never make light of the king
Ecclesiastes11:1 Give generously
Ecclesiastes11:2 Divide your gifts among many
Ecclesiastes11:3 When the clouds are heavy
Ecclesiastes11:4 If you wait for perfect conditions
Ecclesiastes11:5 God's ways are as hard to discern as the pathways of the wind
Ecclesiastes11:6 Be sure to stay busy and plant a variety of crops
Ecclesiastes11:7 Light is sweet; it's wonderful to see the sun!
Ecclesiastes11:8 When people live to be very old
Ecclesiastes11:9 Young man
Ecclesiastes11:10 So banish grief and pain

Ecclesiastes12:2 It will be too late then to remember him

Ecclesiastes12:4 And when your teeth are gone
Ecclesiastes12:5 You will be afraid of heights and of falling
Ecclesiastes12:6 Yes
Ecclesiastes12:7 For then the dust will return to the earth

Ecclesiastes12:10 Indeed

Ecclesiastes12:12 But
Ecclesiastes12:13 Here is my final conclusion: Fear God and obey his commands
Ecclesiastes12:14 God will judge us for everything we do

Isaiah1:1 These visions concerning Judah and Jerusalem came to Isaiah son of Amoz during the reigns of Uzziah
Isaiah1:2 Hear
Isaiah1:3 Even the animals--the donkey and the ox--know their owner and appreciate his care
Isaiah1:4 Oh
Isaiah1:5 Why do you continue to invite punishment? Must you rebel forever? Your head is injured
Isaiah1:6 You are sick from head to foot--covered with bruises
Isaiah1:7 Your country lies in ruins
Isaiah1:8 Jerusalem stands abandoned like a watchman's shelter in a vineyard or field after the harvest is over. It is as helpless as a city under siege.
Isaiah1:9 If the LORD Almighty had not spared a few of us
Isaiah1:10 Listen to the LORD
Isaiah1:11 I am sick of your sacrifices
Isaiah1:12 Why do you keep parading through my courts with your worthless sacrifices?
Isaiah1:13 The incense you bring me is a stench in my nostrils! Your celebrations of the new moon and the Sabbath day
Isaiah1:14 I hate all your festivals and sacrifices. I cannot stand the sight of them!
Isaiah1:15 From now on
Isaiah1:16 Wash yourselves and be clean! Let me no longer see your evil deeds. Give up your wicked ways.
Isaiah1:17 Learn to do good. Seek justice. Help the oppressed. Defend the orphan. Fight for the rights of widows.
Isaiah1:18 Come now
Isaiah1:19 If you will only obey me and let me help you
Isaiah1:20 But if you keep turning away and refusing to listen
Isaiah1:21 See how Jerusalem
Isaiah1:22 Once like pure silver
Isaiah1:23 Your leaders are rebels
Isaiah1:24 Therefore
Isaiah1:25 I will turn against you. I will melt you down and skim off your slag. I will remove all your impurities.
Isaiah1:26 Afterward I will give you good judges and wise counselors like the ones you used to have. Then Jerusalem will again be called the Home of Justice and the Faithful City.
Isaiah1:27 Because the LORD is just and righteous
Isaiah1:28 But all sinners will be completely destroyed
Isaiah1:29 Shame will cover you when you think of the times you offered sacrifices to idols in your groves of sacred oaks. You will blush when you think of all the sins you committed in your sacred gardens.
Isaiah1:30 You will wither away like an oak or garden without water.
Isaiah1:31 The strongest among you will disappear like burning straw. Your evil deeds are the spark that will set the straw on fire
Isaiah2:1 This is another vision that Isaiah son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem:
Isaiah2:2 In the last days
Isaiah2:3 Many nations will come and say
Isaiah2:4 The LORD will settle international disputes. All the nations will beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning hooks. All wars will stop
Isaiah2:5 Come
Isaiah2:6 The LORD has rejected the people of Israel because they have made alliances with foreigners from the East who practice magic and divination
Isaiah2:7 Israel has vast treasures of silver and gold and many horses and chariots.
Isaiah2:8 The land is filled with idols. The people bow down and worship these things they have made.
Isaiah2:9 So now everyone will be humbled and brought low. The LORD cannot simply ignore their sins!
Isaiah2:10 Crawl into caves in the rocks. Hide from the terror of the LORD and the glory of his majesty.
Isaiah2:11 The day is coming when your pride will be brought low and the LORD alone will be exalted.
Isaiah2:12 In that day the LORD Almighty will punish the proud
Isaiah2:13 He will cut down the tall cedars of Lebanon and the mighty oaks of Bashan.
Isaiah2:14 He will level the high mountains and hills.
Isaiah2:15 He will break down every high tower and wall.
Isaiah2:16 He will destroy the great trading ships and all the small boats in the harbor.
Isaiah2:17 The arrogance of all people will be brought low. Their pride will lie in the dust. The LORD alone will be exalted!
Isaiah2:18 Idols will be utterly abolished and destroyed.
Isaiah2:19 When the LORD rises to shake the earth
Isaiah2:20 They will abandon their gold and silver idols to the moles and bats.
Isaiah2:21 They will crawl into caverns and hide among the jagged rocks at the tops of cliffs. In this way
Isaiah2:22 Stop putting your trust in mere humans. They are as frail as breath. How can they be of help to anyone?

Isaiah5:2 He plowed the land
Isaiah5:3 Now
Isaiah5:4 What more could I have done to cultivate a rich harvest? Why did my vineyard give me wild grapes when I expected sweet ones?
Isaiah5:5 Now this is what I am going to do to my vineyard: I will tear down its fences and let it be destroyed. I will break down its walls and let the animals trample it.
Isaiah5:6 I will make it a wild place. I will not prune the vines or hoe the ground. I will let it be overgrown with briers and thorns. I will command the clouds to drop no more rain on it.
Isaiah5:7 This is the story of the LORD's people. They are the vineyard of the LORD Almighty. Israel and Judah are his pleasant garden. He expected them to yield a crop of justice
Isaiah5:8 Destruction is certain for you who buy up property so others have no place to live. Your homes are built on great estates so you can be alone in the land.
Isaiah5:9 But the LORD Almighty has sealed your awful fate. With my own ears I heard him say
Isaiah5:10 Ten acres of vineyard will not produce even six gallons of wine. Ten measures of seed will yield only one measure of grain.

Isaiah5:12 You furnish lovely music and wine at your grand parties; the harps

Isaiah5:15 In that day the arrogant will be brought down to the dust; the proud will be humbled.
Isaiah5:16 But the LORD Almighty is exalted by his justice. The holiness of God is displayed by his righteousness.
Isaiah5:17 In those days flocks will feed among the ruins; lambs and kids will pasture there.
Isaiah5:18 Destruction is certain for those who drag their sins behind them
Isaiah5:19 They even mock the Holy One of Israel and say
Isaiah5:20 Destruction is certain for those who say that evil is good and good is evil; that dark is light and light is dark; that bitter is sweet and sweet is bitter.
Isaiah5:21 Destruction is certain for those who think they are wise and consider themselves to be clever.
Isaiah5:22 Destruction is certain for those who are heroes when it comes to drinking
Isaiah5:23 They take bribes to pervert justice. They let the wicked go free while punishing the innocent.
Isaiah5:24 Therefore
Isaiah5:25 That is why the anger of the LORD burns against his people. That is why he has raised his fist to crush them. The hills tremble

Isaiah5:27 They will not get tired or stumble. They will run without stopping for rest or sleep. Not a belt will be loose
Isaiah5:28 Their arrows will be sharp and their bows ready for battle. Sparks will fly from their horses' hooves as the wheels of their chariots spin like the wind.
Isaiah5:29 Roaring like lions
Isaiah5:30 The enemy nations will growl over their victims like the roaring of the sea. A cloud of darkness and sorrow will hover over Israel. The clouds will blot out the light.

Jeremiah44:5 But my people would not listen or turn back from their wicked ways. They kept right on burning incense to these gods.

Jeremiah44:7 And now the LORD God Almighty
Jeremiah44:8 Why arouse my anger by burning incense to the idols you have made here in Egypt? You will only destroy yourselves and make yourselves an object of cursing and mockery for all the nations of the earth.
Jeremiah44:9 Have you forgotten the sins of your ancestors
Jeremiah44:10 To this very hour you have shown no remorse or reverence. No one has chosen to follow my law and the decrees I gave to you and your ancestors before you.
Jeremiah44:11 Therefore
Jeremiah44:12 I will take this remnant of Judah that insisted on coming here to Egypt
Jeremiah44:13 I will punish them in Egypt just as I punished them in Jerusalem

Lamentations1:1 Jerusalem's streets
Lamentations1:2 She sobs through the night; tears stream down her cheeks. Among all her lovers
Lamentations1:3 Judah has been led away into captivity
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Jerusalem are in mourning
Lamentations1:5 Her oppressors have become her masters
Lamentations1:6 All the beauty and majesty of Jerusalem are gone. Her princes are like starving deer searching for pasture
Lamentations1:7 And now in the midst of her sadness and wandering
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem has sinned greatly
Lamentations1:9 She defiled herself with immorality with no thought of the punishment that would follow. Now she lies in the gutter with no one to lift her out. LORD
Lamentations1:10 The enemy has plundered her completely
Lamentations1:11 Her people groan as they search for bread. They have sold their treasures for food to stay alive. O LORD
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 He has sent fire from heaven that burns in my bones. He has placed a trap in my path and turned me back. He has made me desolate
Lamentations1:14 He wove my sins into ropes to hitch me to a yoke of captivity. The Lord sapped my strength and gave me to my enemies; I am helpless in their hands.
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has treated my mighty men with contempt. At his command a great army has come to crush my young warriors. The Lord has trampled his beloved city as grapes are trampled in a winepress.
Lamentations1:16 For all these things I weep; tears flow down my cheeks. No one is here to comfort me; any who might encourage me are far away. My children have no future
Lamentations1:17 Jerusalem pleads for help
Lamentations1:18 And the LORD is right
Lamentations1:19 I begged my allies for help
Lamentations1:20 LORD
Lamentations1:21 Others heard my groans
Lamentations1:22 Look at all their evil deeds
Lamentations2:1 The Lord in his anger has cast a dark shadow over Jerusalem. The fairest of Israel's cities lies in the dust
Lamentations2:2 Without mercy the Lord has destroyed every home in Israel. In his anger he has broken down the fortress walls of Jerusalem. He has brought to dust the kingdom and all its rulers.
Lamentations2:3 All the strength of Israel vanishes beneath his fury. The Lord has withdrawn his protection as the enemy attacks. He consumes the whole land of Israel like a raging fire.
Lamentations2:4 He bends his bow against his people as though he were their enemy. His strength is used against them to kill their finest youth. His fury is poured out like fire on beautiful Jerusalem.
Lamentations2:5 Yes
Lamentations2:6 He has broken down his Temple as though it were merely a garden shelter. The LORD has blotted out all memory of the holy festivals and Sabbath days. Kings and priests fall together before his anger.
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has rejected his own altar; he despises his own sanctuary. He has given Jerusalem's palaces to her enemies. They shout in the LORD's Temple as though it were a day of celebration.
Lamentations2:8 The LORD was determined to destroy the walls of Jerusalem. He made careful plans for their destruction
Lamentations2:9 Jerusalem's gates have sunk into the ground. All their locks and bars are destroyed
Lamentations2:10 The leaders of Jerusalem sit on the ground in silence
Lamentations2:11 I have cried until the tears no longer come. My heart is broken
Lamentations2:12 Mama
Lamentations2:13 In all the world has there ever been such sorrow? O daughter of Jerusalem
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets have said so many foolish things
Lamentations2:15 All who pass by jeer at you. They scoff and insult Jerusalem
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies deride you. They scoff and grind their teeth and say
Lamentations2:17 But it is the LORD who did it just as he warned. He has fulfilled the promises of disaster he made long ago. He has destroyed Jerusalem without mercy and caused her enemies to rejoice over her and boast of their power.
Lamentations2:18 Cry aloud before the Lord
Lamentations2:19 Rise during the night and cry out. Pour out your hearts like water to the Lord. Lift up your hands to him in prayer. Plead for your children as they faint with hunger in the streets.
Lamentations2:20 O LORD
Lamentations2:21 See them lying in the streets--young and old
Lamentations2:22 You have invited terrors from all around as though you were calling them to a day of feasting. In the day of the LORD's anger
Lamentations3:1 I am the one who has seen the afflictions that come from the rod of the LORD's anger.
Lamentations3:2 He has brought me into deep darkness
Lamentations3:3 He has turned against me. Day and night his hand is heavy upon me.
Lamentations3:4 He has made my skin and flesh grow old. He has broken my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He has attacked me and surrounded me with anguish and distress.
Lamentations3:6 He has buried me in a dark place
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me in
Lamentations3:8 And though I cry and shout
Lamentations3:9 He has blocked my path with a high stone wall. He has twisted the road before me with many detours.
Lamentations3:10 He hid like a bear or a lion
Lamentations3:11 He dragged me off the path and tore me with his claws
Lamentations3:12 He bent his bow and aimed it squarely at me.
Lamentations3:13 He shot his arrows deep into my heart.
Lamentations3:14 My own people laugh at me. All day long they sing their mocking songs.
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness. He has given me a cup of deep sorrow to drink.
Lamentations3:16 He has made me grind my teeth on gravel. He has rolled me in the dust.
Lamentations3:17 Peace has been stripped away
Lamentations3:18 I cry out
Lamentations3:19 The thought of my suffering and homelessness is bitter beyond words.
Lamentations3:20 I will never forget this awful time
Lamentations3:21 Yet I still dare to hope when I remember this:
Lamentations3:22 The unfailing love of the LORD never ends! By his mercies we have been kept from complete destruction.
Lamentations3:23 Great is his faithfulness; his mercies begin afresh each day.
Lamentations3:24 I say to myself
Lamentations3:25 The LORD is wonderfully good to those who wait for him and seek him.
Lamentations3:26 So it is good to wait quietly for salvation from the LORD.
Lamentations3:27 And it is good for the young to submit to the yoke of his discipline.
Lamentations3:28 Let them sit alone in silence beneath the LORD's demands.
Lamentations3:29 Let them lie face down in the dust; then at last there is hope for them.
Lamentations3:30 Let them turn the other cheek to those who strike them. Let them accept the insults of their enemies.
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord does not abandon anyone forever.
Lamentations3:32 Though he brings grief
Lamentations3:33 For he does not enjoy hurting people or causing them sorrow.
Lamentations3:34 But the leaders of his people trampled prisoners underfoot.
Lamentations3:35 They deprived people of their God-given rights in defiance of the Most High.
Lamentations3:36 They perverted justice in the courts. Do they think the Lord didn't see it?
Lamentations3:37 Can anything happen without the Lord's permission?
Lamentations3:38 Is it not the Most High who helps one and harms another?
Lamentations3:39 Then why should we
Lamentations3:40 Instead
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift our hearts and hands to God in heaven and say
Lamentations3:42 We have sinned and rebelled
Lamentations3:43 You have engulfed us with your anger
Lamentations3:44 You have hidden yourself in a cloud so our prayers cannot reach you.
Lamentations3:45 You have discarded us as refuse and garbage among the nations.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have spoken out against us.
Lamentations3:47 We are filled with fear
Lamentations3:48 Streams of tears flow from my eyes because of the destruction of my people!
Lamentations3:49 My tears flow down endlessly. They will not stop
Lamentations3:50 until the LORD looks down from heaven and sees.
Lamentations3:51 My heart is breaking over the fate of all the women of Jerusalem.
Lamentations3:52 My enemies
Lamentations3:53 They threw me into a pit and dropped stones on me.
Lamentations3:54 The water flowed above my head
Lamentations3:55 But I called on your name
Lamentations3:56 and you heard me! You listened to my pleading; you heard my weeping!
Lamentations3:57 Yes
Lamentations3:58 Lord
Lamentations3:59 You have seen the wrong they have done to me
Lamentations3:60 You have seen the plots my enemies have laid against me.
Lamentations3:61 LORD
Lamentations3:62 the plots my enemies whisper and mutter against me all day long.
Lamentations3:63 Look at them! In all their activities
Lamentations3:64 Pay them back
Lamentations3:65 Give them hard and stubborn hearts
Lamentations3:66 Chase them down in your anger
Lamentations4:1 How the gold has lost its luster! Even the finest gold has become dull. The sacred gemstones lie scattered in the streets!
Lamentations4:2 See how the precious children of Jerusalem
Lamentations4:3 Even the jackals feed their young
Lamentations4:4 The parched tongues of their little ones stick with thirst to the roofs of their mouths. The children cry for bread
Lamentations4:5 The people who once ate only the richest foods now beg in the streets for anything they can get. Those who once lived in palaces now search the garbage pits for food.
Lamentations4:6 The guilt of my people is greater than that of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Our princes were once glowing with health; they were as clean as snow and as elegant as jewels.
Lamentations4:8 But now their faces are blacker than soot. No one even recognizes them. Their skin sticks to their bones; it is as dry and hard as wood.
Lamentations4:9 Those killed by the sword are far better off than those who die of hunger
Lamentations4:10 Tenderhearted women have cooked their own children and eaten them in order to survive the siege.
Lamentations4:11 But now the anger of the LORD is satisfied. His fiercest anger has now been poured out. He started a fire in Jerusalem that burned the city to its foundations.
Lamentations4:12 Not a king in all the earth--no one in all the world--would have believed an enemy could march through the gates of Jerusalem.
Lamentations4:13 Yet it happened because of the sins of her prophets and priests
Lamentations4:14 They wandered blindly through the streets
Lamentations4:15 Get away! the people shouted at them. You are defiled! Don't touch us! So they fled to distant lands and wandered there among foreign nations
Lamentations4:16 The LORD himself has scattered them
Lamentations4:17 We looked in vain for our allies to come and save us
Lamentations4:18 We couldn't go into the streets without danger to our lives. Our end was near; our days were numbered. We were doomed!
Lamentations4:19 Our enemies were swifter than the eagles. If we fled to the mountains
Lamentations4:20 Our king
Lamentations4:21 Are you rejoicing in the land of Uz

Lamentations5:1 LORD
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We are orphaned and fatherless. Our mothers are widowed.
Lamentations5:4 We have to pay for water to drink
Lamentations5:5 Those who pursue us are at our heels; we are exhausted but are given no rest.
Lamentations5:6 We submitted to Egypt and Assyria to get enough food to survive.
Lamentations5:7 It was our ancestors who sinned
Lamentations5:8 Slaves have now become our masters; there is no one left to rescue us.

Ezekiel1:1 On July 31 of my thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 This happened during the fifth year of King Jehoiachin's captivity.
Ezekiel1:3 The LORD gave a message to me
Ezekiel1:4 As I looked
Ezekiel1:5 From the center of the cloud came four living beings that looked human
Ezekiel1:6 except that each had four faces and two pairs of wings.
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight like human legs
Ezekiel1:8 Beneath each of their wings I could see human hands.
Ezekiel1:9 The wings of each living being touched the wings of the two beings beside it. The living beings were able to fly in any direction without turning around.
Ezekiel1:10 Each had a human face in the front
Ezekiel1:11 Each had two pairs of outstretched wings--one pair stretched out to touch the wings of the living beings on either side of it
Ezekiel1:12 They went in whatever direction the spirit chose
Ezekiel1:13 The living beings looked like bright coals of fire or brilliant torches
Ezekiel1:14 And the living beings darted to and fro like flashes of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 As I looked at these beings
Ezekiel1:16 The wheels sparkled as if made of chrysolite. All four wheels looked the same; each wheel had a second wheel turning crosswise within it.
Ezekiel1:17 The beings could move forward in any of the four directions they faced
Ezekiel1:18 The rims of the four wheels were awesomely tall
Ezekiel1:19 When the four living beings moved
Ezekiel1:20 The spirit of the four living beings was in the wheels. So wherever the spirit went
Ezekiel1:21 When the living beings moved
Ezekiel1:22 There was a surface spread out above them like the sky. It sparkled like crystal.
Ezekiel1:23 Beneath this surface the wings of each living being stretched out to touch the others' wings
Ezekiel1:24 As they flew their wings roared like waves crashing against the shore
Ezekiel1:25 As they stood with their wings lowered
Ezekiel1:26 Above the surface over their heads was what looked like a throne made of blue sapphire. And high above this throne was a figure whose appearance was like that of a man.
Ezekiel1:27 From his waist up
Ezekiel1:28 All around him was a glowing halo

Ezekiel26:12 They will plunder all your riches and merchandise and break down your walls. They will destroy your lovely homes and dump your stones and timbers and even your dust into the sea.
Ezekiel26:13 I will stop the music of your songs. No more will the sound of harps be heard among your people.
Ezekiel26:14 I will make your island a bare rock
Ezekiel26:15 This is what the Sovereign LORD says to Tyre: The whole coastline will tremble at the sound of your fall
Ezekiel26:16 All the seaport rulers will step down from their thrones and take off their royal robes and beautiful clothing. They will sit on the ground trembling with horror at what they have seen.
Ezekiel26:17 Then they will wail for you
Ezekiel26:18 Now the coastlands tremble at your fall. The islands are dismayed as you pass away.'
Ezekiel26:19 For the Sovereign LORD says: I will make Tyre an uninhabited ruin. You will sink beneath the terrible waves of enemy attack. Great seas will swallow you.
Ezekiel26:20 I will send you to the pit to lie there with those who descended there long ago. Your city will lie in ruins

Ezekiel44:7 You have brought uncircumcised foreigners into my sanctuary--people who have no heart for God. In this way
Ezekiel44:8 You have not kept the laws I gave you concerning these sacred rituals
Ezekiel44:9 So this is what the Sovereign LORD says: No foreigners
Ezekiel44:10 And the men of the tribe of Levi who abandoned me when Israel strayed away from me to worship idols must bear the consequences of their unfaithfulness.
Ezekiel44:11 They may still be Temple guards and gatemen
Ezekiel44:12 But they encouraged my people to worship other gods
Ezekiel44:13 They may not approach me to minister as priests. They may not touch any of my holy things or the holy offerings
Ezekiel44:14 They are to serve as the Temple caretakers and are relegated to doing maintenance work and helping the people in a general way.
Ezekiel44:15 However
Ezekiel44:16 They are the ones who will enter my sanctuary and approach my table to serve me. They are the ones who will fulfill all my requirements.
Ezekiel44:17 When they enter the gateway to the inner courtyard
Ezekiel44:18 They must wear linen turbans and linen undergarments. They must not wear anything that would cause them to perspire.
Ezekiel44:19 When they return to the outer courtyard where the people are
Ezekiel44:20 They must neither let their hair grow too long nor shave it off completely. Instead
Ezekiel44:21 The priests must never drink wine before entering the inner courtyard.
Ezekiel44:22 They may choose their wives only from among the virgins of Israel or the widows of the priests. They may not marry other widows or divorced women.
Ezekiel44:23 They will teach my people the difference between what is holy and what is common
Ezekiel44:24 They will serve as judges to resolve any disagreements among my people. Their decisions must be based on my regulations. And the priests themselves must obey my instructions and laws at all the sacred festivals
Ezekiel44:25 A priest must never defile himself by being in the presence of a dead person unless it is his father
Ezekiel44:26 But such a priest can only return to his Temple duties after being ritually cleansed and then waiting for seven days.
Ezekiel44:27 The first day he returns to work and enters the inner courtyard and the sanctuary
Ezekiel44:28 As to property
Ezekiel44:29 Their food will come from the gifts and sacrifices brought to the Temple by the people--the grain offerings
Ezekiel44:30 The first of the ripe fruits and all the gifts brought to the LORD will go to the priests. The first samples of each grain harvest and the first of your flour must also be given to the priests so the LORD will bless your homes.
Ezekiel44:31 The priests may never eat meat from any bird or animal that dies a natural death or that dies after being attacked by another animal.

Daniel1:8 But Daniel made up his mind not to defile himself by eating the food and wine given to them by the king. He asked the chief official for permission to eat other things instead.
Daniel1:9 Now God had given the chief official great respect for Daniel.
Daniel1:10 But he was alarmed by Daniel's suggestion. My lord the king has ordered that you eat this food and wine

Daniel1:15 At the end of the ten days

Daniel1:20 In all matters requiring wisdom and balanced judgment

Daniel2:20 saying

Daniel2:37 Your Majesty

Joel1:1 The LORD gave this message to Joel son of Pethuel.
Joel1:2 Hear this
Joel1:3 Tell your children about it in the years to come. Pass the awful story down from generation to generation.

Joel1:5 Wake up
Joel1:6 A vast army of locusts has invaded my land. It is a terrible army
Joel1:7 They have destroyed my grapevines and fig trees
Joel1:8 Weep with sorrow
Joel1:9 There is no grain or wine to offer at the Temple of the LORD. The priests are mourning because there are no offerings. Listen to the weeping of these ministers of the LORD!
Joel1:10 The fields are ruined and empty of crops. The grain
Joel1:11 Despair
Joel1:12 The grapevines and the fig trees have all withered. The pomegranate trees

Joel1:15 The day of the LORD is on the way
Joel1:16 We watch as our food disappears before our very eyes. There are no joyful celebrations in the house of our God.
Joel1:17 The seeds die in the parched ground
Joel1:18 How the animals moan with hunger! The cattle wander about confused because there is no pasture for them. The sheep bleat in misery.
Joel1:19 LORD
Joel1:20 Even the wild animals cry out to you because they have no water to drink. The streams have dried up
Joel2:1 Blow the trumpet in Jerusalem! Sound the alarm on my holy mountain! Let everyone tremble in fear because the day of the LORD is upon us.
Joel2:2 It is a day of darkness and gloom
Joel2:3 Fire burns in front of them and follows them in every direction! Ahead of them the land lies as fair as the Garden of Eden in all its beauty. Behind them is nothing but desolation; not one thing escapes.
Joel2:4 They look like tiny horses
Joel2:5 Look at them as they leap along the mountaintops! Listen to the noise they make--like the rumbling of chariots
Joel2:6 Fear grips all the people; every face grows pale with fright.
Joel2:7 The attackers march like warriors and scale city walls like trained soldiers. Straight forward they march
Joel2:8 They never jostle each other; each moves in exactly the right place. They lunge through the gaps
Joel2:9 They swarm over the city and run along its walls. They enter all the houses
Joel2:10 The earth quakes as they advance
Joel2:11 The LORD leads them with a shout! This is his mighty army
Joel2:12 That is why the LORD says
Joel2:13 Don't tear your clothing in your grief; instead
Joel2:14 Who knows? Perhaps even yet he will give you a reprieve

Amos2:9 Yet think of all I did for my people! I destroyed the Amorites before my people arrived in the land. The Amorites were as tall as cedar trees and strong as oaks

Obadiah1:1 This is the vision that the Sovereign LORD revealed to Obadiah concerning the land of Edom. We have heard a message from the LORD that an ambassador was sent to the nations to say
Obadiah1:2 The LORD says
Obadiah1:3 You are proud because you live in a rock fortress and make your home high in the mountains. `Who can ever reach us way up here?' you ask boastfully. Don't fool yourselves!
Obadiah1:4 Though you soar as high as eagles and build your nest among the stars
Obadiah1:5 If thieves came at night and robbed you
Obadiah1:6 Every nook and cranny of Edom will be searched and looted. Every treasure will be found and taken.
Obadiah1:7 All your allies will turn against you. They will help to chase you from your land. They will promise you peace
Obadiah1:8 At that time not a single wise person will be left in the whole land of Edom! says the LORD. For on the mountains of Edom I will destroy everyone who has wisdom and understanding.
Obadiah1:9 The mightiest warriors of Teman will be terrified
Obadiah1:10 And why? Because of the violence you did to your close relatives in Israel. Now you will be destroyed completely and filled with shame forever.
Obadiah1:11 For you deserted your relatives in Israel during their time of greatest need. You stood aloof
Obadiah1:12 You shouldn't have done this! You shouldn't have gloated when they exiled your relatives to distant lands. You shouldn't have rejoiced because they were suffering such misfortune. You shouldn't have crowed over them as they suffered these disasters.
Obadiah1:13 You shouldn't have plundered the land of Israel when they were suffering such calamity. You shouldn't have gloated over the destruction of your relatives
Obadiah1:14 You shouldn't have stood at the crossroads
Obadiah1:15 The day is near when I
Obadiah1:16 Just as you swallowed up my people on my holy mountain
Obadiah1:17 But Jerusalem will become a refuge for those who escape; it will be a holy place. And the people of Israel will come back to reclaim their inheritance.
Obadiah1:18 At that time Israel will be a raging fire
Obadiah1:19 Then my people living in the Negev will occupy the mountains of Edom. Those living in the foothills of Judah will possess the Philistine plains and take over the fields of Ephraim and Samaria. And the people of Benjamin will occupy the land of Gilead.
Obadiah1:20 The exiles of Israel will return to their land and occupy the Phoenician coast as far north as Zarephath. The captives from Jerusalem exiled in the north will return to their homeland and resettle the villages of the Negev.
Obadiah1:21 Deliverers will go up to Mount Zion in Jerusalem to rule over the mountains of Edom. And the LORD himself will be king!
Jonah1:1 The LORD gave this message to Jonah son of Amittai:
Jonah1:2 Get up and go to the great city of Nineveh! Announce my judgment against it because I have seen how wicked its people are.
Jonah1:3 But Jonah got up and went in the opposite direction in order to get away from the LORD. He went down to the seacoast
Jonah1:4 But as the ship was sailing along
Jonah1:5 Fearing for their lives
Jonah1:6 So the captain went down after him. How can you sleep at a time like this? he shouted. Get up and pray to your god! Maybe he will have mercy on us and spare our lives.
Jonah1:7 Then the crew cast lots to see which of them had offended the gods and caused the terrible storm. When they did this
Jonah1:8 What have you done to bring this awful storm down on us? they demanded. Who are you? What is your line of work? What country are you from? What is your nationality?
Jonah1:9 And Jonah answered
Jonah1:10 Then he told them that he was running away from the LORD. The sailors were terrified when they heard this. Oh

Jonah1:12 Throw me into the sea
Jonah1:13 Instead
Jonah1:14 Then they cried out to the LORD
Jonah1:15 Then the sailors picked Jonah up and threw him into the raging sea
Jonah1:16 The sailors were awestruck by the LORD's great power
Jonah1:17 Now the LORD had arranged for a great fish to swallow Jonah. And Jonah was inside the fish for three days and three nights.

Micah1:3 Look! The LORD is coming! He leaves his throne in heaven and comes to earth
Micah1:4 They melt beneath his feet and flow into the valleys like wax in a fire
Micah1:5 And why is this happening? Because of the sins and rebellion of Israel and Judah. Who is to blame for Israel's rebellion? Samaria
Micah1:6 So I
Micah1:7 All her carved images will be smashed to pieces. All her sacred treasures will be burned up. These things were bought with the money earned by her prostitution
Micah1:8 Because of all this
Micah1:9 For my people's wound is far too deep to heal. It has reached into Judah
Micah1:10 Don't tell our enemies in the city of Gath; don't weep at all. You people in Beth-leaphrah
Micah1:11 You people of Shaphir
Micah1:12 The people of Maroth anxiously wait for relief
Micah1:13 Quick! Use your swiftest chariots and flee
Micah1:14 Send a farewell gift to Moresheth-gath; there is no hope of saving it. The town of Aczib has deceived the kings of Israel
Micah1:15 You people of Mareshah
Micah1:16 Weep
Micah2:1 How terrible it will be for you who lie awake at night
Micah2:2 When you want a certain piece of land
Micah2:3 But this is what the LORD says: I will reward your evil with evil; you won't be able to escape! After I am through with you
Micah2:4 In that day your enemies will make fun of you by singing this song of despair about your experience: We are finished
Micah2:5 Others will set your boundaries then
Micah2:6 Don't say such things
Micah2:7 Should you talk that way
Micah2:8 Yet to this very hour my people rise against me! You steal the shirts right off the backs of those who trusted you
Micah2:9 You have evicted women from their homes and stripped their children of all their God-given rights.
Micah2:10 Up! Begone! This is no longer your land and home
Micah2:11 Suppose a prophet full of lies were to say to you
Micah2:12 Someday
Micah2:13 Your leader will break out and lead you out of exile. He will bring you through the gates of your cities of captivity
Micah3:1 Listen
Micah3:2 but you are the very ones who hate good and love evil. You skin my people alive and tear the flesh off their bones.
Micah3:3 You eat my people's flesh
Micah3:4 Then you beg the LORD for help in times of trouble! Do you really expect him to listen? After all the evil you have done
Micah3:5 This is what the LORD says to you false prophets: You are leading my people astray! You promise peace for those who give you food
Micah3:6 Now the night will close around you
Micah3:7 Then you seers will cover your faces in shame
Micah3:8 But as for me
Micah3:9 Listen to me
Micah3:10 You are building Jerusalem on a foundation of murder and corruption.
Micah3:11 You rulers govern for the bribes you can get; you priests teach God's laws only for a price; you prophets won't prophesy unless you are paid. Yet all of you claim you are depending on the LORD. No harm can come to us
Micah3:12 So because of you
Micah4:1 In the last days
Micah4:2 Many nations will come and say
Micah4:3 The LORD will settle international disputes. All the nations will beat their swords into plowshares and their spears into pruning hooks. All wars will stop
Micah4:4 Everyone will live quietly in their own homes in peace and prosperity
Micah4:5 Even though the nations around us worship idols
Micah4:6 In that coming day
Micah4:7 They are weak and far from home
Micah4:8 As for you
Micah4:9 But why are you now screaming in terror? Have you no king to lead you? He is dead! Have you no wise people to counsel you? All are gone! Pain has gripped you like it does a woman in labor.
Micah4:10 Writhe and groan in terrible pain
Micah4:11 True
Micah4:12 But they do not know the LORD's thoughts or understand his plan. These nations don't know that he is gathering them together to be beaten and trampled like bundles of grain on a threshing floor.
Micah4:13 Rise up and destroy the nations
Micah5:1 Mobilize! Marshal your troops! The enemy is laying siege to Jerusalem. With a rod they will strike the leader of Israel in the face.
Micah5:2 But you
Micah5:3 The people of Israel will be abandoned to their enemies until the time when the woman in labor gives birth to her son. Then at last his fellow countrymen will return from exile to their own land.
Micah5:4 And he will stand to lead his flock with the LORD's strength
Micah5:5 And he will be the source of our peace. When the Assyrians invade our land and break through our defenses
Micah5:6 They will rule Assyria with drawn swords and enter the gates of the land of Nimrod. They will rescue us from the Assyrians when they pour over the borders to invade our land.
Micah5:7 Then the few left in Israel will go out among the nations. They will be like dew sent by the LORD or like rain falling on the grass
Micah5:8 The remnant of Israel will go out among the nations and be as strong as a lion. And the other nations will be like helpless sheep
Micah5:9 The people of Israel will stand up to their foes
Micah5:10 At that same time
Micah5:11 I will tear down your walls and demolish the defenses of your cities.
Micah5:12 I will put an end to all witchcraft; there will be no more fortune-tellers to consult.
Micah5:13 I will destroy all your idols and sacred pillars
Micah5:14 I will abolish your pagan shrines with their Asherah poles and destroy the cities where your idol temples stand.
Micah5:15 I will pour out my vengeance on all the nations that refuse to obey me.
Micah6:1 Listen to what the LORD is saying: Stand up and state your case against me. Let the mountains and hills be called to witness your complaints.
Micah6:2 And now
Micah6:3 O my people
Micah6:4 For I brought you out of Egypt and redeemed you from your slavery. I sent Moses
Micah6:5 Don't you remember
Micah6:6 What can we bring to the LORD to make up for what we've done? Should we bow before God with offerings of yearling calves?
Micah6:7 Should we offer him thousands of rams and tens of thousands of rivers of olive oil? Would that please the LORD? Should we sacrifice our firstborn children to pay for the sins of our souls? Would that make him glad?
Micah6:8 No
Micah6:9 Listen! Fear the LORD if you are wise! His voice is calling out to everyone in Jerusalem: The armies of destruction are coming; the LORD is sending them.
Micah6:10 Will there be no end of your getting rich by cheating? The homes of the wicked are filled with treasures gained by dishonestly measuring out grain in short measures.
Micah6:11 And how can I tolerate all your merchants who use dishonest scales and weights?
Micah6:12 The rich among you have become wealthy through extortion and violence. Your citizens are so used to lying that their tongues can no longer tell the truth.
Micah6:13 Therefore
Micah6:14 You will eat but never have enough. Your hunger pangs and emptiness will still remain. And though you try to save your money
Micah6:15 You will plant crops but not harvest them. You will press your olives but not get enough oil to anoint yourselves. You will trample the grapes but get no juice to make your wine.
Micah6:16 The only laws you keep are those of evil King Omri; the only example you follow is that of wicked King Ahab! Therefore
Micah7:1 What misery is mine! I feel like the fruit picker after the harvest who can find nothing to eat. Not a cluster of grapes or a single fig can be found to satisfy my hunger.
Micah7:2 The godly people have all disappeared; not one fair-minded person is left on the earth. They are all murderers
Micah7:3 They go about their evil deeds with both hands. How skilled they are at using them! Officials and judges alike demand bribes. The people with money and influence pay them off
Micah7:4 Even the best of them is like a brier; the straightest is more crooked than a hedge of thorns. But your judgment day is coming swiftly now. Your time of punishment is here.
Micah7:5 Don't trust anyone--not your best friend or even your wife!
Micah7:6 For the son despises his father. The daughter defies her mother. The daughter-in-law defies her mother-in-law. Your enemies will be right in your own household.
Micah7:7 As for me
Micah7:8 Do not gloat over me
Micah7:9 I will be patient as the LORD punishes me
Micah7:10 Then my enemies will see that the LORD is on my side. They will be ashamed that they taunted me
Micah7:11 In that day
Micah7:12 People from many lands will come and honor you--from Assyria all the way to the towns of Egypt
Micah7:13 But the land will become empty and desolate because of the wickedness of those who live there.
Micah7:14 O LORD
Micah7:15 Yes
Micah7:16 All the nations of the world will stand amazed at what the LORD will do for you. They will be embarrassed that their power is so insignificant. They will stand in silent awe
Micah7:17 They will come to realize what lowly creatures they really are. Like snakes crawling from their holes
Micah7:18 Where is another God like you
Micah7:19 Once again you will have compassion on us. You will trample our sins under your feet and throw them into the depths of the ocean!
Micah7:20 You will show us your faithfulness and unfailing love as you promised with an oath to our ancestors Abraham and Jacob long ago.
Nahum1:1 This message concerning Nineveh came as a vision to Nahum
Nahum1:2 The LORD is a jealous God
Nahum1:3 The LORD is slow to get angry
Nahum1:4 At his command the oceans and rivers dry up
Nahum1:5 In his presence the mountains quake
Nahum1:6 Who can stand before his fierce anger? Who can survive his burning fury? His rage blazes forth like fire
Nahum1:7 The LORD is good. When trouble comes
Nahum1:8 But he sweeps away his enemies in an overwhelming flood. He pursues his foes into the darkness of night.
Nahum1:9 Why are you scheming against the LORD? He will destroy you with one blow; he won't need to strike twice!
Nahum1:10 His enemies
Nahum1:11 Who is this king of yours who dares to plot evil against the LORD?
Nahum1:12 This is what the LORD says: Even though the Assyrians have many allies
Nahum1:13 Now I will break your chains and release you from Assyrian oppression.
Nahum1:14 And this is what the LORD says concerning the Assyrians in Nineveh: You will have no more children to carry on your name. I will destroy all the idols in the temples of your gods. I am preparing a grave for you because you are despicable and don't deserve to live!
Nahum1:15 Look! A messenger is coming over the mountains with good news! He is bringing a message of peace. Celebrate your festivals
Nahum2:1 Nineveh
Nahum2:2 For the land of Israel lies empty and broken after your attacks
Nahum2:3 Shields flash red in the sunlight! The attack begins! See their scarlet uniforms! Watch as their glittering chariots move into position
Nahum2:4 The chariots race recklessly along the streets and through the squares
Nahum2:5 The king shouts to his officers; they stumble in their haste
Nahum2:6 But too late! The river gates are open! The enemy has entered! The palace is about to collapse!
Nahum2:7 Nineveh's exile has been decreed
Nahum2:8 Nineveh is like a leaking water reservoir! The people are slipping away. Stop
Nahum2:9 Loot the silver! Plunder the gold! There seems no end to Nineveh's many treasures--its vast
Nahum2:10 Soon the city is an empty shambles
Nahum2:11 Where now is that great Nineveh
Nahum2:12 O Nineveh
Nahum2:13 I am your enemy! says the LORD Almighty. Your chariots will soon go up in smoke. The finest of your youth will be killed in battle. Never again will you bring back plunder from conquered nations. Never again will the voices of your proud messengers be heard.
Nahum3:1 How terrible it will be for Nineveh
Nahum3:2 Listen! Hear the crack of the whips as the chariots rush forward against her. Wheels rumble
Nahum3:3 See the flashing swords and glittering spears in the upraised arms of the cavalry! The dead are lying in the streets--dead bodies
Nahum3:4 All this because Nineveh
Nahum3:5 No wonder I am your enemy! declares the LORD Almighty. And now I will lift your skirts so all the earth will see your nakedness and shame.
Nahum3:6 I will cover you with filth and show the world how vile you really are.
Nahum3:7 All who see you will shrink back in horror and say
Nahum3:8 Are you any better than Thebes
Nahum3:9 Ethiopia and the land of Egypt were the source of her strength
Nahum3:10 Yet Thebes fell
Nahum3:11 And you
Nahum3:12 All your fortresses will fall. They will be devoured like the ripe figs that fall into the mouths of those who shake the trees.
Nahum3:13 Your troops will be as weak and helpless as women. The gates of your land will be opened wide to the enemy and set on fire and burned.
Nahum3:14 Get ready for the siege! Store up water! Strengthen the defenses! Make bricks to repair the walls! Go into the pits to trample clay
Nahum3:15 But in the middle of your preparations
Nahum3:16 Merchants
Nahum3:17 Your princes and officials are also like locusts
Nahum3:18 O Assyrian king
Nahum3:19 There is no healing for your wound; your injury is fatal. All who hear of your destruction will clap their hands for joy. Where can anyone be found who has not suffered from your cruelty?
Habakkuk1:1 This is the message that the prophet Habakkuk received from the LORD in a vision.
Habakkuk1:2 How long
Habakkuk1:3 Must I forever see this sin and misery all around me? Wherever I look
Habakkuk1:4 The law has become paralyzed and useless
Habakkuk1:5 The LORD replied
Habakkuk1:6 I am raising up the Babylonians to be a new power on the world scene. They are a cruel and violent nation who will march across the world and conquer it.
Habakkuk1:7 They are notorious for their cruelty. They do as they like
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards. They are a fierce people
Habakkuk1:9 On they come
Habakkuk1:10 They scoff at kings and princes and scorn all their defenses. They simply pile ramps of earth against their walls and capture them!
Habakkuk1:11 They sweep past like the wind and are gone. But they are deeply guilty
Habakkuk1:12 O LORD my God
Habakkuk1:13 You are perfectly just in this. But will you
Habakkuk1:14 Are we but fish to be caught and killed? Are we but creeping things that have no leader to defend them from their enemies?
Habakkuk1:15 Must we be strung up on their hooks and dragged out in their nets while they rejoice?
Habakkuk1:16 Then they will worship their nets and burn incense in front of them. These nets are the gods who have made us rich! they will claim.
Habakkuk1:17 Will you let them get away with this forever? Will they succeed forever in their heartless conquests?
Habakkuk2:1 I will climb up into my watchtower now and wait to see what the LORD will say to me and how he will answer my complaint.
Habakkuk2:2 Then the LORD said to me
Habakkuk2:3 But these things I plan won't happen right away. Slowly
Habakkuk2:4 Look at the proud! They trust in themselves
Habakkuk2:5 Wealth is treacherous
Habakkuk2:6 But the time is coming when all their captives will taunt them
Habakkuk2:7 Suddenly
Habakkuk2:8 You have plundered many nations; now they will plunder you. You murderers! You have filled the countryside with violence and all the cities
Habakkuk2:9 How terrible it will be for you who get rich by unjust means! You believe your wealth will buy security
Habakkuk2:10 But by the murders you committed
Habakkuk2:11 The very stones in the walls of your houses cry out against you
Habakkuk2:12 How terrible it will be for you who build cities with money gained by murder and corruption!
Habakkuk2:13 Has not the LORD Almighty promised that the wealth of nations will turn to ashes? They work so hard
Habakkuk2:14 For the time will come when all the earth will be filled
Habakkuk2:15 How terrible it will be for you who make your neighbors drunk! You force your cup on them so that you can gloat over their nakedness and shame.
Habakkuk2:16 But soon it will be your turn! Come
Habakkuk2:17 You cut down the forests of Lebanon. Now you will be cut down! You terrified the wild animals you caught in your traps. Now terror will strike you because of your murder and violence in cities everywhere!
Habakkuk2:18 What have you gained by worshiping all your man-made idols? How foolish to trust in something made by your own hands! What fools you are to believe such lies!
Habakkuk2:19 How terrible it will be for you who beg lifeless wooden idols to save you. You ask speechless stone images to tell you what to do. Can an idol speak for God? They may be overlaid with gold and silver
Habakkuk2:20 But the LORD is in his holy Temple. Let all the earth be silent before him.
Habakkuk3:1 This prayer was sung by the prophet Habakkuk:
Habakkuk3:2 I have heard all about you
Habakkuk3:3 I see God
Habakkuk3:4 Rays of brilliant light flash from his hands. He rejoices in his awesome power.
Habakkuk3:5 Pestilence marches before him; plague follows close behind.
Habakkuk3:6 When he stops
Habakkuk3:7 I see the peoples of Cushan and Midian trembling in terror.
Habakkuk3:8 Was it in anger
Habakkuk3:9 You were commanding your weapons of power! You split open the earth with flowing rivers!
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains watched and trembled. Onward swept the raging waters. The mighty deep cried out
Habakkuk3:11 The lofty sun and moon began to fade
Habakkuk3:12 You marched across the land in awesome anger and trampled the nations in your fury.
Habakkuk3:13 You went out to rescue your chosen people
Habakkuk3:14 With their own weapons
Habakkuk3:15 You trampled the sea with your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I trembled inside when I heard all this; my lips quivered with fear. My legs gave way beneath me
Habakkuk3:17 Even though the fig trees have no blossoms
Habakkuk3:18 yet I will rejoice in the LORD! I will be joyful in the God of my salvation.
Habakkuk3:19 The Sovereign LORD is my strength! He will make me as surefooted as a deer and bring me safely over the mountains. (For the choir director: This prayer is to be accompanied by stringed instruments.)
Zephaniah1:1 The LORD gave these messages to Zephaniah when Josiah son of Amon was king of Judah. Zephaniah was the son of Cushi
Zephaniah1:2 I will sweep away everything in all your land
Zephaniah1:3 I will sweep away both people and animals alike. Even the birds of the air and the fish in the sea will die. I will reduce the wicked to heaps of rubble
Zephaniah1:4 I will crush Judah and Jerusalem with my fist and destroy every last trace of their Baal worship. I will put an end to all the idolatrous priests
Zephaniah1:5 For they go up to their roofs and bow to the sun
Zephaniah1:6 And I will destroy those who used to worship me but now no longer do. They no longer ask for the LORD's guidance or seek my blessings.
Zephaniah1:7 Stand in silence in the presence of the Sovereign LORD
Zephaniah1:8 On that day of judgment
Zephaniah1:9 Yes
Zephaniah1:10 On that day
Zephaniah1:11 Wail in sorrow
Zephaniah1:12 I will search with lanterns in Jerusalem's darkest corners to find and punish those who sit contented in their sins
Zephaniah1:13 They are the very ones whose property will be plundered by the enemy
Zephaniah1:14 That terrible day of the LORD is near. Swiftly it comes--a day when strong men will cry bitterly.
Zephaniah1:15 It is a day when the LORD's anger will be poured out. It is a day of terrible distress and anguish
Zephaniah1:16 trumpet calls
Zephaniah1:17 Because you have sinned against the LORD
Zephaniah1:18 Your silver and gold will be of no use to you on that day of the LORD's anger. For the whole land will be devoured by the fire of his jealousy. He will make a terrifying end of all the people on earth.

Zephaniah3:1 How terrible it will be for rebellious
Zephaniah3:2 It proudly refuses to listen even to the voice of the LORD. No one can tell it anything; it refuses all correction. It does not trust in the LORD or draw near to its God.
Zephaniah3:3 Its leaders are like roaring lions hunting for their victims--out for everything they can get. Its judges are like ravenous wolves at evening time
Zephaniah3:4 Its prophets are arrogant liars seeking their own gain. Its priests defile the Temple by disobeying God's laws.
Zephaniah3:5 But the LORD is still there in the city
Zephaniah3:6 I have wiped out many nations
Zephaniah3:7 I thought
Zephaniah3:8 So now the LORD says: Be patient; the time is coming soon when I will stand up and accuse these evil nations. For it is my decision to gather together the kingdoms of the earth and pour out my fiercest anger and fury on them. All the earth will be devoured by the fire of my jealousy.
Zephaniah3:9 On that day I will purify the lips of all people
Zephaniah3:10 My scattered people who live beyond the rivers of Ethiopia will come to present their offerings.
Zephaniah3:11 And then you will no longer need to be ashamed of yourselves
Zephaniah3:12 Those who are left will be the lowly and the humble
Zephaniah3:13 The people of Israel who survive will do no wrong to each other
Zephaniah3:14 Sing
Zephaniah3:15 For the LORD will remove his hand of judgment and will disperse the armies of your enemy. And the LORD himself
Zephaniah3:16 On that day the announcement to Jerusalem will be
Zephaniah3:17 For the LORD your God has arrived to live among you. He is a mighty savior. He will rejoice over you with great gladness. With his love
Zephaniah3:18 I will gather you who mourn for the appointed festivals; you will be disgraced no more.
Zephaniah3:19 And I will deal severely with all who have oppressed you. I will save the weak and helpless ones; I will bring together those who were chased away. I will give glory and renown to my former exiles
Zephaniah3:20 On that day I will gather you together and bring you home again. I will give you a good name
Haggai1:1 On August 29 of the second year of King Darius's reign
Haggai1:2 This is what the LORD Almighty says: The people are saying
Haggai1:3 So the LORD sent this message through the prophet Haggai:
Haggai1:4 Why are you living in luxurious houses while my house lies in ruins?
Haggai1:5 This is what the LORD Almighty says: Consider how things are going for you!
Haggai1:6 You have planted much but harvested little. You have food to eat
Haggai1:7 This is what the LORD Almighty says: Consider how things are going for you!
Haggai1:8 Now go up into the hills
Haggai1:9 You hoped for rich harvests
Haggai1:10 That is why the heavens have withheld the dew and the earth has withheld its crops.
Haggai1:11 I have called for a drought on your fields and hills--a drought to wither the grain and grapes and olives and all your other crops
Haggai1:12 Then Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel
Haggai1:13 Then Haggai
Haggai1:14 So the LORD sparked the enthusiasm of Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel

Haggai2:2 Say this to Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel
Haggai2:3 Is there anyone who can remember this house--the Temple--as it was before? In comparison
Haggai2:4 But now take courage
Haggai2:5 My Spirit remains among you
Haggai2:6 For this is what the LORD Almighty says: In just a little while I will again shake the heavens and the earth. I will shake the oceans and the dry land
Haggai2:7 I will shake all the nations
Haggai2:8 The silver is mine
Haggai2:9 The future glory of this Temple will be greater than its past glory
Haggai2:10 On December 18 of the second year of King Darius's reign
Haggai2:11 This is what the LORD Almighty says! Ask the priests this question about the law:

Haggai2:14 Then Haggai said

Haggai2:17 I sent blight and mildew and hail to destroy all the produce of your labor. Yet
Haggai2:18 On this eighteenth day of December--the day when the foundation of the LORD's Temple was laid--carefully consider this:
Haggai2:19 I am giving you a promise now while the seed is still in the barn

Haggai2:21 Tell Zerubbabel

Zechariah1:2 I
Zechariah1:3 Therefore
Zechariah1:4 Do not be like your ancestors who would not listen when the earlier prophets said to them

Zechariah1:7 Then on February 15 of the second year of King Darius's reign

Zechariah1:9 I asked the angel who was talking with me

Zechariah1:12 Upon hearing this

Zechariah1:21 What are these men coming to do? I asked. The angel replied

Zechariah4:1 Then the angel who had been talking with me returned and woke me
Zechariah4:2 What do you see now? he asked. I answered
Zechariah4:3 And I see two olive trees
Zechariah4:4 Then I asked the angel
Zechariah4:5 Don't you know? the angel asked. No
Zechariah4:6 Then he said to me

Matthew1:1 This is a record of the ancestors of Jesus the Messiah
Matthew1:2 Abraham was the father of Isaac. Isaac was the father of Jacob. Jacob was the father of Judah and his brothers.
Matthew1:3 Judah was the father of Perez and Zerah (their mother was Tamar). Perez was the father of Hezron. Hezron was the father of Ram.
Matthew1:4 Ram was the father of Amminadab. Amminadab was the father of Nahshon. Nahshon was the father of Salmon.
Matthew1:5 Salmon was the father of Boaz (his mother was Rahab). Boaz was the father of Obed (his mother was Ruth). Obed was the father of Jesse.
Matthew1:6 Jesse was the father of King David. David was the father of Solomon (his mother was Bathsheba
Matthew1:7 Solomon was the father of Rehoboam. Rehoboam was the father of Abijah. Abijah was the father of Asaph.
Matthew1:8 Asaph was the father of Jehoshaphat. Jehoshaphat was the father of Jehoram. Jehoram was the father of Uzziah.
Matthew1:9 Uzziah was the father of Jotham. Jotham was the father of Ahaz. Ahaz was the father of Hezekiah.
Matthew1:10 Hezekiah was the father of Manasseh. Manasseh was the father of Amos. Amos was the father of Josiah.
Matthew1:11 Josiah was the father of Jehoiachin and his brothers (born at the time of the exile to Babylon).
Matthew1:12 After the Babylonian exile: Jehoiachin was the father of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the father of Zerubbabel.
Matthew1:13 Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud. Abiud was the father of Eliakim. Eliakim was the father of Azor.
Matthew1:14 Azor was the father of Zadok. Zadok was the father of Akim. Akim was the father of Eliud.
Matthew1:15 Eliud was the father of Eleazar. Eleazar was the father of Matthan. Matthan was the father of Jacob.
Matthew1:16 Jacob was the father of Joseph
Matthew1:17 All those listed above include fourteen generations from Abraham to King David
Matthew1:18 Now this is how Jesus the Messiah was born. His mother
Matthew1:19 Joseph
Matthew1:20 As he considered this
Matthew1:21 And she will have a son
Matthew1:22 All of this happened to fulfill the Lord's message through his prophet:
Matthew1:23 Look! The virgin will conceive a child! She will give birth to a son
Matthew1:24 When Joseph woke up
Matthew1:25 but she remained a virgin until her son was born. And Joseph named him Jesus.

Mark4:2 He began to teach the people by telling many stories such as this one:
Mark4:3 Listen! A farmer went out to plant some seed.
Mark4:4 As he scattered it across his field
Mark4:5 Other seed fell on shallow soil with underlying rock. The plant sprang up quickly
Mark4:6 but it soon wilted beneath the hot sun and died because the roots had no nourishment in the shallow soil.
Mark4:7 Other seed fell among thorns that shot up and choked out the tender blades so that it produced no grain.
Mark4:8 Still other seed fell on fertile soil and produced a crop that was thirty
Mark4:9 Anyone who is willing to hear should listen and understand!

Mark4:15 The seed that fell on the hard path represents those who hear the message
Mark4:16 The rocky soil represents those who hear the message and receive it with joy.

Mark4:18 The thorny ground represents those who hear and accept the Good News

Luke1:1 Most honorable Theophilus: Many people have written accounts about the events that took place among us.
Luke1:2 They used as their source material the reports circulating among us from the early disciples and other eyewitnesses of what God has done in fulfillment of his promises.
Luke1:3 Having carefully investigated all of these accounts from the beginning
Luke1:4 to reassure you of the truth of all you were taught.

Luke3:21 One day when the crowds were being baptized
Luke3:22 and the Holy Spirit descended on him in the form of a dove. And a voice from heaven said
Luke3:23 Jesus was about thirty years old when he began his public ministry. Jesus was known as the son of Joseph. Joseph was the son of Heli.
Luke3:24 Heli was the son of Matthat. Matthat was the son of Levi. Levi was the son of Melki. Melki was the son of Jannai. Jannai was the son of Joseph.
Luke3:25 Joseph was the son of Mattathias. Mattathias was the son of Amos. Amos was the son of Nahum. Nahum was the son of Esli. Esli was the son of Naggai.
Luke3:26 Naggai was the son of Maath. Maath was the son of Mattathias. Mattathias was the son of Semein. Semein was the son of Josech. Josech was the son of Joda.
Luke3:27 Joda was the son of Joanan. Joanan was the son of Rhesa. Rhesa was the son of Zerubbabel. Zerubbabel was the son of Shealtiel. Shealtiel was the son of Neri.
Luke3:28 Neri was the son of Melki. Melki was the son of Addi. Addi was the son of Cosam. Cosam was the son of Elmadam. Elmadam was the son of Er.
Luke3:29 Er was the son of Joshua. Joshua was the son of Eliezer. Eliezer was the son of Jorim. Jorim was the son of Matthat. Matthat was the son of Levi.
Luke3:30 Levi was the son of Simeon. Simeon was the son of Judah. Judah was the son of Joseph. Joseph was the son of Jonam. Jonam was the son of Eliakim.
Luke3:31 Eliakim was the son of Melea. Melea was the son of Menna. Menna was the son of Mattatha. Mattatha was the son of Nathan. Nathan was the son of David.
Luke3:32 David was the son of Jesse. Jesse was the son of Obed. Obed was the son of Boaz. Boaz was the son of Salmon. Salmon was the son of Nahshon.
Luke3:33 Nahshon was the son of Amminadab. Amminadab was the son of Admin. Admin was the son of Arni. Arni was the son of Hezron. Hezron was the son of Perez. Perez was the son of Judah.
Luke3:34 Judah was the son of Jacob. Jacob was the son of Isaac. Isaac was the son of Abraham. Abraham was the son of Terah. Terah was the son of Nahor.
Luke3:35 Nahor was the son of Serug. Serug was the son of Reu. Reu was the son of Peleg. Peleg was the son of Eber. Eber was the son of Shelah.
Luke3:36 Shelah was the son of Cainan. Cainan was the son of Arphaxad. Arphaxad was the son of Shem. Shem was the son of Noah. Noah was the son of Lamech.
Luke3:37 Lamech was the son of Methuselah. Methuselah was the son of Enoch. Enoch was the son of Jared. Jared was the son of Mahalalel. Mahalalel was the son of Kenan.
Luke3:38 Kenan was the son of Enosh. Enosh was the son of Seth. Seth was the son of Adam. Adam was the son of God.

Ephesians1:2 May grace and peace be yours
Ephesians1:3 How we praise God
